description:
Dice Dodgems is a 30 minute strategy game of dodging and bumping for 2-4 players, designed by City Square Studios.

In Dice Dodgems, players control miniature dodgems as they dodge and bump around the arena. The goal of the game is to be the last dodgem standing. The winner is the last player with dice.

While the game's rules are easy to understand for newcomers, the bouncing mechanics and ability to see and predict other dodgems moves make for a deeper strategic experience, with great replay-ability. A chess-like battle can occur when two or more strategic players bounce around the arena.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Akhorne the Squirrel Star Player Promo Card
description: This is a promo card for Akhrone the Squirrel that was contained in White Dwarf #458 (November 2020). This contains all the rules for using the Akhrone star player in your games of Blood Bowl. The official model comes in the Blood Bowl (Second Season Edition): Treeman kit.

Equipment is crucial to summiting the mountains. Pre-ordered copies of 7 Summits come with this pack of three additional equipment cards.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Ave senator, times are tough within the republic. Barbarians harass Rome from without and corruption seeps from within. You fellow senators are scheming to claim the seat of Emperor for themselves by amassing their influence, might, and wealth. It is up to you, senator, to protect Rome from her foes; both barbarians and corrupt politicians. Either the light and majesty of your Roman Republic will prevail... or someone else will win. Collect Influence, Might, or Wealth cards to challenge the senate and defeat your fellow senators. Play your action cards to advance your political position and elect decrees that work in your favour. But beware for Rome has many enemies. You will have to work together with your fellow senators to protected Rome from Crises and Invasions all while plotting to stab them in the back.

ENTERTAINING FUN: This is a strategic board game for up to eight players that will provide hours of play for family and friends.
GREAT GAMEPLAY: Take on the role of a member of the senate and strive to become Emperor of Rome using your Influence, Might, and Wealth.
162 CARDS: Collect and play Influence, Might, and Wealth action cards to steer the game in your favour. There are many different cards to use against your fellow senators. On the other hand, you may need to work together to overcome crises and invasions.
DEVISE GRAND STRATEGIES: Outsmart and outwit your opponents through clever plans and devious thinking. Bide your time, strengthen your hand, and lure your opponents into your trap to snatch victory from their grasp.
GREAT REPLAY VALUE: The more you play, the better you'll know the cards and the more plans you can create. Enact laws that change the base rules of the game so that every round you play is different.

—description from the publisher

One of the early expansions to Alias - it still carries the Nelospelit logo of the early 1990's.

300 new cards with 8 words each, for a total of 2400 new words to explain.

boardgame: Ragnarok: Dawn of the Heavens' Massacre CCG – Witness A Divine Comedy
description:
The Witness A Divine Comedy expansion is when the hero Dreania Reeve hero and her accompanying cards was introduced to the game. 
The expansion also introduced powerful enchantments,spells and card stealing skills.

The success of your company is opening up new markets full of opportunities! With your expertise and logistical innovations over the last 3 years, the refinement requirements of this new era are even more demanding. With the emergence of new markets, new technologies and innovations have become available for your business to utilize. Additionally, these emerging markets have brought about new ways of evaluating your business. Will you take advantage of the new ways to exploit the markets or will your business fall to ruin in this everchanging world?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Heart of Cthulhu is a cutthroat card game consisting of three decks and more than 300 cards. This adventure game combines strategy and luck with aspects of RPG. This combination, the game’s unique lore and stunning visuals produce a new adventure every time the game is played.

The game consists of 3 Decks of cards, 110 cards each. The Peril, Pillage and Discovery Decks are shuffled and placed in separate stacks with the Pillage Deck in the middle. Two discovery cards are revealed and placed face up next to the Discovery Deck.
    
Shuffle the vessel cards and deal one to each captain face down. Captains then reveal their vessels. Take notice of the vessels’ unique strengths. Each captain starts the game with three integrity chips and three pillage cards. Roll to see who goes first.

Players take turns gathering supplies from the pillage deck and paying to reveal discovery cards. The discovery deck contains New Worlds. In order to gain these New Worlds, Captains must defeat perils from the peril deck.

The first player to gain 3 New Worlds wins the game!

—description the designer

Warhammer 2004 Annual is a compilation of Warhammer articles, combined with revised or previously unpublished material.  Here you will find rules for all the latest releases, plus gaming articles and additional gaming material.

This includes new Army Lists for : the Cult of Ulric, Kislev Allies, Wood Elves, Empire Steam Tank and Dogs of War.

It also has profiles for Special Characters such as: Boris Todbringer of Middenheim, Eltharion, Shadowblade, Crom the Conquerer, Valten - The Champion of Sigmar, and Tzarina Katarin the Ice Queen.

Finally is has a complete list of errata.

boardgame: Time of Legends: Joan of Arc – Meeples-at-Arms Promo Card
description:
Originally available for backers of the Man VS Meeple Kickstarter 2020.

This card is an infantry unit which will melee attack with 1 red die and defend with 1 red die. It can also be used to charge (move 1 space and attack the same turn). They have the skill Retaliation.

A unit with the retaliation skill can apply a kill or disrupt result from their defence roll to any melee attacking unit. The player must roll the dice of the retaliating unit separately 
to distinguish them from other units. The attack and the retaliation are considered to be simultaneous.

The player suffering the retaliation cannot use shield results from their attack roll to cancel retaliation results.
family: Game: Time of Legends – Joan of Arc

description:
In Itékiya the players act at the same time, using one of the options available on the cards shown, throughout twelve turns they will try to perform combos to be able to complete the missions and maximize their scores. It is a game designed so that novice players can play alongside more experienced players,  which can take unique challenges that will make a complex gameplay.

—description from the designer

In Trailblazer each player creates a 6x6 grid and then attempts to reconstruct their opponent's grid.

A grid is created by entering a number from 1-25 in any space and then entering the next higher number in an adjacent space, and so on, until most or all of the spaces are filled; some spaces can be filled with an "X" instead of a number, but these cannot be adjacent to each other or the first or last spaces of the "trail."

After the grids are created players take turns asking each other for the contents of one space. When a player thinks they can recreate their opponent's grid they do so and win if they are correct.

boardgame: Super Fantasy Brawl: Circle of Blood
description:
Add to the roster of your Champions with the Circle of Blood! This expansion adds Korvash, Mariusz, and Rath to your Champions. Let’s take a look at them! When Korvash grasped the hilt of the old blade he found in the tomb, his mind was consumed with dark visions; promises of a world on fire, and of his enemies kneeling before him. Filled with a desire to make his dreams a reality, he submitted to the presence within the sword, becoming its willing servant. Korvash would go on to take over his tribe, and lead them in a bloody conquest of the Hillsglade. Korvash’s confidence and power grew with every victory. But the visions never stopped, and Scornblade’s hunger was never sated.

The life of Mariusz Demont has never been easy, despite being born into nobility. A member of the Moontouched, Mariusz showed no sign of lycanthropy, relegating him to a life of service to
his older, more blessed brothers. Treated with contempt throughout his life, it was during an attack that left his father Angren dead that Mariusz’s gift revealed itself. Exploding into a rage-filled beast, he slaughtered friend and foe alike, his years of torment blinding him to his bloodlust. Despised by his own people as a traitor, Mariusz counted none his ally as he continued his cursed existence.

As Kilgore’s armies grew, so too did the strength of his enemies. Many enemies were crushed on the battlefield, but others needed a subtler touch. These enemies attract the attention of Rath, Kilgore’s most trusted and lethal assassin. A subterran of incredible agility and ferocity, he is the deadliest weapon in Kilgore’s arsenal, able to slip quietly into any location, and execute his target. Rath served Kilgore loyally for many years, reveling in the chaos and destruction he wrought.

boardgame: Super Fantasy Brawl: Art of War
description:
Add to the roster of your Super Fantasy Brawl Champions with the Art of War! This expansion adds Sulka, Ko'lel, and Ahket to your Champions. Let’s take a look at them! Subtle and deceptive, Sulka manipulated her people into a civil war that consumed them, profiting herself with their suffering. Her dark magics and powers over the mind left both sides of the war as putty in her scaled hands, and soon Sulka was poised to control what little remained. Beneath her rule, her people turned to foul, long forbidden magics, and conquered Khemara in short order. Her empire would rule for hundreds of years, and it would be long past Sulka’s time that her people would learn that their success was built on the machinations of evil and greed.

Akhet, the Skylord of Khemara, ruled the nation of Khemara until its dying days. A proud and noble King, Akhet commanded the skies as the bravest warrior his people had ever known, ruling the largest dynasty in the history of Fabulosa. Akhet led the charge in any battle he was a part of, breaking the enemy with his tireless ferocity. Even when a dark and sorcerous force arose to lead their most hated foe to victory, the Skylord led the charge, determined to cast down this hidden evil and bring light to the darkest hour of his people.

Records of the Darkmoon Occupation of Yunkaya speak of the legendary gnome Kolel, and her fearsome mount To’paque. Armed with her deadly blowpipe, her combination of skill, speed, and tactical knowledge was believed to have saved thousands of her people, despite the destruction of her jungle. Whilst she is regarded as the greatest hero in Yunkayan history, to the Moontouched she is the boogeyman; she who brought fear to the forests.

Pays du Lunévillois is a local board game based on the principle of questions and answers.

Players start in one of the town centers of the Lunévillois:  Blâmont, Einville, Bayon... and then go discover all the sectors with 550 questions relating to geography, history, celebrities....

The winner will have to be the first one to collect 5 "Site" cards by going to their respective box and answering the question correctly.

Gone Bunkers is a strategy card game. Players take turns building the ultimate hand until someone draws a war card and cannot defeat it. The deck is made up of all types of cards such as; troops, zombies, landmines and of course nukes! Even though your goal is to survive and fight off the war cards, it doesn't mean you can't nuke the player sitting next to you and destroy half their hand.

How to play:
Players take turns by drawing and playing cards until a WAR card is drawn from the deck. Once a WAR card is drawn, that player must defeat that WAR card. Be diplomatic to avoid the WAR, try to fight or be destroyed.

Taking your turn:
Gather all your cards into your hand and look at them. Do one of the following:
1. Draw from the draw pile. If it is a WAR card you can either play Diplomacy card and avoid the WAR or go to WAR and use your troops to defeat the WAR card. You must have the same or greater attack value (attack values are on troop cards) to win the WAR. If you cannot beat the WAR card's value, you lose.
2.Play a card FACE UP on the discard pile and follow the instructions on the card. Note: Zombie cards are placed in front of players not on the discard pile.

Winning the game:
Players will take turns drawing cards, builing their armies and surviving wars. Once one person loses all their cards then the person with the highest attack value (count up your troops attack values) left wins.

Gameplay variations:
Fight to the Death
Play until there is only one player left

15 Round Limit
Play 15 rounds and whomever has the highest attack value left wins

Gameplay Modes:
2 Player Mode
Start with 7 cards each. Each time a WAR card is drawn and a player wins at WAR or plays Diplomacy, put that WAR card back into the deck at random. This will ensure a player loses without the going through the entire deck.

Safe Haven Mode (Fan Favorite)
Do the same initial "Game Setup" except before putting the WAR cards back in the deck, place two cards, per player, face down on the side. Then, complete the regualar game setup and put those extra cards, per player, on top of the deck. This will ensure that each player gets two "safe" turns before a WAR card can be drawn.

Les Trésors Arvernes
A great adventure in the heart of the Auvergne volcanoes. 
Search for 2 treasures: the Gallic shield and the Arverne boar, hidden in the middle of the Volcanoes. 
Choose your path well. 
But beware some may block your way. 
Be strategic and judicious in the decisions you make. 
Stay alert and don't get distracted. 
May luck be with you. 
Will you be the first to reach the Puy de Dôme to fly to victory? 
The treasure hunt is on.


Les Trésors Arvernes is a treasure hunt in the Auvergne Volcanoes Regional Nature Park. 
Goal : find the two treasures hidden under the domes.
Gameplay: players move using cards only. 
Transport cards (foot, bicycle, horseback) are numbered from 1 to 4 regardless of the means of transport.
When a player arrives on a dome, he discreetly lifts it to check if there is a treasure below...

Wtht Vwls is a simple word game for any number of players. At the start of each round each player thinks of a word and then writes the consonants in that word, in order, on a slip of paper for each other player.

After all slips have been distributed the players all try to come up with the longest possible word for each set of consonants; they may add any number of vowels before, after, or in between the consonants, but no consonants may be added and the order cannot be changed. (Y is always a vowel in this game.)

When one player has finished they announce the end of the round immediately. Players score a point for every letter in the words they found. An agreed-upon number of rounds is played.

---You are a member of a tribe when humanity was still in its infancy.

We will efficiently collect resources for tribes to live in the harsh natural world and contribute to a rich life..... ---

This game is a resource management and trick-taking card game.

Each players creates several "roles" in the turn and decides the victory by the total of the points given for each "role".

Players use the Elements Cards (Fire, water, soil, wood) to collect resource cards for "Crystal", "Brick", "Apple", and "Charcoal".

After 3 rounds, the player who meets the 8 types of  and gets the most "contribution points" wins.

boardgame: バロニィ：スペシャルボックス (Barony: Special Box)
description:
Barony + Barony:Sorcery in one Box!

Expand your own territory and take control of the Empire!
In the farthest reaches of the Empire, four barons are vying for supremacy. In the farthest reaches of the empire, four barons are vying for supremacy. They use their many knights to expand their territories, building villages, fortresses and cities. Push your way through the ranks, climb the ladder of power and inherit the throne!

Baronies" are baronies. Baronies are baronies, where you are a small local lord and expand your territory.

Dispatch your knights to expand your territory and raise your barony!
The player can choose to perform one of the following actions each turn: "Collect", "Move", "Build", "New City", "Explore" or "Noble Title".

A knight who expands his territory or defends a village can only be obtained by performing a "Collection" action. Knights can be ordered to "move" to expand their territory. Once there, the knights can "build" villages and fortresses, which can then be developed into "new cities".

As you expand and develop your territory, you gain resource tokens, and use these resource token points to gain titles from Baron to Viscount, Count, Marquis and Duke. The game continues until one of the players becomes a duke, and the winner is the player with the most points.

The simple rules are easy to learn, the clear actions are easy to play, but the choices and tactics of each move are interesting, and the game can be played in a relatively short time.

A wind of magic blows through the Barony!
Explore the mysterious land and find the source of the magic!
The Baronies: Special Box includes the expansion set Sorcery, which allows you to play with five players.

It also adds a new element to the game - magic. By chanting spells, players can make spectacularly game-changing moves. But this will only be possible if your opponent gives you enough time!

Translated with www.DeepL.com/Translator (free version)

自らの領土を拡大し、帝国の覇権を握れ！
帝国の最果ての地で、4人の男爵が覇権を争っています。彼らは大勢の騎士を使って領土を拡大し、村や要塞、そして都市を築きます。自分の支配権を押し通し、権力の階段を上り、王位を継承してください！

『バロニィ』とは男爵領のこと。プレイヤーは地方の小領主となり、領地を広げていくことになります。

騎士をうまく派遣し領土を広げ爵位をあげる
プレイヤーは「徴集」、「移動」、「建設」、「新たな都市」、「探検」、「貴族の称号」のいずれかのアクションを、毎ターンに1つだけ選んで実行します。

領土を広げたり、村を守る騎士は「徴集」を行わなければ得られません。騎士は「移動」を命じることで、領土を広げていくことができます。移動した先で、騎士は村や要塞を「建設」することができ、村は「新たな都市」へと発展させることができます。

領土を広げて発展させることで資源トークンを得て、この資源トークンの点数を使って男爵から子爵、伯爵、侯爵、公爵と称号を得ていきます。いずれかのプレイヤーが公爵となるまでゲームを続け、得点をもっとも獲得していたプレイヤーが勝者となります。

シンプルなルールは覚えやすく、明確なアクションはプレイもしやすいのですが、その一手一手の選択と駆け引きも悩ましく面白い、比較的短時間でプレイできる陣取りゲームで、美しいアートワークや木製のコマが領地を広げて行く箱庭感も魅力です。

魔力の風が男爵領を吹き抜ける！
神秘の地を探検し、魔力の源を探せ！
『バロニィ：スペシャルボックス』には、拡張セット『ソーサリー』が同梱されており、5人プレイにも対応しています。

また、ゲームに新たな要素――魔法が追加されます。呪文の詠唱によって、プレイヤーは目を見張るほどにゲームを変化させる一手を打つことができます。しかし、それが可能になるのは、対戦相手が充分な時間を与えてくれた場合だけです！

Each player receives a set of twelve tiles. Alternately, you have to place the stones on the board in such a way that you form as large a contiguous area of a color as possible. At the same time, one should prevent the opponent from doing the same. The tactic: to define as large areas as possible, in which only your own pentominos fit. The whole thing is finished when no one is able to accommodate one of his pentominos on the board.

A didactic game to make as a family and teach the planet's values to the youngest.

"Shall We Save the Planet?

Pollution is getting stronger and Planet Earth is getting sicker.
Since you can't beat it alone, the Planet has summoned the Guardians of the Earth for an urgent cleaning mission.
Guardians have to act quickly and they need you.
Join them on this adventure and find out how you can help.
Learn how to clean beaches and seas with Solis and Gotas, discover "magic" foods with Bia, protect forests with Flora and become an expert in energy savings with Chamas.

Purpose of the game:
Players must move clockwise around the board to accumulate energy points and / or special "Guardian" cards.
There are two ways to win the game:
- Be the first player to get 50 energy points.
- Be the first player to acquire 6 special "Guardian" cards (one from each).
Players who run out of energy points immediately exit the game, as do their cards."

Each player has to get first to the house of the 7 Dwarfs and save the Snow White before she bites the poisoned apple!

Κάθε παίκτης πρέπει να φτάσει πρώτος με τους 7 νάνους στο τέλος (σπίτι των νάνων στο κέντρο του ταμπλό)και να σώσει τη Χιονάτη που είναι έτοιμη να φάει το δηλητηριασμένο μήλο.

description:
Born out of the odd experiences that hit the nation over the last few months, Hey Corona! is an outrageously fun card game where you try to stockpile as much toilet paper as you possibly can, while keeping yourself virus free by playing various action cards! Simple yet strategic, this game is great for all ages and perfect for socially distanced groups and families.

Save as much toilet paper as you possibly can in your "stockpile" of six face-down cards. Use action cards to switch out what you are hoarding and try to get rid of any cards with the virus.

At the end of each round, Toilet Paper Cards add points and Corona Cards take points away. Player with the most points at the end of 4 rounds wins.

From the Kickstarter and website descriptions.

The peaceful city of Rowan is surrounded by dangerous monsters. It’s up to the citizens to fortify the city, train up warriors, clerics, knights, and mages, and defend Rowan in Meeples & Monsters!

Meeples & Monsters is a big, beautiful bag-building engine builder by designer Ole Steiness (Champions of Midgard and more). Depth, replayability, beautiful components, and a splash of meeple humor will have you coming back for “just one more play” game night after game night!

During a players turn, they draw meeples out of their bag and use those meeples to perform various actions on the board. Those actions include upgrading your meeples, adding new meeples to your bag, fighting monsters, investigating towers, or even upgrading the town, giving players even more options on future turns. At the end of a player’s turn, all the meeples they drew are put onto the “lodging” part of the player’s board.
 During the game, players will score victory points by defeating monsters, completing quests, and investigating towers. At the end of the game, players will score additional victory points by fighting boss monsters and through end-of-game quests. The player with the most victory points wins!

This Kickstarter edition includes a bunch of upgrades and extras, including the 4-towers expansion (20 tiles) and everything you need to play the solo version (7 Royal Guard Order Tiles, 1 Royal Guard Board).

—description from the publisher

NOTE: The retail edition can be found here: Meeples & Monsters.

description:
Operation Market Garden was a military operation fought in the Netherlands from 17 to 25 September 1944. It was planned by Field Marshal Montgomery as combined land - airborne operation. The airborne operation was undertaken by the First Allied Airborne Army with the land operation by XXX Corps of the British Second Army.

The operation's objective was to create a 103 km long corridor into Germany with a bridgehead over the River Rhine, creating an Allied invasion route into northern Germany. This was to be achieved by seizing a series of nine bridges with airborne forces, with land forces swiftly following over the bridges. Despite this ambitious plans, operation turned out to be a fiasco.

Game covers the entire Market Garden operation. It comes with four scenarios, picturing the parts of the battle and the whole battle itself. It uses battalion scale.

"Market Garden" uses WB-95 system, with some modifications that were necessary due to a scale change.

description:
The future of Rokugan hangs in the balance with the "Temptations" cycle for Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game! As dark forces move throughout Rokugan, thanks to the Coils of Power Dynasty Pack, every clan is tempted to dabble in the forbidden arts of mahou, which requires fate to be spent from players' own characters. As with previous cycles, every Dynasty Pack of this cycle offers 60 new cards, with new options for each of the seven Great Clans of Rokugan, split between strongholds, provinces, dynasty deck, and conflict deck.

description:
In Koala and Wine, each player starts with a secret selection of glass cards. They can see their glass cards at the beginning but then the cards will be face down.

The last players to own glass cards win the game.

During the game, players will try to steal each other glass cards but to succeed they will need to use their memory or luck as to succeed a thief they need to match the type of wine of the  "wine thieves" card and the targeted glass.

Players can also use "action" koala cards that will help them during the game to reveal a glass for example or swap the position of glass cards on the table to confuse the others.

Finally, special cards will test the memory of the players and ask them to reveal a particular glass card, if they failed they will lose a glass card.

Koala and Wine is not a drinking game, it doesn't include or required alcohol.

-description from designer

Rival diner owners are under pressure to cook breakfast for their customers as quickly as possible. As some foods take longer to cook, you will need to plan ahead so the entire order is ready to serve at the same time. Players receive points for each successfully completed order and lose points for wasted food and incomplete or incorrect orders. The player with the most points when the flow of customers runs out is the winner.

Setup   
Each player chooses one diner board and places it in front of them.

Sort and place the food tiles and penalty tokens into each slot of the kitchen caddy.

Shuffle the customer order cards. Based on the amount of players, remove the number of cards needed and place the remaining cards back in the box (see chart at right).

The first person to shout out what they ate for breakfast will deal first. Give them the chef’s spoon and cards.

How to Play
This game is played in rounds. Each round consists  of the following actions:
1. Dealer assigns customer order cards.
2. All players select food tiles.
3. All players cook food items.
4. All players serve their customers, if they are able.

After all four actions are completed, the chef’s spoon and the customer order deck are passed clockwise to the next player, and a new round begins.

1. Assigning Customers
The player with the chef’s spoon is the dealer for each round. The dealer draws customer order cards equal to the number of players. The dealer then assigns one customer order to each player, including themselves. You can keep a more difficult card with the higher risk and point value for yourself or assign it to an opponent in hopes of giving them more than they can handle. All players then place their customer order cards on an empty table space within the Orders section of their diner board. In later rounds, if all the table spaces on a player’s diner board are full, they must immediately serve one of their current customers, even if that order will be incomplete, before receiving a new customer order card for that round.

2. Selecting Food Tiles 
Starting with the dealer, players take turns in clockwise order selecting food tiles from the kitchen caddy based on their orders and cooking times. Each player may take up to a total of seven food tiles, from up to three different food types. This is the maximum; you are not required to select more food tiles than you want or need. Take your selected food tiles, and place them in a stack on their corresponding spaces in the Prep column of your diner board.

3. Cooking Food
All players cook their food at the same time. When everyone is ready, the dealer for the round announces, “Cook time!” Now, starting with the top row and working your way down, move all food tiles on your diner boards one space to the right. Keep each round’s stack of tiles together as they move across the board. Some food types do not need to stop in each column. When a tile reaches a space with an arrow, slide it across the arrow until it reaches the column indicated. Once food items are in the Ready to Serve spaces, they can be used to serve any combination of orders (see Serving Customers section).Any unserved food in the final column of the Ready to Serve section will be moved into the Trash column during cook time.

4. Serving Customers
Players may serve their customers during actions 1 or 4 using any food tiles that are in the Ready to Serve area. When you are ready to serve a customer’s order, move the food tiles from your Ready to Serve area and place them on the corresponding spaces of the customer order card to check that you have the correct food items.

End of Game 
Complete the round in which the last customer order cards have been assigned. Then the game enters the final phase. Still taking turns going clockwise, each player has the chance to select one last set of food tiles to place on their diner board. Cooking then occurs as normal. The final phase then continues with all players cooking their food and serving their remaining customer orders (repeating  actions 3 and 4). The game ends once all customer order cards are removed and all food tiles have been served or moved into the Trash column.

Scoring
Players total the scores from their served customer order cards and subtract any penalty tokens received to determine their final scores. The player with the most points wins. In the case of a tie, the player with the fewest penalty tokens wins. If there is still a tie, the player with the five highest-point customer order cards wins.

-description from publisher

Shapes and lines are essential building blocks in our visual vocabulary. If you look, you will see rectangles, squares, cones, triangles, circles, straight lines and curved lines in everything around you. The ability to identify basic shapes and how they relate to each other is the foundation for understanding and solving more complex geometrical and patterning problems.

Asymbol provides you with 47 unique shapes to pick from as you assemble things, beings, places and events/activities to challenge others to guess your vision!

Object of the Game
Players take turns being an Asymbler (builder) by drawing a card, choosing one of the two subjects and using the various shaped pieces to create their subject. At the same time, the other players try to guess what is being created. The correct guesser and the Asymbler each earn a point. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Setup
Place all 47 Asymbol pieces in the middle of the play area.

Shuffle the card deck, and place it facedown next to the Asymbol pieces, creating a draw pile.

Give each player a pass token to be placed near them.

Hand the scorepad and pencil to a player to keep score for each round.

When starting the game, determine how many rounds to play before the game ends. A round ends when each player has had the chance to be the Asymbler.

The first player to name a shape starts as the first Asymbler.

Make sure the Asymbler can reach the pieces and has enough space in front of them to build.

How to Play
On your turn as the Asymbler, draw the top card from the deck, keeping it hidden from the other players, and choose one of its two subjects.

Pass Token
If both subjects are unfamiliar or too challenging, you may use your pass token to discard your current card and draw a new one from the draw pile. After using the token, return it to the box. Each player has only one opportunity to use their token as an Asymbler, so choose wisely.

Categories
When you are ready to start, announce the category of your chosen subject.  Only share the category and not the subject you are going to create.

Assembling and Guessing
To create your subject, use any number of the Asymbol pieces from the play area. You can create with pieces lying flat or by building vertically. Moving the pieces is allowed to show motion and help portray your subject. You may not speak while you build, but making sound effects is allowed. You may use body language (nods and hand gestures) to indicate if a player’s guess is on the right track or not. Once you begin to assemble, all the other players can start guessing. They guess at the same time and can continue to make guesses until someone’s guess is correct. There is no time limit, but you may end your turn if you feel like no one can correctly guess the subject. Otherwise, the turn ends after a correct guess, and play continues clockwise.

Scoring
The first player to correctly guess the subject gets a point. If multiple players guess correctly at the same time, they all get a point. As Asymbler, you also receive a point for successfully creating a subject that another player could recognize. If there are no correct guesses and you choose to end your turn, then no one scores any points that turn.  Return all the pieces you used to the play area, and place your card in the discard pile. Play continues clockwise with the next player becoming the Asymbler, drawing a new card, announcing a category and creating their subject with the pieces.

End of Game 
After the agreed upon number of rounds has been completed, the player with the most points wins. In case of a tie, the players share the victory.

-description from publisher

boardgame: ATS To the Volga I: Fight For The Tractor Works
description:
The Advanced Tobruk System returns to the tractor works of Stalingrad with their first truly new map of this battleground in nearly 20 years.  It's a big one, too, with nine 18½ x 12½ cardstock map panels on which to command your German or Soviet forces.
Contents:
9 linking map panels, each 18½ x 12½"
10 new scenarios
2 countersheets providing German and Soviet infantry, weapons and armoured fighting vehicles
AFV Cards
Four-page Battlefield Walkaround with module-specific rules

Ownership of the ATS Rulebook, play aids and system markers are required to use this product.

The time is summer, 1941. Operation Barbarossa is in full swing, and action on the northern front brings the German 6th Panzer Division on the scene in a headlong rush for Leningrad ... the prize of Armeegruppe Nord.

This is a monster game with a brand new map bisected by the Luga River. This is ATS' first significant treatment of early-war armour on the Eastern Front featuring Pz35 and Pz38 tanks duking it out with the likes of Soviet T-26, T-37 and BT-5 tanks, among many others

Contents:
12 linking map panels, each 18½ x 12½"
8 new scenarios
6 countersheets providing German and Soviet infantry, weapons and early-war armoured fighting vehicles
AFV Cards
Battlefield Walkaround with module-specific rules

Ownership of the ATS Rulebook, play aids and system markers are required to use this product.

boardgame: ATS Small Unit Actions I: The Capture of Balta
description:
The Advanced Tobruk System takes you to the village of Balta, 120 miles northwest of Odessa not far from the border of Ukraine and Romania.  It was there in 1941 that a joint assault on the village by German and Romanian units was interrupted by a pre-emptive Soviet attack, kicked off by a wave fighter-bombers and followed by stiff, combined-arms resistance from within the town itself.  This module marks the debut of Romanian troops and equipment in the ATS milieu.

Contents:
12 linking map panels, each 18½ x 12½"
11 new scenarios
AFV Cards
Two-page Battlefield Walkaround with module-specific rules

Ownership of the ATS Rulebook, play aids and system markers are required to use this product, as well as Romanian personnel, Romanian Weapons/Vehicles and Soviet Early-war vehicles countersheets.

boardgame: ATS Operation Uranus: Stavka Bear Trap

As winter set in, the trap laid carefully by the crafty Russian Bear, vengeance to be exacted by his STAVKA Generals and those in the field ... snapped shut on hapless Axis Satellite soldiers from Romania along with Werhmacht soldiers from 4th Panzer Army and other formations. As the icy blasts swept across the Steppe, one of the greatest military calamities in history was unleashed upon the men of multiple nations. It was Operation URANUS.

This module provides comprehensive rules for waging winter war as well as some new winter-themed terrain features.

Contents:
6 linking map panels, each 18½ x 12½", with new winter art
8 new scenarios
AFV Cards
Two-page Battlefield Walkaround with winter-specific rules

Ownership of the ATS Rulebook, play aids and system markers are required to use this product AND Romanian personnel, Romanian Vehicles and early-war Soviet Vehicles.

boardgame: Stay TF Inside: The Game of Social Distancing
description:
A fun, fast-paced card game just for adults.

This card game is all about stocking up so that you survive the pandemic!

Trade, steal, or panic buy as many necessities as you can to reign supreme during quarantine.

The FIRST player to collect 3 full colored ESSENTIAL ITEM sets, WINS!

Quick Play
1. Deal 5 cards to each player & put the rest in the draw pile at the center.
2. The player seated to the left of the dealer starts the round & draws 2 cards.
3. Play up to 3 moves during your turn in any of the following ways:
 - Put TP cards into your "stockpile" as currency
 - Lay Essential Item cards face up on the board in front of you to build your sets
 - Play a Pandemic card of Collect TP to F*ck over other players!

PRO TIP: Always keep a well-stocked pile of TP. You never know when sh*t will hit the fan and you'll have to pay out.

The Advanced Tobruk System returns to Poland and the very beginning of Hitler's blitzkrieg.  This module contains scenarios set in Sept. 1939 as brave Poles desperately attempt to stave off their relentless German attackers.

Contents:
4 linking map panels, each 18½ x 12½"
8 new scenarios
2 half-countersheets providing Polish infantry, weapons and armoured fighting vehicles
AFV Cards
Two-page Battlefield Walkaround with module-specific rules

Ownership of the ATS Rulebook, play aids and system markers are required to use this product, along with standard German personnel, weapons and AFV counters.

Go on a journey of discovery with the MONOPOLY Biel / Bienne

Biel / Bienne is the largest bilingual city in Switzerland and the second largest city in the canton of Bern.

Biel is the regional and economic center for both the Seeland and the Bernese Jura and parts of the canton of Solothurn. 
It also has several universities of applied sciences. 
Bienne is the most important center of the Swiss watch industry, ahead of Le Locle, Grenchen and La Chaux-de-Fonds.

All spaces are in French and German and the game is supplied with two packs of cards, one for each language.

The Tour de Belgique, officially Baloise Belgium Tour, is a stage race.

It is a 4 stage race.

Short Fuse is all about not blowing up. Players take turns playing down cards to try and get the other players to accumulate "fuse" cards in their bunker, trying to be the last person to not have played down a bomb card with all three "fuse" cards in their bunker. On their turns, as an extra twist, players have to blindly pick a card from another player's hand and play that down as well.

-description from publisher

Four pretty fairies are invited to the grand ball of the castle. But before spinning in a carriage dancing under the stars, each must retrieve their crown, necklace, fan and handbag.

—description from the publisher

Commonality is a family card game about collecting stuff you find in common. Find the commonality between things and collect sets of common images.

The compact, travel-friendly game comes with 150 cards showing a variety of images.

Setup:


     Lay nine cards faceup in a 3x3 stockpile
     Maintain a draw pile
     Deal three secret cards to each player
     Maintain a reserve area in front of each player.


Game Play:

On each player’s turn, a player can perform one of the following actions first:


     Moves a card from the stockpile to their “reserve”
     Discards a secret card and replaces it with a new card from the draw pile
     Steals a visible card from an opponent’s reserve to their reserve


Finally, if possible …


     Reveals and collects a set.


Once the draw pile has been depleted, shuffle the discard pile into a new draw pile.

Continue playing until no draw cards remain or are playable.
The winner has the most cards. In case of a tie, the youngest player wins.

Set Collection:

Players use one or more of their secret cards along with one or more reserve cards to make a set of at least three cards. Explain the connection between all these cards then flip upside down as a collection. Draw one or more new cards to maintain three secret cards.

We've suggested about 60 commonalities like:


     round
     scary
     flat
     mammal
     wearable
     on the farm
     sports


—description from the publisher

"Are the stars unfamiliar here?" she asked, and the sky grew suddenly dark, the star's patterns alien and exotic. "This is the Wandering Sea. The gods have brought you here, and you must wake them if you wish to return home."

In Sleeping Gods, you and up to 3 friends become Captain Sofi Odessa and her crew, lost in a strange world in 1929 on your steamship, the Manticore. You must work together to survive, exploring exotic islands, meeting new characters, and seeking out the totems of the gods so that you can return home.

Sleeping Gods is a campaign game. Each session can last as long as you want. When you are ready to take a break, you mark your progress on a journey log sheet, making it easy to return to the same place in the game the next time you play. You can play solo or with friends throughout your campaign. It's easy to swap players in and out at will. Your goal is to find at least fourteen totems hidden throughout the world. Like reading a book, you'll complete this journey one or two hours at a time, discovering new lands, stories, and challenges along the way.

Sleeping Gods is an atlas game. Each page of the atlas represents only a small portion of the world you can explore. When you reach the edge of a page and you want to continue in the same direction, you simply turn to a new page and sail onward.

Sleeping Gods is a storybook game. Each new location holds wild adventure, hidden treasures, and vivid characters. Your choices affect the characters and the plot of the game, and may help or hinder your chances of getting home!

Welcome to a vast world. Your journey starts now.

—description from the publisher

The Kickstarter edition came in a bigger and nicer box (featuring gold foil and embossing) than the retail edition, featured a painted ship miniature, and included the Sleeping Gods: Dungeons expansion and a set of metallic coins.

The RVM023 product code on the back of the box also indicates the Kickstarter edition.

description:

Do you want to play with Vampirella?

Two to four players will travel around the board attempting to capture monsters (or wrest them away from their captors) until they have the entire slate of monsters on one side of the four sided board captured. Play as Adam, Conrad (aka Dr. Van Helsing), Pendragon or even Vampirella herself!

Port Royal: Big Box contains the Port Royal, the first expansion Just One More Contract..., the second expansion The Adventure Begins..., the Gambler promo card, and the standalone game Port Royal: Make Sail!.

Amino is an entertaining strategic tile based game for the whole family. Amino takes players on a journey into the microcosm of ants.

In Amino - The Ant Game you build a microcosm that every time looks different. In this microcosm you manage an ant colony, and to win you must be the first to collect the best resources. To do this, you lay out new paths and open up new resources and hill. With your opponent you fight for these resources and obstruct their pathways. With "Ant cards" you block paths, build bridges, lend wings to your ant, improve their fighting power or change the existing maze of paths.

The goal of the game is to quickly get the best resources for yourself. There are three different resources, which give you one to three points depending on their type. Now, some may be worth more or less to you than they are to your opponent. The first player to collect seven points wins.

boardgame: Escape The Box: Die vergessene Pyramide
description:
Erlebt eine Schatzjagd im alten Ägypten und erforscht neun Räume, um dem großen Escape-Room-Spiel gemeinsam zu entkommen. Löst alle Rätsel und findet alle Glyphen, Schätze und Geheimnisse, um die Mission eurer Spielfiguren zu meistern. Mit bis zu 4 Spielern erforscht ihr die sagenumwobene Pyramide, gefüllt mit uralten Mysterien, tückischen Fallen und unbeschreiblichen Reichtümern. Aber nehmt euch vor den ägyptischen Göttern in Acht. Manche könnten euch zwar gewogen sein, andere aber auch euer Verderben bedeuten.

Discover a cooperative adventure in the world of mummies, burial chambers and sand, warm sand everywhere you look! Experience a treasure hunt in ancient Egypt and explore nine rooms in order to escape the great escape room game together. Solve all puzzles and find all glyphs, treasures and secrets to master the mission of your characters. With up to 4 players you explore the legendary pyramid, filled with ancient mysteries, treacherous traps and indescribable riches. But beware of the Egyptian gods. Some could be good for you, but others could also mean your ruin.

Setup:
Plant all the vegetables: carrots, turnips, radishes... Don't  forget the rabbits! 
Push the wooden vegetables into the felt, only the green stalk must still protrude. 
Finally place the scarecrow in the center to hold everything.

Gameplay:
Mémotager offers two game modes: 

     a classic memory in which identical pairs have to be gathered
     an advanced mode, in which a soup recipe has to be recomposed. The first player to collect the ingredients for their soup wins.

In Uncle Creepy's Monster Match, you are attempting to amass an army of monsters by collecting two matching monsters from each of the four sides of the game board - the first player to do this wins! And everyone else presumably has a lot of hungry monsters to feed. Will they turn on their captors? You bet!

2 to 6 players spin a paper spinner to move about the board and may claim a monster if they land on it. If you land on an already claimed monster, you must battle the owner for control by betting one of your own monsters against theirs- the highest spin wins them both! As a side option, players may also offer to trade monsters to other players during their turn.

This is one of five somewhat tongue-in-cheek games from Warren magazines (publishers of Creepy, Eerie, Vampirella and others) featuring rad 70s monster artwork.

Monster Match was released in Creepy Magazine #56 (September 1973), Eerie Magazine #51 (September 1973), and the "Famous Monsters Game Book #1" magazine from June of 1982 as "cut out and play editions", as well as available separately in a bundle as "Warren Monster Madness" games by mail order (#2661 for $2.50). The bundle included Capture, Monster Match, Creepy-Crawley Castle (aka Creepy Castle), Werewolf! and Monster's Castle.

Shake the eggs 2 by 2 to find the pairs. 
An original sound memo with no shelf life!

Grab an egg, shake it and try to find the other egg which makes the same sound. A game of listening and awakening the senses with a box of 12 wooden eggs.

Winner is the player who can associate the most sounds.

Le jeu de No , 
the game of No combines luck, strategy and knowledge about the Island of Noirmoutier through questions.

A family game that can be played with children, with questions designed for the younger ones.

Go on foot, by bike, by boat for a fun island tour!

Questions on expressions in patois, activities, the coast, fauna, flora ... not to mention the traffic rules on the road and by the sea.
1,200 questions on Noirmoutier island and on each card a photo of the island.

Take on the role of a farmer trying to build up your farm with pigs, cows, chickens, and sheep

Compete with your fellow players doing the same thing. Try to disrupt their farms while protecting your own.

Collect all three pairs of one kind of farm animal to win the game

Use other card types to buy, sell, trade, and support your farm - or to sabotage your neighbors

Make blind trades with your neighboring farmers to get what you need. Trades are limited only by your creativity

You can choose to be honest in your business dealings, but there is no guarantee other players will!

—description from the designer

BUTTON MASHERS! is a fast-paced, pocket-sized card game for 2 players! Combo punches and kicks while trying to avoid your opponents attacks!

Great to take on the go or for a quick break between larger games, your luck and guessing skills will be put to the test! Guess correctly, and avoid damage. Guess wrong, and you'll take a foot to the face! Don't let your health reach zero!

Variable rules make it easy to adjust the game for all ages! Make it a simple guessing game, or change it up, and make it a game about reading your opponent's moves, or make up your own rules!

Players each have an identical deck of various punches and kicks. After deciding who goes first, the defending player guesses whether the next card to be revealed will be a high(↑), mid, or low(↓) attack. If they guess correctly, they take no damage and become the new attacker. However, if they guess wrong, they take the damage listed on the card and must guess again until they make a correct guess.

For more player control, have the attacking player select which card to put face down instead of just flipping the top card of the deck.

Players start with 25 life. First to reach 0 loses and gives their best cry of defeat!
Ready... FIGHT ON!!!

—description from the designer

Flockade is a laid-back, party game with strategic elements about outwitting and outlasting your opposition.

Command an army of farm animals lead your flock to victory through attacking, defending, deception, and disruption, protecting your general at all costs to be the last flock standing. If your General goes down, you lose the game.

Each general offers its own powerful effect and playstyle to build your flock around. Build an army of basic recruits and powerful heroes to defend your General and attack enemy armies. Use orders to instantly take action or disrupt incoming actions and apply sanctions to other players for powerful shifts in the game if you catch them breaking a treaty!

To win, you'll have to manage your hand wisely and play your cards at the right times for the maximum effect. Shear your enemies with weapons, wolves, and wit to win and wool the world!

What's in the game?
Cards for sevenczones of the world.  Each zone is a different colour and has cards for ten cities (numbered 1-10), plus a Travel Cuide card for the zone. Travelling a zone is done by placing down cards of the same zone (colour), beginning with a low number and ending with a high one (up to ten).  Travel Guide and Travel Insurance cards can be used to gain extra points but they must be played at the beginning of a trip.  Players can go on more than one trip.

The standard game is played by two people.  There are extra rules for four players (two teams).

It is a War Game that reproduces a total of 5 battles in 5 game turns.

Players compete for victory based on the total number of area-dominated victory points that they check each turn.

This game is included in "WARGAME HANDBOOK 2019".

The expansion includes 2 more exciting Fighters that can completely change the game with their speed.

Add Keane the Hurricane and Yabai the Scarlet Fiend, each with a unique ability related to speed.
