     Justify the Losses – Le Haut du Bosq, Normandy, June 26th 1944
     Siegel’s Stand 1 – North of Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 27th 1944
     Siegel’s Stand 2 – North of Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 27th 1944
     Position be Buggered! - Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 27th 1944
     Shout for Piats – Le Haut du Bosq, Normandy, June 27th 1944
     Grainville-Sur-Odon - Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 27th 1944
     We Blessed Them – Belval, Normandy, June 29th 1944
     Destruction of a Squadron – Belval, Normandy, June 29th 1944
     DF 109 – Belval, Normandy, July 1st 1944
     Wide Diamond - Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 28th 1944
     The Breakthrough – South of Grainville-Sur-Odon, Normandy, June 28th 1944

Which good thing is gooder—Brunch Mimosas or Seeing a Bunch of Hot Air Balloons? Air Conditioning or A Clean Public Restroom? 272 Debate Cards and 36,856 possible pairings make up Good vs Gooder, a party game where teams create random matchups and debate which good thing is gooder. Let's share stories, laugh together, and start spreading some goodness!

All players divide into three teams and draw five Debate Cards. Two teams submit their favorite Debate Card. The third team flips over the two Debate Cards and judges Round 1. Teams try to persuade the judges with stories, jokes, real-life experiences, and fun facts. After a few minutes, the judges deliberate and vote for which team was more persuasive.

The winning team draws either a Good Card or a Gooder Card. All Good Cards are worth one point. Some Gooder Cards earn teams more than one point. Some are worth negative points. And some are action cards (like forcing your opponent to impersonate a celebrity during the debate). Teams must decide whether to play it safe or take a chance!

After Round 1, rotate which team judges and keep playing until you've completed 12 total debates. Whichever team has accumulated the most points after the last debate is declared the Goodest of All Time!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Siva Afi: The Fireknife Dance of Samoa
description:
For centuries, Samoan warriors have spun and tossed their weapons in dazzling demonstrations of courage and skill. The traditional wooden war clubs were later replaced with hooked steel machetes. In 1946, a young knife dancer, “Freddie” Letuli, was performing in San Francisco at an event that also featured a Hindu fire eater and a girl who twirled a baton with light bulbs on the ends. Letuli wrapped some towels around the ends of his knife, dipped them in fuel, set them alight, and invented fireknife dancing.

The fireknife dance (siva afi) is now a major feature of Samoan culture and the pinnacle of any luau.

In this game, 2-6 players are fireknife dancers. The game alternates between a practicing phase (where players improve their hands) and a performing phase (where players dance by playing cards). In each dance, the best performer earns points. The first player to score a certain number of points wins the game.

This reimplementation of Fireknife! was developed and tested with the help of professional fireknife dancers. All card text is bilingual (Samoan and English). The cards feature gorgeous art by Samoan artist Troy Safotu.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
We first encountered the Shard under the guise of peaceful trading. They had fairly priced and exotic goods, even if they were a bit eclectic. It wasn't long until some of our ships disappeared. Missing ships aren't unusual in this galaxy, but finding one of our Gatling Lasers in the Shard's trade inventory was... unexpected. It takes nerve to sell goods back to the people you stole them from - and we'd learned how much to trust our new friends.

Expand your Moonrakers universe with new contracts, crew, and ship parts. Includes 15 cards and is compatible with all versions of Moonrakers.

Available first through Veiled Fate Kickstarter and Moonrakers official website.

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL III – Over There! American Great War
description:
GWASL III – Over There!

In this expansion the Americans enter the fray with all the fanfare that was mustered as they boarded ships to save Europe. 
The proud, but untested ‘doughboys’ could not have known what was waiting for them ‘over there’ specifically, battle-hardened German soldiers, led by officers that knew victory first-hand. And were fighting for the Vaterland....

This expansion contains rules, geo boards and counters for the Americans.

Ownership of GWASL I and GWASL II is required... 
as you know the Battle of Belleau Wood pitted Americans, alongside French and British troops as they faced down the Kaiser’s 1918 Spring Offensive!

Scenarios:

     Honored Ground – Brancourt, France, October 8th 1918
     Young Blood and Guts – Pannes, France, September 5th 1918
     Hollenhunde – Belleau Wood, France, June 6th 1918
     Hold Where You Stand – Marigny, France, June 3rd 1918
     Riposte at Chinese Trench – Vierstraat, Belgium, September 1st 1918
     Outworks Objectives – East of Ronssoy, France, September 27th 1918
     Cher Ami – Argonne Forest, France, October 4th 1918
     Cantigny Stand – Cantigny, France, May 28th 1918

Fertile Ground is found all over the world, and as a Gardener, you endeavor to find and Sow the unique flora of the globe in your own Garden. As your Garden grows, your plants help and guide you along this path, accelerating your progress while also necessitating thoughtful orientation of new plants in your Garden. But you're not alone; other Gardeners are watching, and working just as hard to create the most productive Garden by the end of Winter - and only one will prevail!

Gardeners begin with a hand of between 1-7 of the 200 unique Plant Cards and work to Sow these into their Garden. As they do so, they will need to Collect resources (Soil, Sun, Water, Seed) to pay for the cards as well as make sure that they have appropriate space where each plant can survive. Taking turns, Gardeners collect resources, gather cards, and Sow plants into their Gardens; as they accumulate more plants and their associated abilities, their capacity to do each of these increases drastically. The game proceeds through the four seasons (rounds) in which each Gardener has a set number of turns, and at the end of each season Gardeners Pollinate their plants to gather Harvest. At the end of Winter, the value of the accumulated plants, resources, various tokens, and more determine the total value of each Garden, and thus the winner of the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Altar Quest: Call of the Lunarin
description:
Call of the Lunarin is an Expansion to Altar Quest designed with the collaboration of Isaac Childres from Gloomhaven. It adds an additional playable hero : Leyson Pines, with its associated hero upgrade cards for campaign play. It also adds werewolves, packmasters and Moon children miniatures along with a deck of 60 new cards including threat cards and lurker cards.
This expansion was included in Altar Quest Stretch goals box from the associated Kickstarter campaign.

This Expansion contains :

     2 Heroes miniatures (1 human form + 1 werewolf form)
     5 Werewolves miniatures
     5 Moon Children miniatures
     5 Packmasters miniatures
     60 cards deck (including Hero deck, Hero upgrade cards, Threat Deck, Lurker cards)

boardgame: Clank! In! Space!: The Last Starkiller
description: Clank! In! Space!: The Last Starkiller is a promotional card you can use with the Pulsarcade expansion, with this all-purpose card giving you either 3 credits or a 1-token arcade cabinet when you acquire it.

War is imminent. You and your opponent will battle head-to-head, trying to fashion the strongest trench, using secrecy and special cards to bolster your line. Will you try to strike first and declare war? Or will tensions reach the point of no return...

Obliquity: The Trenches is a quick print and play card game that sees players building up their trench, using merely two decks. The variety and possibilities in this game means that no two trenches will ever be the same. Cards will provide Power Points, which is the ultimate decider of who wins in the game, and is provided by infantry cards and bolstered by support cards. Special cards can provide great advantages, if the trench can support their particular placement rules.

Part face-off and part-puzzle, all the cards have their own placement peculiarities, which will need to be thought about. Players will need to decide which place, out of the available places, would the card be best, to facilitate future potential card draws. This is ultimately the crux of the game, the construction of the trench through quick and simple puzzle-solving.

And if that isn't enough, then the Buildings and Bombs expansion allows players to construct buildings in their trenches and utilise No Man's Land, adorning it with dangerous defences.

Good Luck, General.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zombicide: Undead or Alive – Running Wild
description:
It introduces a whole posse of Survivors that can be either on foot, or riding their trusted horses. But it's not all good news, as this expansion also adds the nightmare of a zombie horse!

Each Survivor begins the game on horseback. When they dismount from their horse to enter buildings, their miniatures are swapped and they leave their Lone Horse behind (as a token).

While on horseback, the riders gain skills that enhance their movement and increase their chances of a hit.  Individual riders have specialized Blue level skills that add to their repertoire while in Rider mode.

The Running Wild expansion comes with:

6 Survivor Figures
6 Rider Survivor Figures
1 Abomination Figure
6 Survivor ID Cards
1 Abomination Card
6 Lone Horse Tokens
6 Oversized Color Bases

boardgame: Zombicide: Undead or Alive – Deadstock Abomination Pack
description:
This expansion brings 4 massive Abominations, their cards and a rules leaflet to step up the danger level of missions.

Features: Bizon, Blood Bull, Texas Deadhorn, and Abominabear.

boardgame: Zombicide: Undead or Alive – Special Guest Paolo Parente
description:
Survivors that were specially created for Zombicide: Undead or Alive by Paolo Parente. Expansion contains:


     9 Survivors
     9 Survivor ID Cards

boardgame: Zombicide: Undead or Alive – Abominape vs Crocosaur
description:
This set adds these two massive abominations to your game.

The Abominape is so large that it treats Survivors as its (very tasty) playthings. If it hasn’t done so already, the Abominape snatches up a Survivor in its zone, disabling them until it has been eliminated by a Dynamite.

The Crocosaur is a hardy monstrosity. When it is hit with a Dynamite or Damage 3 weapon, roll a die. On a 4+, it survives the attack! The only way to guarantee its defeat is to spend 2 Dynamite cards instead of 1 when throwing a Dynamite at it.

The Abominape vs Crocosaur Pack contains:


     1 Abominape figure
     1 Crocosaur Figure
     2 Abomination Cards

The game of the Pays Charolais-Brionnais is a local board game based on the principle of questions and answers.

Players start in one of the centers of the Pays Charolais-Brionnais, Briant, Lugny-les-Charolles, Cronat... 
and then go discover all the sites with 550 questions relating to geography, history, celebrities, traditions, heritage....

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL IV – Samsonov's Army: Russian Great War
description:
GWASL IV Samsonov’s Army takes the action to the Great War’ Eastern Front and pits the soldiers of General Ludendorff against invading Russians under General Samsonov. The dark forests, forced-marches, and human waves of a low-morale, poorly led force ... face the crack troops of a still ascendant Germany. The horror of a two-front war is upon the Kaiser’s best leaders and there is only one way out: total victory.

Historians know the battle took place near Allenstein. However, General Erich Ludendorff named it after Tannenberg, some 30 klicks to the west, to avenge the defeat of the Teutonic Knights at the earlier battle of the same name that occurred in 1410.

The world was a far different place during the summer of 1914. Germany was on the offensive on the Western Front. Trench warfare had yet to set in. Crowds, out in the streets welcomed war with open arms, and cheers. A massive Russian Army headed for East Prussia, and eventually, the heart of Germany ... in an unprecedented invasion—ten Russian Armies in all could be mobilized. Hindenburg was called out of retirement to manage the German side along with Ludendorff. Despite overwhelming numerical advantages by the enemy, the battle resulted in the almost complete destruction of the Russian Second Army, and the suicide of its commanding general, Alexander Samsonov.

A series of follow-up battles (1st Masurian Lakes) destroyed most of the First Army as well, and kept the Russians off-balance until the spring of 1915. The Battle of Tannenberg set the stage for the First Battle of the Masurian Lakes a week later, where the reinforced German Eighth Army now faced only the Russian First Army and forced it back over the prewar border. 
Russian forces would not again march on German soil until the end of World War II.
 Ownership of GWASL I is required. 
Includes eight scenarios, two sheets of counters, all the geo boards needed to play the scenarios, folio, and special rules.

Scenarios:

     Opening Blow – Stalluponen, East Prussia, August 17th 1914
     Disaster at Gumbinnen - Gumbinnen, East Prussia 1914
     Fresh Blood – Usdau, East Prussia, August 27th 1914
     Samsonov’s End – Frogenau, East Prussia, August 29th 1914
     The Kerensky Offensive – Lvov, Ukraine, July 1st 1917
     Sacrifice for Joffre – Vilna, Russia, March 20th 1916
     Action Along the Wilja – Vileyka, Russia, September 22nd 1915
     East of Sosenka – East of Sosenka, Russia, September 24th 1915

At the dawn of man, tribes fought against both nature and others of their own kind to secure their slim chance at survival. Their ability to adapt and evolve in the face of constant hardship allowed the strongest of those tribes to succeed. You are in control of one of these tribes, and their future is entirely in your hands.

PrimEVO is a miniatures agnostic skirmish system intended to be played solo or among a group of people. It offers quick gameplay (1 vs 1) and offers not only world challenges that all tribes must face together, but also those that they will have to overcome individually. Literally no two campaigns will be alike.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ATTLO: Avoid Taking The Last One
description:
Nim variants with six different boards and fifteen counters. Players take turns to remove one or two adjacent counters that must be joined by a black line. The player who is forced to take the last counter loses the game.

Variant 1: players take turns to remove at least one counter or at most a row of counters provided there is no missing counters in between.

Variant 2: players take turns to remove from one to three adjacent counters with no missing counters in between, but a players must not take the same number of counters as the previous player.

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL V – Hapsburg Ambitions: Austria-Hungary
description:
A key player in the Central Powers, Austria-Hungary and its territorial ambitions represented a match to the tinder-box that become World War I. 
The Hapsburgs, namely Emperor Franz Josef, dreamed the dreams of conquerors. Ever an expansionist, Josef’s latest conquest was Bosnia-Herzegovina. And it was a Bosnian Serb that assassinated Crown Prince Franz Ferdinand, in Sarajevo. The Austrian ultimatum to Serbia followed on 28 July 1914. On 1 August Germany declared war on Russia, the protector of the Slavs. Within a few days Austria declared war on Russia, then on Belgium on 7 August. By the end of 1914, Austria-Hungary and Germany would be at war with France, Britain and Japan, and later, following the attack on Serbia, Montenegro. There were setbacks in the campaign against Russia in Galicia. The Austrian army had more success in 1915, and by the middle of 1916 had conquered Serbia and Montenegro, pushed the Russians back from Poland and by the end of 1916 a combined German-Austrian-Bulgarian attack knocked out Romania. The Austrians also had to face Italian attacks on the South Tirol and the Isonzo River. It was the success of British and French reinforcements, reaching the Balkans and Italy, that forced the Austrian Emperor to sue for peace in October 1918. On 29 October, as the Austrian Army fell back from the Piave River, British and Italian aircraft fired into the fleeing columns. 
The Austrian armistice took effect on 4 November 1918. 
A total of 1,495,200 Austro-Hungarian soldiers died during World War I.

Ownership ASL-RB, GWASL I and all preceding GWASL modules are required to play.

Scenarios:

     Polish Legion – Kostiuchnowka, Ukraine, June 8th 1916
     Bread Not Blood – Radoszyce, Galicia, November 20th 1914
     Brzo, Brzo! – Radoszyce, Hungary, November 17th 1914
     Polish Hill – Kolki, Hungary, July 4th 1916
     To The Very Last Hour – Przemysl, Galicia, March 20th 1915
     Cossack Terror – Buczacz, Hungary, June 8th 1916
     Opportunity In Bukovina – Okna, Austria-Hungary, June 6th 1916
     The Rojische Bridgehead – Rojische, Ukraine, June 9th 1916
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You are not alone...

Escape from Mars is a single player hidden traitor game.

An alien virus has infected the crew of Mars Base Epsilon, the first off world colony, and strange insect like creatures swarm the outpost. Players must discover and quarantine the alien host, living among them, inside one of the crew, they must also gather alien gene samples, then via the communication room, request rescue. It’s a race against time as crew become infected, drone aliens will be drawn to the colony until eventually an alpha alien will arrive and wreak havoc on the colony and its crew.

Scour the colony for useful items and weapon, locate the clues to discover the alien host and send them to quarantine, keep crew infection down, research the alien gene sample codes, deal with crisis events and distrustful crew members, seal off parts of the base and set defenses, send the robot to rescue fallen crew, prepare medicines and vaccines, stock up on ammo, jump in a space suit and search the plant surface, destroy the alien drones then hold out until the rescue ship arrives, just make sure you get home...alone.

The game is played over a set number of rounds (which also determined the difficulty), a crew marker counts down the rounds and at the other end of the round tracker an Alien marker moves in the opposite direction, if the markers ever meet, the Alpha Alien will arrive and the ‘Host’ crew member will die.... or evolve?

Players start off with a limited number of active crew, some must be rescued, other will be distrustful and need convincing to join. Player must scour the ship looking for clues as to the identity of the Alien Host, looking for blood samples and crew logs, players also need to gather alien samples for HQ. The colony is also full of weapons, tools, events, and more. Players risk crew when venturing out in colony as the longer they search, the more exposure to the alien virus they risk. The game is about managing and taking risks, use your crew wisely as they have different strengths and weaknesses.

The game ends and the players win, when they have discovered and quarantined the Alien Host, found and reported the Alien samples to HQ, all get into the airlock and the rescue ship arrives.

The player loose if no one makes it out alive, and this can happen in many ways...

Turbulences is a pick-up-and-deliver game in which players need to pay attention to the weather — and the mountains! — while making their way from town to town.


The Collector's Edition includes every component in one box: the basic game, the expert mode, the mystery box and the collector pack, including, e.g. three legendary aircrafts.

boardgame: Seinfeld: The Party Game About Nothing
description:
Celebrate the hit ’90s TV show in this hilarious head-to-head party game that will challenge what you know about the “show about nothing.”

Score points by answering trivia questions, charades-style acting, and guessing who said or did some of the most hilarious bits ever aired on television! You’ll even get a chance to fish for the marble rye like George and Jerry did!

It’s hundreds of Seinfeld-celebrating activities for outrageous fun!

Players will form teams of two and each turn roll the challenge die. There are five types of challenges and one "Newman!" icon. The different challenges will be asked to the current team and if they get them correct they will score some points.

The first team to 70 points or more wins! However if both teams have at least 50 points, one team may skip their regular turn to attempt the Marble Rye dexterity challenge (yes, just like the show!)

-description from publisher

Gods & Monsters is the first expansion for PrimEVO and deals with Jurassic elements of play including more scenarios, more additional rules, 11 dinosaur species each with their own AI, and a system allowing your tribe to build their God as they see fit while periodically bringing them to the battlefield, all of which can be incorporated with everything in the core book.

- description from publisher

The Sound of Metal is a fully cooperative (or solo) tabletop miniatures skirmish game. With a rogue-lite campaign system, players take on the roles of survivors of humans, trying to get by after the downfall of civilization, a result of the Great Robot Uprising.

After a large meteor crashes into the planet, the Robot overlords start to experience communication issues and many start to malfunction. Humans dubbed this event as "The Glitch" and see it as their opportunity to take back their planet. This is no easy task, as the human population is just a fraction of what it once was, and communication is a challenge. Although the Robots have been greatly disrupted, they are still, in every sense of the word, a great threat.

Players create survivors, find a vehicle, and traverse the countryside, gathering resources, finding food and weapons, and learning skills to complete mission objectives. In doing so, players characters will level up, gain notoriety, and try to find and destroy the machines central core.

The game features an action-point based system - These points double as action points and your characters health. As your character takes wounds, they lose action points. Run out of action points, and you collapse.

Combat/skill checks are performed by rolling dice, bolstered by stats and gained skills.

Similar to the great tactical, turn-based video games of the 90s, such as X-Com and Jagged Alliance, players can spend extra action points to perform actions at higher levels of competency, being able to aim, change their stances, or focus on specific tasks.

Enemies are controlled by the game's AI  - each enemy has its own order of operations reactionary programming. Players face these enemies in campaign missions, as well as random encounters.

The world unfolds in front of the players - as they track their progress on the overworld map. Moving costs food and fuel, and dangers lurk around every corner. The map is be randomly generated each game, and players will find settlements, points of interest and missions in different areas every game.

Players never control more than 6 survivors at a time, and can be played with 1-6 players cooperatively, splitting up the survivors among players.

—description from the designer

Each player has nine pawns. Two players may play as allies. The goal of the game is to score points by entering the citadel in the center of the game board an by capturing enemy pieces.

Movement is one space per turn in any direction, but not diagonal. The pawns may capture enemies by jumping over them. Capture in mandatory if possible. Allied pawns may be jumped over but are not captured. Jumping over pawns of the same color is not permitted.

Pawns that reach the citadel are put out of the game, if they are not compelled to leave right away to capture an enemy pawn. Captured pawns are exchanged and reenter the game on the spaces of the same color.

The game ends, when one allied party has no pawn left on the board or, if there are no allies, when only pawns of one color remain on the board.

—user summary

The life of the little stuffed Teddy comes to life at night, his duty is to take care of the dreams of his child, throughout the hours of the night he must go through a series of adventures with the aim that his child wakes up as calm possible, but you must be careful, the world of nightmares is open and Little Guss is prowling the room, you will have to collect as many magic buttons as possible to help protect your child's dreams.

The Teddy's Adventures is a roll & write style game but with buttons instead of dice, it is played by taking colored buttons out of a bag, from these buttons the active player will choose which buttons will be painted in each section of his game sheet and which button It will be eliminated, in addition to which button the other players will paint, the game sheet will fill with colors as the rules explain, and you will have to check the progress of the board of the world of nightmares that can trigger the end sooner than provided. Once the end of the game is triggered, points will be counted in each section of the sheet and the player who scores more points than the others will win.

—description from the designer

Tri-Dice is a unique, quirky fun two person game. Both players move around a two track board, obtaining playing cards and optionally switching tracks, trying to score points. First player to 30 points wins. Sounds simplistic but there are many twists which require a good deal of strategy. For example, players may "capture" each other leading to cat and mouse chases.

—description from the designer

Horizon Wars: Infinite Dark is a science-fiction spaceship tabletop miniatures wargame. It allows for solitaire, co-operative, and competitive versus play. Players create a fleet of ships, customizing both their statistics and their heroic pilots. When played solo or cooperatively,  the AI adversary is controlled through an AI and a standard deck of playing cards. Scenarios are objective-based and range from one-off encounters to narrative campaigns, complete with character progression.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
To play Entre os Feixes, you just have to grab some bottle caps, print two sheets to cut out, and the rules.

The story of this game takes place thousands of years ago, in the city of Bethlehem of Judea, when poor people collected, among the plantations, ears of wheat and barley that fell to the ground, to satisfy their own hunger. Ruth, the protagonist of the story, is one of these people, a young and foreign widow looking for ways to support her mother-in-law, picking ears in a plantation in the region, because according to Jewish law on behalf the hungry, it was justice for the owners of land to leave the ears that fell to the ground for people in need to collect for themselves. The game is based on chapter 2 from the book of Ruth of the Bible, telling the story of this notorious young foreigner who, because of her faithfulness and dedication, marries the owner of the plantation and joins the genealogical line of Jesus Christ.

A game that, through its mechanics, can be used to address issues such as: social equality, self-preservation, respect for women, the value of loyalty, work, and the correct authority that cares and leads people in a loving way.

In summary, each player plays with 3 pieces that move like queens, but instead of capturing the opponent's pieces, they capture the squares of the board, collecting them to sum points at the end of the game.

(Description by the designer)

Some little gnomes want to start their life as bussiness landlords in the Backyard of Frutabaja, but a monster living there is trying to scare off al clients with dirt.

Players must work as a team to keep the board clean at the same time they try to settle new bussinesses and earn money.

The winner is determined scoring points and reaching goals. But each players will lose if the Blorrrdo places each dirt token in the backyard.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Drax Hero Pack
description:
Killed by Thanos in a wicked act of cruelty, Arthur Douglas was reincarnated as Drax, a knife-wielding warrior with a thirst for vengeance. Now a member of the Guardians of the Galaxy, Drax travels far and wide, driven and determined to put an end to the mad titan once and for all.

As a member of the Guardians of the Galaxy, Drax charges into the scene with unrelenting fury in this expansion pack, which introduces Drax as a brand-new playable hero, along with his fifteen signature cards. With a pre-built Protection deck ready to go, you’ll be able to try out Drax’s unique vengeance-fueled gameplay from the moment you open the box. Punish the villains who wrong you and show them why Drax is a force to be reckoned with!

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
An educational memory game about nature preservation. Find pairs of animals, connect animal families and plant protective forest for them. Can also be played as a classic memory game.

The game includes 16 animal species (2 adults and one baby each), and baobab trees as home trees for species. You try to find the pairs and deliver them to safety. Winner is the one with most animals saved.

Each year, the most skillful master chocolatiers coming from the most remote corners of the world gather to determine who among them will be able to wear the coveted Dark Choco Crown. In order to win, they will need a good sense of risk, a sprinkle of strategy, and a just a pinch of luck: are you ready to sit down at their table and prove what you're made of?
The most skilled master chocolatiers compete to wear the coveted Dark Choco Crown!

In order to win, one needs strategy, luck, and to know when to take risks.


     Draw cards from your own deck. Be careful not to Bust!
     Use them to buy exquisite Ingredients to add to your deck.
     Score bonus points by conquering precious Desserts!

A clown figure is attached to a game board by means of a brass tube. Players take turns throwing rope rings from ten feet away. For every successful throw or "Ringer", 25 points is scored. The player with the highest score after ten turns wins. Alternatively, players can play to an agreed-upon score to win the game.

Remnants of a lost cause. Survivors of a massacre. For a country that abandoned them, the war is over. For this band of men, it has just begun. Left for dead and deserted by their homeland, these survivors of the Blood Creek Massacre have only vengeance as their refuge, stalking their oppressors in the dead of night, becoming themselves the haunting specters of Saratoga and they will not rest until the men who murdered their brothers join them in Hell!

The Blood Creek Bushwhackers posse is one of sabotage and ambush. What they lack in range, they make up for in surprise. When the Bushwhackers are on the table, no space is safe and no loot crate is secure as these gray ghosts can pop up anywhere to attack an unsuspecting target or detonate crates, destroying them entirely and dealing damage to all around them!

Deep in the swamps of Pascagoula County sits a small barrel town of moonshiners founded by the Jenkins family. After the Governor’s violent crackdown of the industry left most of the family dead, the last surviving patriarch has gathered his kin and – with nothing left to lose -  takes the fight straight city hall. But this will be no peaceful protest.

The Jenkins Brothers are a trio of close range punishment. Two of the brothers, Jethro and Jonah, brandish powerful shotguns that execute carnage through cover and doors, while the giant Clifton’s brutish strength and combo attacks sends his opponents flying in every direction.

A retired colonel, hunting the man who murdered his lover, teams up with a gunslinging stranger with no name to form one of the deadliest bounty-hunting posses Saratoga has ever seen. One is quick on the draw, the other has the range of a marksman. Together, they hunt the notorious outlaws of the West, cleaning the streets and filling their pockets.

Monco & McAtte are a dynamic duo that work better together than apart, but can stand on their own if they have to.

This game was edited with the help of scientist Paul-Emile Victor.
The game has two phases.
1. The preparatory cours.
During the preparatory course, the players will gather the equipment necessary for their expedition. Each player rolls the dice and advances his pawn on the dedicated course by following the arrows. He earns material tokens he will need for his expedition and places them on his route plan.
2.The expeditions.
We mix the "panoramas", "jokers" and "stop" cards. each player draws 8 cards at random. The rest is the pickaxe. Each player will then seek to obtain the 8 panorama cards of his expedition by asking for them from his opponents or by iterating them in the deck. Progress is indicated in pencil on the route plan. During the game, players will have to answer general knowledge questions about the poles and thus earn points. The player who completes his expedition first earns 500 bonus points.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Hit parade is a game based on the same spirit as the monopoly one.

The player must collect 4 "Gold disc" from different artist to win the game. To do that they will throw the dice and move their pawn on the board to buy artist cards.

Once a player as an artist card, he should develop them to get higher in the artist ranking until winning a Gold disc.

When an artist card is already taken by a player, an other player can challenge him: the players write on a piece of paper an amount of money. If the one who challenged the card wins, he should take the card and pay the difference between the two amounts to the bank. If the one who was challenged  wins, he keeps his card and the other player must pay him the difference between the two amounts. If the amount are equal, nothing happens.

The player can lose or win money all along the play depending of the boxes they go in (taxes, royalties, bank) and they can sell artists of disc gold to get money back.

When those deputized to enforce justice only sought to twist and corrupt it to their own ends, this Man of Metal and his bushranging renegades took the law into their own hands. But when everyone has their own ideas of order, the compass tends to tailspin into chaos, and that’s precisely what transpired at the Bloodbath of Brunswany, where the unstoppable, armored Nelson Abernathy and his Abernathy Gang went down in infamy as ruthless outlaws with a thirst for violence.

The Abernathy Gang are heavy hitting gunfighters, that can take just as much punishment as they can give. Using torches, the gang can light entire buildings ablaze while Nelson’s impenetrable metal plating absorbs his enemies’ attacks for days and the sight of a brass star brings out a monster in Nelson that has become the nursery rhyme for misbehaving children across the country.

The principle of the game is simple: the players must move from one star to another by the simplest and shortest possible way. Players advance along the constellations, depending on the result of a 12-sided die. If players need to move from one constellation to another, they use a ruler to gauge the distances.
For each journey completed, the players earn points. Whoever has the most wins.

Stigmergy is a drawless territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board).

Stigmergy is based on Michał Zapała's Tumbleweed.

				
				
					DefinitionsTwo stones, or a stone and an empty cell, see each other if they lie on the same straight line of adjacent cells and there are no other stones between them along that line.

You control a cell if the number of stones of your color seen by that cell is greater than half the number of cells (empty or occupied) adjacent to that cell.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Pass, provided that there are no empty cells or every empty cell is controlled by some player.
     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell not controlled by your opponent.
     Remove an enemy stone from a cell you control and place a stone of your color on that cell.


The game ends when both players pass in succession. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the number of stones of your color on the board, plus the number of empty cells you control, plus komi in the case of White. The button is used to avoid ties.

				
				
					Komi and buttonKomi is the whole number of points that is added to White's score at the end of the game as compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, the first player chooses the value of komi, and then the second player chooses sides. Alternatively, experienced players may agree on a standard value for all games.

The button is a special token that is placed next to the board at the start of the game. It is only used when komi is an odd number. On your turn, if neither player has taken the button yet, you may not pass, but you may take the button instead of making a board play. At the end of the game, a half point is added to the score of the player who has taken the button.

Carentan, 10 - 13th June 1944 is an Operational level board-wargame which shows fierce fighting in the 1944 Normandy Campaign between Germans and Americans. In starting phase of the battle enemy armies used elite airborne formations and later less experienced mechanized forces. In effect training and determination factors were balanced. Americans achieved success because of material advantage and better coordination of combined arms actions.
Could the battle outcome have been different? On tactical level German fallschirmjägers had edge, because they were masters in using urban terrain. But lack of ammo and high casualties forced them to leave Carentan. On the other hand Americans used consistently artillery support and they gained ground step by step. These battlefield elements are shown in "Great Battles of Small Units" system. 
Two scenarios are included. 
"Purple Heart Lane" depicts struggle for Carentan between enemy paratroopers. Players can use optional rule and see what would be battle finale, if troops from 17th SS Panzergrenadier Division appeared earlier on the battlefield. 
The scenario "Battle of Bloody Gulch" shows American defence of Carentan against German counterattack. In this fighting American armored units are present as well.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Great battles of Small Units (Strategemata)

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL VI – Blood on the Isonzo: Italian WW1
description:
Italy upended their Central Powers allies by entering the war on the side of the Allies in May of 1915, following secret negotiations. Italy fought mostly against Austria-Hungary along the northern border, including high up in the now-Italian Alps and along the Isonzo river. The war was initially a failure for Italy despite being numerically superior to Austria-Hungary. The Italian army repeatedly attacked Austria, making little progress and suffering heavy losses, and then being routed in 1917 by a German-Austrian counteroffensive after Russia left the war allowing the Central Powers to move reinforcements to the Italian Front from the Eastern Front. In October 1918, as civil unrest increased in Austria-Hungary, the Italians attacked again. The Austrian army broke, and the Italians drove deep into Austrian territory. Fighting ended on 3 November 1918.

Ownership ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     Hill 383 - Plava, Italy, June 20th 1915
     City of Violets - Gorizia, Italy, May 5h 1915
     First Rush - Podgorica, Italy, July 2nd 1915
     Isonzo Marsch - Tolmino, Italy, September 10th 1917
     Tolmino Bridgehead - Tolmino, Italy, September 12th 1917
     Cappuccio Wood - Mount San Michele, Italy, July 25th 1915
     Vrtojba - Vrtojba, Italy, November 4th 1916
     On To Monte Majur - Monte Majur, Italy, October 25th 1917

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL VII – Soldiers of the Sultan: Turkish WW1
description:
The soldiers of the Ottoman Empire fought a variety of combatants during World War I. 
There were five main campaigns in the theatre: the Sinai and Palestine Campaign, the Mesopotamian Campaign, the Caucasus Campaign, the Persian Campaign, and most famously, the Gallipoli Campaign. 
The Ottomans joined the Central Powers, goaded by Germany, in its hope that Russian troops would be drained away from the fronts in Galicia and Poland. Scenarios in this module cover the Battle of Sarikamish, as well as the campaigns in Mesopotamia, Palestine, and OF COURSE … Gallippoli, one of the most disastrous campaigns of the Conflict. 
The Dardanelles campaign was fought on Turkish soil, as the ANZAC tried to create a passage through to the Black Sea. Gallipoli was the brainchild of Winston Churchill, and was a bitter failure. After massive hardship, ANZAC soldiers were withdrawn, having failed in most cases to even get off the beaches. Russia also invaded Turkish territory, in the north. Following the collapse of the Tsar’s government, the Turks swept north and entered Russian territory proper. By the Russian surrender in the Spring of 1918, the Turkish forces had taken Baku on the Caspian Sea.

Ownership ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     The Bergmann Offensive - Caucasus Front, November 7th 1914
     Stand At Pine Ridge - Pine Ridge, Gallipoli Peninsula, April 25th 1915
     Plugge's Point - Z Beach, Geba Tabe, Gallipoli Peninsula, April 25th 1915
     Valley Of Death - Kereves Dere, Gallipoli Peninsula, June 21st 1915
     Forgotten Arabs - Gallipoli Peninsula, August 6th 1915
     Achi Baba Nulla - Cape Helles, Gallipoli Peninsula, June 4th 1915
     Fir Tree Spur - Cape Helles, Gallipoli Peninsula, May 8th 1915
     Australian Achilles - Gallipoli Peninsula, May 19th 1915

Players take turns rolling a dice and placing paper trails in the form of white counters on the spaces allocated on the game board. After all 48 tiles have been laid, players then take turns moving their pawns and collecting the counters either using forward movement or a combination of forward and backward movement, the sum of which must equal to the dice roll. When all players have completed the circuit, the game ends and the winner is the player with the most counters collected.

Players race through a track peppered with skull and crossbones spaces and treasure chest spaces. If a player lands on a skull and crossbones space, he/she takes a skull and crossbones card and go back X - Y spaces. If a player lands on a treasure chest space, he/she takes a treasure chest card and move forward X + Y spaces. The values of X and Y are given on each card.

boardgame: Land Rush: A Game of Of-Fence and De-Fence
description:
Land Rush is played with Chess pieces on a Chess board, and for the most part the pieces have their normal Chess moves. (Pawn movement is slightly different.) However, other than kings pieces cannot capture, and the goal of the game is to build a connected chain of your pieces between the left and right edges of the board which fences off more than 32 squares.

The board starts empty. On a turn a player may create a piece or move an existing piece. Pieces are created on the owner's first rank and have point values: in order to create a piece there must be at least as many pieces in its file as its point value. (Pawns have a value of 0 so can be created at the start of the game.)

In Wizwords! one or more players gradually build up a crossword grid as the game progresses. (The box says "1+" but six seems like a reasonable maximum number of players.)

On a turn a player draws a letter card and adds a word containing that letter to the grid, connected it to existing words as in Scrabble.

The grid starts with no black squares; after the first turn a player's turn starts by covering letters in previous words with black tiles to leave shorter words, or incomplete words which they complete on their turn.

In Zypher players race to the end of a track by unscrambling anagrams in 30 seconds.

A player may choose to unscramble a five-letter anagram for a small benefit (e.g. advancing one's own piece or moving an opponent's back), or a six-letter anagram for a larger benefit (e.g. both moving your own piece forwards and another back).

Strings (one of two games in a article titled Suit Up!) is a no-luck card game.

Each player gets the 13 cards of a suit from a standard deck of cards, and takes turn playing one card at a time to a set of "strings" on the table. A "string" is a row of cards in ascending sequence with no gaps, e.g. a 4 must be followed by a 5, then a 6, etc.

A string can be started with any card except an ace, 10, or a court card; the latter are wild.

There is a limit of seven strings with three players and nine with four, which may make it impossible for a player to play later in the game, in which case they discard a card.

When all cards are gone players assess each string to see who wins it: 2-10 count their face values, aces count 11, and court cards count 5. The winner takes the string; if there is a tie the cards are split evenly. The player winning the most cards wins the game.

Several variants are provided.
Variant of Checkers with four players

Bidwise (one of two games in a article titled Suit Up!) is an auction game played using standard playing cards.

Each player gets the 13 cards in one suit. The property cards to be bid on are the ace-10 of the remaining suit/another deck, and are placed face-up on the table.

Each player shuffles their cards and each turn draws one and plays it. Cards are generally placed under property cards as bids of their face value; court cards can be bids worth five or can be used to remove an opposing bid card.

When all cards have been played the player bidding the most for each property wins it. The player with the greatest total value of properties wins the game.

A variant is given in which the bid cards can only be used on properties of matching value or one different.

Contains a cloth bag with 44 wooden token upgrades and 30 additional champion cards to add to the market with new abilities which help you push your luck or hinder your opponents in the quest to claim the Valiant Throne.

Züge XXL 2 is an extension to connect the exit of Zooloretto Exotic with the Aquaretto adventure train. With this, after walking through the zoo, visitors will ride the adventure train through the Aquaretto water park.

Needed are:
Zooloretto
Zooloretto Exotic
The Aquaretto adventure train and if necessary
Aquaretto

—description from the designer (translated)

Es ist eine Erweiterung um den Ausgang von Zooloretto Exotic mit der Erlebnisbahn von Aquaretto zu verbinden. Damit fahren die Besucher nach dem Gang durch den Zoo mit der Erlebnisbahn durch den Aquaretto Wasser-Park.

Benötigt werden:
Zooloretto
Zooloretto Exotic
Die Erlebnisbahn von Aquaretto und ggf.
Aquaretto

—description from the designer

boardgame: Divide & Conquer: A Game of Galactic Isolation
description:
Divide & Conquer is played on a small hexagonal grid with seven pieces for each player.

On a turn a player can move one of their ships to an adjacent empty space, as long as the ship is adjacent to at least one friendly ship.

Instead, a player can move an opposing ship which is adjacent to one of their ships (which is in turn adjacent to another friendly ship). They must move the opposing ship to a space where it isn't adjacent to any of its own ships, or to one of the black holes at the edges of the board, where it is lost forever.

The object of the game is to isolate or remove all the opposing ships.

If the players agree the game is stalemated a set of cards numbered 1-10 is used to remove a ship from each player.

Hex-A-Gone is a solitaire puzzle game played on a hexagonal grid in a rhombus shape, five on a side.

There are 16 pieces: four each numbered 1-4. A move consists of jumping a piece over exactly the matching number of adjacent pieces. One of the jumped pieces is removed. The goal is to leave as few pieces as possible.

A starting position is given but others can be used.

In Robot Tricks two-player teams each control a robot moving on a checkerboard, attempting to retrieve treasure chests.

The robots are controlled via card play, specifically by playing Spades. However, no bidding takes place and the number of tricks won is irrelevant. Instead, the winner of a trick chooses one of the four cards in it and moves their team's robot accordingly: the suit determines the direction and the rank determines how many spaces it moves, according to a table. Court cards allow the robot to be rotated.

However, if a trick contains an ace the winner chooses one of the other cards and moves the opposing team's robot instead.

When a robot moves to one of its treasures exactly it retrieves it. Opposing treasures and robots may be pushed.

The game can be set up with two treasures per team for a short game or four each for a long game.

Khedu The Card Game is a fast paced 1-4 player fantasy card game where players compete against each other to build combat configurations with cards and dice. The objective is to earn as many points as you can before taking your last turn. Players spend energy to deploy heroes and spells -and earn points by building rewarding combat configurations.

The game ends when the last player has spent all their Energy tokens and completes their last turn. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins

-description from publisher

boardgame: Death In The Dark Continent: Lite – Wargames in 19th Century Africa
description:
Death in the Dark Continent Lite

A complete set of rules for playing small battle wargames in 19th Century Africa.

This ‘cut down’ version of the full DitDC game provides all the rules you need; 
introducing new players to this exciting system and colourful period, and acting as a handy quick reference guide for existing players.

Published in April 2021 issue of Wargames Illustrated #400

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Mercato: Dominate the World's Market is a dynamic game in which players acquire properties to gain control over key industries while navigating external events like wars, global warming, disruptive innovation, and pandemics.

Enjoy trading and attempting hostile takeovers on your quest for market dominance.

In Mercato: Dominate the World's Market, players engage in intense competition, strategically buying properties to concentrate ownership on global industries. The game's dynamic nature allows any player to build on their strategy, aiming to become the dominant player in the market and potentially bankrupt the rest of the players.

boardgame: Phoenix Command: Lock and Load Vietnam: 1965 - 1971 Supplement
description:
Clearly one of the defining military conflicts of our time, the Vietnam War pitted the military might of the USA against the willpower of the Vietnamese Communists. Political and military leaders on both sides struggled to dictate the direction of a new kind of war, a guerilla war on an immense scale. The lessons learned in Vietnam have been applied to countless battlefields.

It was the soldier in the field who provided the lessons, by  learning them the hard way. This Scenario Pack allows players to re-create the Vietnam War as it was fought by the infantrymen of the forces involved: US Marines, US Army, South Vietnamese ARVN, ARVN Rangers, Regional Force/Popular Force, NVA Regulars, NVA Sappers and Viet Cong Guerillas.

Lock and Load includes seven scenarios drawn from the war: Jungle Patrol, Search and Destroy, Night Ambush, Street Fight, Fire Base defense, Rescue Operation, and Flank Security.

Each Scenario includes a pull page map designed for easy enlargement  to any s8ize. Equipment Options allow the scenarios to be customized with Punji Stakes, Trip Flares, Barbed Wire, Bangalore Torpedoes, Booby-Trapped Grenades, and more. Pre-Generated Troop data provides complete Combat and Weapon Data for 59 types of Combatants with 13 Weapons, allowing players to quickly set-up and play any scenario. Included are m1/M2 Carbine, M3 "Grease Gun", M1 Garand, BAR, M-14 and SKS Rifles, M-16 and AK-47 Assault Rifles, M-60 and RPD Machine Guns and RPG-2, RPG-7 and M-79 Grenade launchers.

The Revolution in Game Design Continues!
- from the back of the book

The land is in peril! Two brave heroes, along with their trusty companion, must take on enemies of progressing difficulty, along with a challenging final boss, using only their wits and their abilities. Each player rolls 3 dice for their character, using push-your-luck mechanics to try to activate their most powerful abilities, or settle for whatever damage they can get, depending on their strategy.

Roll Crawl is a 1 to 2 player print and play game, with two heroes trying to activate their own abilities to reduce the health of their opponents to zero. They can forego activating their own abilities and combine their dice to active the abilities of their companion. But whatever you do, deal as much damage as you can. Your opponents are ready to hit back, with more dice than you. The more health they have, the more dice they roll, so players must be strategic about deciding when it is the right time to push their luck.

—description from the designer

Chemline is a card game consisting of 104 cards, corresponding to the first chemical elements.

Goal: create lines by placing cards in the order of, either the element's atomic number or, its year of discovery. 
In doing so, players become familiar with atomic elements and can discover different information about each element. 
The etymology of each name, the main uses of the elements, their molar masses and their electronegativity can all be found.

This is a free game (in French) available from their creators, the ENSCMu – Ecole Nationale Supérieure de Chimie de Mulhouse
Great Tank Battle of Kursk is a game of tactical combat between German tanks and Soviet tanks during the Battle of Kursk, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #37.

Solitaire game of the Battle of Leyte Gulf, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #46.
The player plays the role of Imperial Japanese Navy commander, organizes four fleets (Ozawa, Shima, Kurita and Nishimura) and intercepts U.S. Navy.

Solitaire game of the Battle of Midway, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #49.
The player plays the role of Imperial Japanese Navy commander and runs after two hares — aims at bombing both Midway Island and U.S. aircraft carriers.

Solitaire game of the Battle of Britain in 1940, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #54.
The player plays the role of Luftwaffe commander and aims at bombing London and other strategic targets.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL VIII – Gallipoli and Beyond: ANZAC WW1
description:
When war broke out in August of 1914, the news was met by wildly cheering crowds in Australia. 
Following some months of mobilization, the AIF exited the country in November 1914 and, after several delays due to the presence of German naval vessels in the Indian Ocean, arrived in Egypt. Their first task was to defend the Suez Canal. In early 1915, however, it was decided to carry out an amphibious landing on the Gallipoli Peninsula with the goal of opening up a second front and securing the passage of the Dardanelles. The Australians and New Zealanders, grouped together as the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC), went ashore on 25 April 1915 and for the next eight months the Anzacs, alongside their British, French and other allies, fought a costly and ultimately unsuccessful campaign against the Turks. The force was evacuated from the peninsula in December 1915 and returned to Egypt, where the AIF was expanded. In early 1916 it was decided that the infantry divisions would be sent to France, where they took part in many of the major battles fought on the Western Front. Most of the light horse units remained in the Middle East until the end of the war, carrying out further operations against the Turks in Egypt and Palestine.

Ownership ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     Driven Out - Mont St. Quentin, France, July 23rd 1916
     The O.G. Lines - Pozieres, France, July 29th 1916 
     First Dash - Pozieres, France, July 23rd 1916
     Pozieres Heights - Pozieres, France, July 29th 1916
     First Puncture - Bapaume, France, May 2nd 1917
     Determination and Valour - Amiens, France, April 4th 1918
     The Sugarloaf - Fromelles, France, July 20th 1916 
     Bayonet To Bayonet - Broodseinde, Belgium, October 3rd 1917

boardgame: Third Battle of the Solomon Sea
description:
Solitaire game of the Third Battle of the Solomon Sea, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #57.
The player plays the role of Imperial Japanese Navy commander, organizes fleets and aims at transporting Imperial Japanese Army reinforcement to Guadalcanal Island.

An abstract chess-like game based on the Punic Wars. The playing pieces are casted in resin (deluxe edition) or wooden blocks (normal edition), colored red (Rome) or blue (Carthage). Both red pieces and blue pieces are subdivided into one commander, ten infantry and four cavalry. Each player aims to making a friendly piece move to the opponent's deepest square or removing the opponent's commander.

Have you ever thrown your bones like dice? You could try it in Scatterbones.
Scatterbones is a bone throwing and die rolling fun game for two players. Each player tries to assemble their skeleton while preventing the opponent from doing the same.

Game play:

     Each player throws a tombstone to determine which colour their skeleton must have - black or red. Both players can play for the same colour.
     Player 1 rolls their 6 skeleton parts (the bones). If a ribcage in the correct colour can be found all other parts in the same colour can be connected to it.
     Player 2 does the same with his bones. Both players must start with a ribcage, otherwise they have to rattle all the bones again in their next turn.
     Each play picks up the left bones and the die and rolls them together. If a missing part is shown on the die, it can be added to the skeleton. But there is also the colour changing side and the scatter side. Both mean the player has to start again. If the opponent already has the wanted part, the active play may choose to steal it from the opponent.
     The first player to finish their skeleton wins the game. So the game can be over in 2 minutes if luck is on your side.

In 2019, a set of promotional cards called "Dixit: Chilean Way" was released by Asmodee Chile and was a great success among the fans of this title. Now, in the midst of the pandemic, they have repeated the work, creating a new and amazing set of 10 promo cards. After an open call, which brought together the most varied artists from all over Chile, a team of five emerging illustrators was chosen, who were selected in France by Libellud.

For the purchase of a normal Dixit or Dixit Odyssey, Chilean can get the "Dixit Chilean way" pack containing 10 cards with exclusive illustrations by Chilean artists as a gift!

NOTE:  As long as you keep (only) my copyright statement, its Dedication, and my war&history quotes on it, you are - ANYone is - free to print off copies of my English language edition in postcard 4"x6"/A6 format to send to family and friends or whomever.

VERY fast and simple hex-and-counter Postcard Barbarossa! postcard size/format board wargame about the 1941 Nazi German invasion of Russia. 4 bi-monthly turns. 3 !! hexes Warsaw to Moscow. Plays in 30? minutes.

3 German Army Group, 4 Panzer Group, 2 Army, and 1 Panzer Korps pieces, plus a Romanians Army Group piece.

4 Russian operational Tank Groups - Rokossovsky, Lelyushenko, Katukov, Vatutin - 8 Fronts, and 1 Siberians pieces.

Simple, basic mechanics, but with lots of basic combat options.

Unlike Barbarossa Brief from which it was adapted, Postcard Barbarossa! uses a simple factor differential/comparison combat system which can be (and usually is) made nightmarishly unpredictable by a Special Factor Pressed Attack or Desperate Defense rule.  This makes it an excellent solitaire game.

China's Banner of War will soon be publishing the game in larger, boxed format with already mounted pieces. The postcard is also being translated into Russian in Volgograd/Stalingrad!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Crommelin Comet (27P) reaches its closest distance to Earth ‒ its periapsis, every 27 years. Scientists have recently discovered a mistake in its orbital calculations and predict that it will collide with Earth in the year 2038!

There is only one way to prevent this catastrophic event: destroy the comet. A secret experimental rocket station built in 1929, when rocket technology was still in its infancy, is our only hope. In order to complete this mission, a rocket full of explosive cargo must be launched at the comet each time it approaches Earth, weakening and ultimately destroying the menace over the course of four periapsis events.

Four brave scientists will simultaneously time travel between the last four years of periapsis ‒ 1930, 1957, 1984, and 2011, to attempt to destroy the deadly comet. They must ensure the explosive cargo reaches the Rocket Station in time. The only consistent form of travel across these years is rail, so they must build an iron path for the cargo to reach its destination.

If they can build a track for all four trains to reach the Rocket Station, while repairing damaged rails and navigating obstacles, they can save the planet! But they have to be careful: their choices in the past affect the future, and if any one of the trains falls off the track... the Earth is DOOMED.

In 27Panic you play cooperatively as a team of scientists building a railroad track in four years simultaneously. You build tracks by playing cards and ones that are built in the past carry forward into the future. You must plan closely with your team in order to strategically build a path for explosive cargo to follow in each year. If you are able to get the cargo to a nearby rocket station in all of the years by avoiding obstacles and repairing damaged tracks, you'll win the game!

—description from the designer

The objective of the game is to guess the exact combination of triangles, squares, and circles and their exact positions. The first player creates a combination of four shapes using triangles, squares, or circles without the second player looking and covers it. The second player then tries to guess the correct combination. After every guessing attempt, the first player marks each square grid that has the correct shape with a circle, and each square grid that has the correct shape and position with a square. If the second player is unable to guess correctly after eight attempts, the first player wins.

Equipment: 1 game board, 1 privacy screen, 34 discs, 34 squares, 19 triangles.

The first expansion for On the Rivet: The Race for the Champion's Stripes, which adds cards and tokens to include a 6th and 7th player as well as Tactic cards which gives players unique abilities to play throughout the game.

Also included are more map tiles for greater course variability and two new event cards.

Components:
- Additional Orange and Purple team, thus playing between 3-7 people.
- 14 Tactics cards that help players avoid events or make super moves.
- 2 additional Event cards.
- 2 additional double-sided map tiles for further distance and terrain variability.

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL IX – Bulgarian Summer: Bulgarian WW1
description:
Following Bulgaria’s entry into the war on the side of the Central Powers on October 14th, 1915, the death knell for Serbia and Romania began to sound. 
The Bulgarian Summer of 1915 saw the Entente attempt to win the hearts and support of Bulgaria. It was all for naught, as the country entered the war aligned with Germany and her allies. And mobilization soon began, followed by a declaration of war against Serbia. Around this time the Germans and Austro-Hungarians had penetrated into Serbia on a front that was 140 kilometers in length and 15 kilometers in depth. In order to close the 90-kilometer gap between the flanks of the German 11th Army and the Bulgarian 1st Army Mackensen ordered the latter to invade the valley of the river Morava and take Niš and Aleksinac. In accordance with this order the Bulgarians attacked along the entire front of their 1st Army, quickly driving out the Serbian units and taking control of the border area. Following this easy success however the speed of the advance was much reduced due to the bad weather, which turned roads into mud, and a dense fog that sometimes limited visibility to 50 meters. In addition the stiffening Serbian resistance and the mountainous character of the area caused the flanks of the 1st Army to halt before the fortresses of Pirot and Zajecar. The exploits of the Bulgarian 2nd Army in Macedonia convinced the Serbians that the danger of complete encirclement is high and forced them to begin withdrawing their forces to Kosovo while offering stiff but not desperate resistance to Army Group Mackensen. In November, while the decisive Serbian defeat unfolded, the French attempted to exert pressure on the Bulgarian 2nd Army but were soon forced to halt their attempts to drive north.

Ownership of ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     Pristina Halt - Pristina, Serbia, November 24th 1915
     Fall Back To Lerin - Gornichevo, Salonika Front, September 14th 1916
     The Dobrich Epopee (Epic) - Southern Dobruja, Romania, September 5th 1916
     A Good Field - Dobro Pole, Serbia, September 15th 1918
     Chegan Operation - Banitsa, Serbia, August 17 1916
     Contested Prophet - Sabac, Serbia, September 30th 1916
     Rakija Courage - Gnjilane, Serbia, December 2nd 1915
     Show Of Force - Serbia, August 18 1916
The players start at space 1. The first to reach space 80 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
"Le Beau Soleil d'Austerlitz" is a two player Grand-Tactical board-wargame depicting the Battle of Austerlitz fought in December 1805 between Napoleon and the Allied forces of Russia and Austria. Austerlitz is regarded as one of the classic Napoleonic battles and involves thousands of troops.

The battle was characterized by the early morning mist which concealed much of the dispositions and forces present.

The game system is innovative and requires players to devise a plan for the battle and accept restricted command. The battlefield is divided into areas rather than hexagons.

Units (counters) are divisions while command is command is activated at Corps level.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Beyond Tic Tac Ten is played on a 9x9 grid divided into nine 3x3 grids, as is the fake "Tic Tac Ten" game that inspired it. (This appeared in one of the fake ads in an earlier issue of GAMES.)

Players take turns marking an empty space. After the first move, each move must be made in the 3x3 corresponding to the position of the previous move in its 3x3 grid. E.g. if the first move is made in the upper right corner of its 3x3 grid, the second move must be made in the 3x3 grid in the upper right corner of the "board."

The first player to get three spaces in a line (a tic-tac-toe) in a single 3x3 grid wins. However, several variants are suggested, including one in which players try to get tic-tac-toes in three grids in a line, and both players can get a tic-tac-toe in the same grid

Quoz is played with a deck of eight suits each numbered 0-4, plus 12 numbered "Draw" cards and 12 "Quoz" cards.

On a turn a player plays two cards with matching number or suit, optionally accompanied by a Draw card. The next player to play is determined by the rank of the cards played (higher if different) - count that many players around the table. If it ends on the current player they choose who plays next.

Instead of playing a pair, a player may draw a card or play a Quoz card, which forces the previous player to also play a Quoz card. If they can't they collect all played cards and also draw cards as indicated by any Draw cards played. (Players may also have to collect cards if they can't play a pair under some circumstances.)

When a player is out of cards everyone else scores the value of their remaining cards. Low score when someone reaches 200 points wins.

At the beginning of the game, each player places 10 coins on space 6. The players roll two dice and places 5 coins on the space with the rolled number if the space is empty. If not, he wins the 5 coins from this space. On the roll of a 6, five coins are payed every time. If a 12 is rolled, the player wins the game and all coins on the board.
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
Escapade is played on a oblong hexagonal grid board using 13 pieces for each player, one of which is marked as a General. The other pieces are Guards.

The General and six Guards for each player start on the board, with the other six Guards as off-board reserves. On a turn a player may place a Guard from their reserve on any empty space, or they may move a piece on the board any number of empty spaces in a straight line.

A Guard is captured if an enemy piece is moved so that is directly between two enemy pieces. A General is captured if enemy pieces occupy all its adjacent spaces.

The object of the game is to move one's General to the goal area on the opposite edge of the board. One can also win by capturing the opposing General or lose if all the spaces in your target goal area become occupied.

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL X – Slavic Warriors: Serbian WW1
description:
Serbia was blamed for the start of World War I following the assassination of Archduke Franz-Ferdinand in Sarajevo. Yet the Serbs would enjoy the first Allied victory of the war, after they drove the Austro-Hungarians out of the town of Sabac on 19 August 1914. Before they could, however, civilians were rounded up in the town and massacred. On the 5 October 1914, Austria launched their offensive against Serbia. The Serbian Army, weakened by Typhus, couldn’t hold out and the capital, Belgrade, was evacuated on the 9th. Two days later, Bulgaria declared war on Serbia, hoping to annex the Serb possession of Macedonia. By the 24th, the Bulgarians had driven a wedge between the Serb army and the Allied armies in Greece attempting to come to their aid. The Serbs fought with courage, but found their army critically low on ammunition stocks. With defeat staring them in the face, over 200,000 soldiers and civilians fled over the rugged mountains and into Albania. Many were evacuated by sea to the island of Corfu, in what would stand as the world’s largest evacuation by sea until the ‘miracle at Dunkirk’ in 1940. Surviving Serbian soldiers made their way to Greece, and remained in uniform to serve in the Yugoslav Division of Serbs, Croats, Macedonians, Montenegrins and Slovenes. When Bulgaria surrendered on 30 September 1918, Serb, Yugoslav, French and British forces united to drive the Austrians and Germans out of Serbia.

Ownership of ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     Delay Before The Drina - Podrinje, Serbia, August 12th 1914
     Kosaningrad - Tekeris, Serbia, August 15th 1914
     Mounted Clash - Tekeris, Serbia, August 16th 1914
     In The Valley Of Jadar - Sabac, Serbia, August 16th 1914
     Back Across The Drina - Cer, Serbia, August 17th 1914
     The Liberation Of Sabac - Sabac, Serbia, August 24th 1914
     The Youngest Corporal - Cer, Serbia, August 14th 1914
     The Battle of Kolubara - Serbia, November 18th 1914

Spy is played on a 11x11 grid with 20 spaces designated as cities. There are five tiles in each of four colors, numbered 1-5, which are randomly placed on the cities at the start of the game.

Each player has four spies: one large, one medium, and two small. On a turn a player either places a spy on an empty edge space or move a spy any number of empty spaces orthogonally. A spy can end its move on a space with a smaller enemy spy, which is placed one space away in the direction the moving spy came from.

When four of the black tiles spaces are occupied those tiles are taken. The blue tiles are next, etc. The fifth tile in a color can be taken by any spy landing on its space.

When there is just one tile left the game ends. Players score the values of their tiles, plus a ten-point bonus for collecting three or four of the same number.

Mating Season is played with a set of 36 insect cards/tiles. Each card is a unique combination of one of six colors, one of three wing patterns, and having or not having antennae. The cards are double-sided, with a male on one side and a female on the other.

In the two-player game players start with five-card "hands" (the cards are actually face-up on the table) and a single card to start a common insect family tree. Players take turns playing one or more cards to the tree; before playing a player may draw up to five more cards from the deck, choosing their genders.

Two insects will only mate if they are of different genders, of complementary colors, have different wing patterns, and have opposite antenna status. Their offspring (up to two) will have the wing pattern different from either parent's and any of the other four colors.

There can only be five generations in play; if a sixth is started the top one is shuffled and placed at the bottom of the deck.

The first player to empty their hand wins.

In the solitaire game an insect is set aside at the start and the goal is to evolve a mate for it within five generations.

Bayamigo is a game about buying and reselling fruit and vegetables in local markets. The goal is to achieve the greatest profit of all players, surviving unexpected obstacles in transport and price levels while facing fierce competition from other players.
Bayamigo is played over three days (different lengths can be agreed on). At the beginning of each day, players buy goods in bulk, and rent motorcycles or vans for the transport. Then they begin a journey through different markets. At each market, players can announce that they want to sell goods there. All players who want to sell, pay the required tax, then dice are rolled to determine the selling price. Each market has a maximum capacity, so turn order is important. Players who are not happy with the price can move on, but won’t get their taxes back. At the end of the day, vehicles have to be returned, and interest has to be paid on any debts the players may have. Obstacle cards can get in your way, reflecting the risks that small-scale resellers face in their trade.
The player who has made the greatest profit during the three days, wins the game.

Treasure Hunters is played on a board of seven spaces arranged in a ring. The players move around this - one clockwise and one counterclockwise - picking up four different values of treasure.

Movement is done by playing cards - one player has all the black cards from a standard deck and the other has the red, with random hands drawn from each. Most cards move the player around the board, but court cards have special effects.

When a player lands on a space they take the least-valuable treasure there, generally speaking. At the end of a turn a player may turn over one treasure on the board; treasures that have been turned over are worth more.

When all the treasures have been taken or all cards have been played the player with the most points in treasures wins.

boardgame: ASL Comp: GWASL XI – Balkan Aspirations: Romanian WW1
description:
Romania entered the war in August of 1916, on the Allied side, in an attempt to seize the prize: Transylvania. 
This region in Austria-Hungary had a Romanian ethnic majority but was under Hungarian control at the time. 
Despite initial successes, the combined forces of Russia and Romania suffered several defeats, and by the end of 1916 only Moldavia remained under Allied control. After several defensive victories in 1917, with different groups competing for authority over the Russian troops in Romania in the aftermath of the October Revolution, Romania signed an armistice at Focani. On November 10, 1918, just one day before the German armistice and when all the other Central Powers had already capitulated, Romania re-entered the war. By then, about 220,000 Romanian soldiers had been killed. Despite their small size, the Germans worried about the prospect of Romania entering the war, Hindenburg writing: “It is certain that so relatively small a state as Romania had never before been given a role so important, and, indeed, so decisive for the history of the world at so favorable a moment.” Romanian soldiers would fight in battles that would be compared with some of the most brutal campaigns on the Western Front at little-known places like the Bran-Câmpulung area, the Carpathains, and the Prahova Valley.

Ownership of ASL-RB, GWASL 1 and all preceding GWASL modules required to play.

Scenarios:

     Across The Argesch - Argeș River, Romania, December 3rd 1916
     Stand At Brasso - Brassó, Transylvania, October 8th 1916
     Sweep Of Cinghinarele Island - Cinghinarele Island, Bulgaria, October 8th 1916
      Erich and August - Silistra, Bulgaria, September 4th 1916
     Turkey Shooters - Amuzaca, Romania, September 24th 1916
