AbsoluteZero is a 2 player card game from Japan.

Summon monsters and use magic and skills to defeat your opponent! This is a TCG-type card game using a common deck of cards!

You can summon higher-level monsters by sacrificing (discarding) monsters, including those of your opponent! Summon one monster per turn and you only have life of 10 HP! There's a lot of counter-magic, so your tactics are the key!

モンスター召喚と魔法・スキルを使って相手を倒せ！山札共通型TCG風カードゲーム！
相手モンスター含め生贄（召喚時に対象捨て札）に捧げられる上位モンスター召喚がある！
召喚は1ターン1匹、ライフはたった10！対抗魔法が多めなので駆け引きが肝！
publisher's summary

Pilo is a finite territory game for two players: Blue and Orange. It is played on the spaces (squares) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board sizes are between 6x6 and 10x10 squares.

Both players must have access to a sufficient number of disks of their own color and a sufficient number of neutral blocks.

				
				
					DefinitionsA stack is a disk lying directly on the board along with all other disks piled onto it on the same square.

The height of a stack is the number of disks in it.

A group is a stack along with all other like-colored stacks one could reach from it through a series of steps between orthogonally adjacent stacks of its color.

Similarly, a patch is a square not occupied by a block along with all other such squares one could reach from it through a series of steps between orthogonally adjacent squares not occupied by blocks. Note a patch may contain any number of groups, as well as empty squares.

The size of a group or patch is the number of squares it spans.

				
				
					PlayBlue plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, if possible, make exactly one of these board plays:

- Place a disk of your color on an empty square.

- Move a stack of your color any number of squares in a straight orthogonal line, without jumping over any blocks or enemy stacks, to a square occupied by an enemy stack of the same height. Then, remove this enemy stack, add a disk to your stack on that square and place a block on the square from which you just moved your stack. Note a stack can jump over friendly stacks.

If you have no board plays available on your turn, you must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed.

The game ends when both players pass in succession. Then, for each patch on the board, the players jointly remove pairs of opposite-colored groups of the same size from it until there are no more such pairs to remove. The player with the biggest group left over in a patch owns the patch. Empty patches are owned by neither player.

The player with the higher score in the resulting position wins. Your score is the sum of the sizes of the patches that you own. If there is a tie, whoever made the last board play loses.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means Orange will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

Quaxtep is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (squares) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. The board squares alternate between a light and a dark color in a checkered fashion. On odd-sized boards, the center square must be light-colored.

Black starts the game by performing one action. From then on, the players alternate performing two actions per turn. The available actions are:


     Placing a friendly stone on an empty light square.
     Moving a friendly stone from a light square to an adjacent, empty dark square.


You can only pass your turn, or the second action of a turn, if you have no legal actions available.

You win if there is a chain of connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color. Two like-colored stones are connected if they occupy orthogonally adjacent squares or diagonally adjacent dark squares.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mythos Encounters: A Cooperative Horror Miniature Skirmish Game
description:
Cooperative Miniature Encounters in the Cthulhu Mythos.

Mythos Encounters is a miniature skirmish game of the Cthulhu Mythos. This is a cooperative game, in which Investigators work together to face the horrors of ruthless cultists, mysterious alien creatures and the elder gods themselves.

Your investigators can face enemies head-on, trusting in your strength and weaponry, or take a more subtle approach using stealth and gathering clues to gain an advantage.

Enemy creatures in Mythos Encounters change from encounter to encounter, and their behavior is determined by a variety of AI tactics.

As your investigators progress through each encounter, they will gain new abilities, equipment and allies that will allow them to face even greater threats. They may also gain permanent physical injuries or debilitating mental disorders.

Mythos Encounters is played as a series of linked encounters using your own terrain and miniature figures. This game has no official terrain or miniatures.

—description from the publisher

Rediscover the power of your imagination as you take on the role  of a storyteller. Use the Bimba Cards to interpret phrases in your own unique way. Then convince your fellow players that your interpretation is the most meaningful. Score points for every vote you get and every vote you can sway in your favour. You win if you have the highest number of points at the end of the game.

Game Play..

Each player is dealt with 5 cards and as game begins, One player picks a phrase card and reads it out to the other players,

Each other player selects the card in their hands that best matches the phrase and gives the selected card to the dealer, without showing it to the others.

The dealer shuffles the cards with all the received cards. All pictures are shown face up and every player votes for the cards that best suits the phrase. They cannot vote for their own card. Then one of the player challenges the votes and tries to convince the others about why another card is better suited and pitch for it. Players can tell stories, bring out instances to convince the other players to change their votes. The challenger scores one pink token for every vote they turn in their favour.

Players score points for every vote for their own card and collects the tokens.

Apart from the phrase cards, players can opt to create their own Stories, Verse, Phrase or a word. If they win this round, they get an additional 20 points apart from the votes. 
The game ends when the deck is empty or when the players decide to end the game.

The base game and all expansions have 84 main Bimba cards each with phrase cards, Vote cards, 1 play mat, 84 winning tokens and 1 Rule book.

The objective of The Fort is to knock down men consisting of a King, a Queen, their Bishops, and Guards. Players use a cue to push balls into a U-shaped fort with the men placed in predefined positions.

boardgame: Call to Adventure: Name of the Wind – Worldbuilders Exclusive: Secret Friend & Hero of Destiny (alt card)
description:
Alternate artwork for previously released Hero of Destiny card featuring Kvothe.

All new promo card Secret Friend featuring Auri.

Worldbuilders-exclusive, originally available in return for donation through the 2019 Worldbuilders fundraiser.

Cards bundled together, not available separately.

Attacktica is a two player abstract strategy game with dice to show you how to move.
Each player has 4 ships with 20 life points with maximum 6 life points per ship.
There are 2 sea monsters which are used from both sides.


     Life points are divided up to each ship, the life points are not visible from the opponent.
     Two dice per player, dice one; is for which ship or sea monster is to move and dice two; is for the amount of moves or life points to be taken, depending if it is stage one or stage two of the game.
     Stage One; One ship must reach the opponents start port to declare war.
     Stage Two, when two opposite ships are aligned, battle can commence.
     The two sea monsters play a big role in the game, they can obstruct ships, they can give moves and  they can give and take life points, depending on the dice.
     The winner is the player with the last ship/ships in the play arena.


—description from the designer

At the beginning each player has 5 action cards, 5 coronas & 5 nerve cards in hand. Try to keep your nerve in this card game. You only have five of them at the beginning. In return, you should do everything you can to get rid of the coronas. Whoever got rid of all of their coronas first wins the game.

There are 60 action cards in the game. One action card is played per round. If you demonstrate strategic skill, you win the card game at the end.

As soon as a player falls below 0 nerve cards, he is eliminated. So you always have to have at least one of these nerve cards in hand.

When it is your turn, you have the option of using your action cards to have an effect on all players or only on a certain player - you can assign them even more coronas or take away nerves. After you have played an action card, you draw a new one. You continue to play until the stack of cards is used up or a player has already got rid of all his coronas or all others have lost their nerve.

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Bei CORONA NERVT! starten alle MitspielerInnen mit 5 Nerv-, 5 Corona- und 5 Spielkarten. Die Aufgabe ist es nun Coronas loszuwerden, dabei die Nerven beisammenzuhalten und dies mit den eigenen Spielkarten geschickt zu steuern.

Wenn mich beispielsweise meine MitspielerInnen besuchen, setzen wir uns alle einem bewussten Risiko aus und erhalten daher alle eine Coronakarte und müssen eine Nervkarte abgeben. Nur nicht die Nerven verlieren und Corona wieder loswerden. Das ist die Strategie!

Es ist doch echt frustrierend, da hat schon wieder jemand beim Niesen nicht den Ellenbogen davorgehalten und im Bus trägt der Sitznachbar die Maske unter der Nase! Zack wieder drei Coronas gesammelt und drei Nerven verloren. Aber da! Ein Lichtblick! Impfstofflieferung in Sicht und endlich Sommer!

Ein schnelles Spiel in dem man nicht nur die eigenen Karten im Blick behalten muss, sondern auch die der MitspielerInnen. Kann ich mit der nächsten Karte eine/n MitspielerIn rausschmeißen? Wer ist fast geheilt und braucht dringend mal wieder eine Schippe Corona? Werde deine Coronas los, behalte die Nerven und bring die anderen zur Verzweiflung.

Dieses spannende Spiel, versüß die Zeit nicht nur im Lockdown und lässt uns über Corona und seine Auswirkungen auch mal schmunzeln!

Wer gute Nerven beweist und alle eigenen Coronas besiegt, gewinnt das Spiel. Wir sagen DANKE Jana!!

boardgame: Forge of Nations: The Strategy Card Game
description:
A history-themed card drafting and city building game commemorating two hundred years of Singapore's history.

Player starts with a jungle, and your job is to grow it to a village, town and towards a city.

This can be done through growing the four developmental pillars of your settlement: Population, Economy, Governance, Infrastructure. During your turn, you can a) build your settlement, b) trade with fellow players or c) cause crisis situations to them. Grow your settlement while watching out for your rivals. First player to build the city wins.

Depending on the specific pillars you draft and build, grow your city to either a hippie tourism spot, economic bustling city with governance, or offshore gambling den... the possibilities of your settlement are endless!

A new arena has just been opened to the public and needs groups of brave participants willing to test their mettle and skill in the arts of combat.
Defeat all your enemies and receive ultimate glory as Heroes of the Land. Should you fail, worry not, you will be remembered as Nearly Heroes.

Nearly Heroes is a cooperative game where you and your friends compete in an arena against exotic enemies. The only thing is that our Heroes aren’t quite heroes. None of them are trained or skilled enough to wield the weapons they come across and suffer greatly for their misuse.
They’ve gotten in way over their heads and it’s up to you to lead them to victory together through strategy and a heavy dose of luck.

Frozen Winds: Seas of Ice is a fast-paced naval miniatures wargame set in a DaVinci inspired ice age.

The planet has frozen over.  
Gale-force winds howl across the ice as you maneuver your ice skiffs to dominate the frigid domain.  
Based on ships of the line style naval games, Seas of Ice plays much, much faster.

A two-player game with half a dozen ships can be played out in about 30 minutes.  
A six-player game with 20 to 30 boats can run for an afternoon on a kitchen table.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Kingly Fight: The Battle of Bouvines, July 27, 1214
description:
“The knights and the auxiliaries, armed and arranged into ordered echelons, prepared in all haste for the battle. The horses’ bridles were tightened by the auxiliaries. The armour shone in the splendor of the sun and it seemed that the light of day was doubled. The banners unfolded in the winds and offered themselves to the currents; they presented a delightful spectacle to the eyes.” –comment by the author of the Relation Marchianensis de Pugna Bovinis on the French army’s preparation for battle.

Following the Third Crusade tensions among the monarchs of Western Europe was at a new high. Otto IV, King of Portugal and newly crowned Holy Roman Emperor was facing revolt in the German provinces, many of whom were actively supported by the French King, Philip II, The Emperor had also garnered the enmity of Pope Innocent III soon after he was crowned when Otto claimed the Kingdom of Sicily, which he then attacked in 1209, which caused the Pope to excommunicate him. English King, John, was also involved with a growing conflict with the French, who were steadily taking lands along the border with Normandy. Indeed, John had only recently assumed the thrown following the death of his older brother, Richard (the Lionhearted) during a siege to retake one of the castles the French had captured earlier while the English king was on crusade. Tensions boiled over to outright war when the Pope authorized King Philip to launch a crusade against the upstart Holy Roman Emperor and his allies. The Battle of Bouvines, fought near the city of Tournai, was one of the largest ever fought in the Medieval period, and its outcome would affect the course of history for centuries.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

—description from the designer

description:
Our debut game, You Decide, is a roll and move game full of choices!  With each move, You Decide which choice gives you the best chance of getting your pawns to Finish, first!  Sometimes your choice involves moving an opponent back!  Really want to make things interesting?  Play the optional "Snake Eye Switch" where you get to switch places with an opponent, and vice versa!

A single You Decide game is for 2 players, but have your guests bring their You Decide game and add up to two more players per game, laying the boards side by side for the ultimate party game!

Will your decisions make YOU the WINNER?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Aus der Zeit des großen Königs
description:
The players start at space 1. The first to reach space 50 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
The objective of the game is to be the first to get his/her whole field cleared of planes. Each player takes turns spinning a spinner and clearing a hangar of his/her plane which number the spinner lands on. The first to clear all six planes win.

—user summary

The players start at space 1. The first to reach space 45 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On some spaces events hinder the players. Te effects differ according to the chosen mode of transportation of each player. The players may use an automobile, a bicycle, a coach, a horse or travel by foot. Also the result of the dice roll is used differently:
Automobile and bike: the result counts double
Coach and horse: the results count normal.
Pedestrian: 1=0, 2=1, 3=1, 4=2, 5=2, 6=3.
The pedestrians may use the 3 shortcuts, if the reach the appropriate spaces.
The faster the mode of transportation, the worse are the effects of the event spaces. There are no ill effects for the pedestrians at all.

—user summary

boardgame: Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game – Peace at Any Cost
description:
The future of Rokugan hangs in the balance with the Temptations cycle for Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game!

As dark forces move throughout Rokugan in Peace at Any Cost, every clan is tempted to dabble in the forbidden arts of mahou, which requires fate to be spent from players' own characters. Hand-in-hand with this comes a new keyword, dire, which triggers when a character has no fate on it. As with previous cycles, every Dynasty Pack of this cycle offers sixty new cards, with new options for each of the seven Great Clans of Rokugan, split between strongholds, provinces, dynasty deck, and conflict deck.
family: Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

description:
Theme
The Art of Boxing Clever simulates almost all aspects of the real-life boxing match into a highly specific and player controlled, intense decision-making match experience. Aspects recreated in the board game include the stance and style of the boxers; boxer initiative, 10 attributes of the selected boxer; head/body area target selection; punch type selection, defence move selection; damage resistance attributes, increasing damage tracking; judge scoring, knockdown count sequence. In essence this unrivalled boxing game mechanism creates the feel of the real ebb and flow of a typical boxing match and the unpredictability of ‘one punch can make all the difference’.

Goal
The goal is to outthink and outmanoeuvre your opponent to consistently inflict damage by exploiting their weaknesses, whilst protecting your own. Damage is realised by the depletion of the opponent’s card hand until either the hand is completely depleted, or a KO or TKO is achieved through a visible damage tracking system.

Gameplay
The game centres around a timely selection of appropriate punch / defence cards from a tailored hand of cards specific to each fighter to inflict damage to selected areas of the opponent’s head and body. Damage is tracked and consequences are specific to each fighter. 
Players select their boxer depending on style, stance and attributes. 
For each match-up a predetermined hand of cards is dealt representing the attributes of the boxer. 
A 10-round match with 3 scoring judges. 
Each round takes the form of 5 mini-rounds which involves 5 turns of attacking and defending. Each turn includes 2 potentially defendable attack moves, one to a selected area of the head and one to an area of the body. The selection of the cards and target area is critical to achieve damage. Any damage inflicted is tracked on the gameboard and consequences to the opponent’s hand of cards is realised and the judges scoring is revealed.
The play sequence thereby creates poker style opportunities for bluffing and counter bluffing, minimizing ‘Chance’ to maximize player control, creating a high sense/degree of anticipation. 
The variety of the cards provide multiple ways and exciting combinations to out manoeuvre your opponent. The highly player controlled mini rounds are countered by an unpredictable judge scoring system, potentially influencing player strategy.

—description from the publisher

In The Soap Opera Game players are aspiring soap opera stars. They roll and move around New York City trying to collect bit acting parts and roles in commercials.

Once a player has enough acting credits they can move to the central area of the board where they act in a soap opera and try to accumulate all the necessary plot elements: sex, health, disaster, marriage, economy, and crime. The first player to get all of these wins the game.

The NASCAR Racing Game is a simple racing game. Cars are moved according to the roll of two standard dice. Landing on a green or yellow flag space requires drawing one of the matching cards. Pit cards must be drawn every five laps.

The rulebook includes 16 pages of NASCAR trivia, but this is not used in the game.

Orchards is a strategic card game in which players will plant, grow, harvest and sell fruit in order to fulfil orders from the market. Each order fulfilled earns the player victory points. At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

Plant the domino-shaped cards in a grid to form the orchard. Grow by adding cards to your hand matching the cards adjacent to the one planted. Harvest fruit by enclosing a card of that type within the orchard. Sell harvested fruit at the market to earn victory points and win the game.

—description from the publisher

The board is a regular checkers board with indentations for balls to roll into place. At the beginning of the game the balls are placed on the board and the board is moved until all the balls are set in place. Any ball that rolled to the king row is replaced with a larger ball to represent a king and has all the powers of a king in regular checkers. Play then continues with regular checkers rules.

boardgame: F.1 Grand Prix, circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
description:
F.1 Grand Prix is a game for 2-5 players. It is a true competition of Formula 1 miniatures on the Belgian automobile circuit from Spa-Francorchamps, one of the most beautiful automobile circuit in the world, by his trace and his landscape, that of the Belgian Ardennes.

For to start, each player selects his car and his stand A B C D or E. Each car is placed in the car park stand, and now the game starts by the qualification, in order to know what pilot will occupy the  after the trails.

Then the players start to race by drawing cards from a deck to determine how far the race around the track. Some cards force you to draw a card from a second deck which could be an advantage or dis-advantage for your car.

The first player who achieves 5 circuit turns and who goes over the check pattern flag is winner. The others continue to play to know their arrival order.
One suggestion! You can thus make championships in 3, 4 or 5 sleeves attributing for each competition, 9 points for first, 7 points for second, 5 points for third, 3 points for fourth and 1 point for the fifth. You determine thus the Champion of the Champions.

Cyber Odyssey is an openworld cyberpunk board game with investigation at its core.

Play as the head of a Faction, recruit agents and send them all over the 5 Districts of New Eden to discover what goes wrong in this city...

Turn after turn, during a definite number of day, player will choose between great amount of actions in order to gather clue, or advantages. They can even fight for them on separate tactical boards.

If a player manages to gather all the clues before everyone, they get to win. Otherwise, each player will add up their clue points which will give the final score.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Have you ever asked yourself why the washed clothes in the washing machine still have stains?
Maybe the detergent is not good enough, or the washing machine doesn't work perfectly.
 However, in this game, you would face a completely different story!
 You would encounter the stains which are alive and try to live. The stains are trying to increase by moving from one cloth to another. They have to compete with each other while trying to fight the washing machine and its washing order. In this story, each time the washing machine starts to rotate, the stains also create a competition to make a higher impact on the clothes. However, sometimes the washing machine and its detergent succeeds and can stop the stains.
you play as a stain and your aim is to try to occupy more space in the washing machine and heavily penetrate into the clothes.
In this game there are a series of rounds that is played in two phases:

     Players phase (Stains Phase)# Washing machine phase


in phase one players can do one of these actions: 

     importing a stain on clothes
     moving existing stains on  clothes
     play an Enzyme card


and in phase two, one of the players roll the dice as the washing machine.

Besides competing with each other, each player should resist the washing machine and its tendency to clean the clothes. At the end of the game, each player who has more stains on clothes will win the game.

—description from the designer

Your team accepted the invitation of a renowned journalism school to Berlin, where a student turned to you for help: Her friend Christopher suddenly went into hiding because he had got wind of a suspicious matter. Christopher was in the utmost panic and said that it was a matter of life and death, but the police must not be called on under any circumstances! Your investigative talent is more in demand than ever in the new city. You make your way through unknown Berlin to help Christopher. Are you late or can you still provide last-minute help?
Instant Numbers initiates an arithmetic contest in which players race against a 60 seconds timer to score as many points as possible by tackling simple arithmetic.

Players engage in an anagramming contest while racing a 60 seconds timer to score as many points as possible .
Points are allocated based on quality of words formed, and player with highest points after pre-agreed number of rounds wins.

Inspired by the arcade games of the 1980's/1990's, Porradaria features characters with special abilities that battle against each other using weapon, battlefield, "punch" as well special cards and the final goal of the game is the elimination of the other characters. This is a 2 (two) deck card game, with 5 (five) characters in each deck, which can be played by 2 to 5 players with 1 deck, or up to 10 players with 2 decks.

In Instant rhymes, players engage in a contest of calling words with correct suffix

At the start of a round, a games master (or player acting as one) calls the active suffix and the first player to call a correct word with that suffix gets the card.
Play continues till pre-agreed number of cards are exhausted.
Player with the most cards at the end of the round wins the game.

-description from designer

A casual family game of spatial strategy, tree planting and animal rescue for all ages above 6.

In Replantica, you restore lost forest and rescue displaced animals. Players start by planting saplings, then water them to grow large trees. Special planting configurations on the playmat yield various bonuses for the players. Throughout the game, players must populate the new forest with animals and can win through a combination of clever strategy and lucky dice. The goal of the game is to be the first player to plant all your large trees and rehome all your rescued animals, one of which should be in your home habitat.

Replantica is produced entirely of natural materials (glass pebbles, wooden dice, canvas playmat, cotton drawstring bag for storage, cardboard and paper).

Every box of Replantica that is sold includes a donation to plant 10 real trees through reforestation charities.

—description from the publications

Players engage in a singing contest by recalling relevant songs word-lyrics fastest.

On a turn, a games master (or player acting as one) calls a word and fastest player to sing a song gets the card.

Play continues till number of pre-agreed cards is exhausted or reached by a player.

Player with most cards at the end of the game wins.

-description from designer

For decades the Blanco family has run the town of San Luis on the New Mexico border. So, they didn’t take to kindly to John Bannon when he rode into town and made himself the sheriff. Soon the silver mines, saloons and stage-coach were all working for him. Tensions between the Blancos and Bannon were boiling over when a quiet stranger with a wicked gun rode into town. Who will win control of San Luis?

The mini-campaign is loosely based on the film "A Fistful of Dollars" and is structured in quick “acts” that could be played in a single evening. 
Both sides face decisions that allow for a great deal of replay value and let the players write their own story in the West....

Players choose their own adventure with one side picking the scenario and the other choosing the set up.  
There are a possible 27 different battles that can be played.
The mini-campaign will consist of three of these gunfights.

RUTHLESS, a complete set of western gunfight rules, is also included!  
This booklet has everything you need to play!

—description from the publisher
A mini-expansion from the Matchbox Collection Encore set, this adds 2 new Boss cards and a player aid to the base game of Eiyo.
This mini-expansion is part of the Matchbox Collection Encore series; it introduces three powerful one-time effects that are obtained when players close one another's shelves.

Virus Veteran is a card game in which it is your goal to have less virus points than your opponents. The card game is split in two kinds of cards, action cards including tourists, mutations, veterans and more... and into standard virus cards. The standard cards will determine your number of points at the end!

To gain the upper hand you can either use tourists and mutations to double the numbers of your opponents points, or you can use medics and lockdowns to block or annihilate aggressive cards of the other players. Bottlenecks and veteran cards allow you to draw cards from your opponents or to get rid of your own virus points.

The single cure card determines the end of the game and boosts your chance to win the game.

The cards are designed in a humoristic comic style which is inspired by the ongoing pandemic. I mean look at the toilet-paper hoarders, aren`t they perfect fitting joker cards?

—description from the publisher

Are you a fan of Imposter or Murder Mystery games? Then Imposters On Mars is the perfect game for you. It turns your house (location) into a real-life game board — a colony on Mars. Beware, an Imposter is sabotaging your hard work. Complete jobs and work together to repair the colony. Keep a close eye and figure out who the Imposters are. Vote and cast them out. But be careful not to vote for the wrong person.

Beware, an Imposter is sabotaging your hard work. Complete jobs and work together to repair the colony. Keep a close eye and figure out who the Imposters are. Vote and cast them out. But be careful not to vote for the wrong person.

Your house is now a real-life game board. The rooms in your house (or location) are the game board's spaces where you’ll complete mini-games. These mini-games are called Jobs. Every Player starts with four cards. The first choose a Player’s color. The Player then takes their matching color Vote card. The third card determines who the Imposters are. Finally, every Player chooses a Career and their Job order.

Every room is set up with a mini-game (matching, math, memory, etc.). These mini-games are called Jobs. Players complete Jobs and share proof of completing them. Playing as the Imposter gives you the ability to sabotage Jobs. Imposters are randomly selected. Imposters sabotage Jobs and force Players to freeze their gameplay for a period of time. This allows the Imposter to make a move and kill or deceive players.

Step away from the table and turn your house into a real-life murder mystery party. Turn off the lights and Bring Your Own Flashlight (BYOF) if you dare to play in the dark.

Oh no! A magical tornado has devastated the library and torn away the pages of the grimoire. Apprentice magicians, it's up to you to make up stories and bring back the pages... in the correct order!

In Team Story, players are either Listener or Storytellers. Once 20 cards are placed face down and the scroll board is created, each Storytellers pick 3 cards without letting the  Listener see them.

Storyteller starts and continue the story using a card from their hand. Once they take part in the story with a card, they place it on the scroll board face down and draw another one from the pool. When the scroll board is full, the round ends. All cards are shuffled and given to the Listener. They now have to retrace the story.

But wait! Some cards have Twins! Will you be able to find the correct card and remake the story through the cards in 60 seconds?

The game ends once everyone has been Listener once. The player who scored the most stars wins the game!

The game is played with a ball and two dice, a number die to determine the number of spaces the ball may move (even = straight directions, odd = diagonally) and a special die. The special die has the Letters A (2x), R, C (2x) and N with the following meanings:
A = move ahead
B = move back
C = move ahead and roll again
N = don't move
The spaces on the game board marked with letters also have special meanings derived from the soccer rules.

The game ends after a prearranged time or number of goals, with the player with the most goals winning the game.

—user summary

Paint from a new perspective! In Canvas: Reflections, the artistic puzzle has a new layer -- Mirror Cards! These reversible cards add deeper strategy, and also give you increased flexibility to string together combinations of Elements. Additionally, the new board offers a wider selection of Art Cards that allows you to better plan ahead and presents more choices even when running low on Inspiration Tokens.

Also features:
Gold Bonus Ribbons - Grab a Gold Bonus Ribbon for lining up a specific Element next to a Gold Bonus icon.

New Scoring Cards for puzzling new challenges and added replayability.
​
-description from designer

description:
In Escape Velocity, players will compete to become the most profitable company by investing in space elevators or mass drivers, establishing colonies on Mars or Ceres, building colony ships destined for Ganymede or Callisto, and helping to industrialize the final frontier.

Each round, players will select and execute actions, pick up and deliver materials, research new technologies, and spend their money carefully. Planets move each round and players will need to watch carefully as they plan their routes.

—description from the publisher

The four vessels of the Suffren-class were based on the Duquesne-class, though each was built to slightly different standards as lessons were learned from the preceding ship. Their hull form was sleek and graceful, taking a classic cruiser form. 
While the Duquesne-class lineage was apparent, the Suffren-class had a much more modern look about them. Armour protection was somewhat better than the preceding class, though speed was reduced as a consequence.

Foch spent the early period of WWII in Dakar as part of Force M and formed part of a force bombarding Italian assets near Genoa. 
After the fall of France Foch joined Vichy French forces before being scuttled with other French vessels in late 1942.

—description from the publisher's website

The four vessels of the Suffren-class were based on the Duquesne-class, though each was built to slightly different standards as lessons were learned from the preceding ship. Their hull form was sleek and graceful, taking a classic cruiser form. 
While the Duquesne-class lineage was apparent, the Suffren-class had a much more modern look about them. Armour protection was somewhat better than the preceding class, though speed was reduced as a consequence. 
Suffren remained at Alexandria from 1940 to 1943, when she joined the Allied cause.

Suffren, lead ship in her class, operated in the Dodecanese Islands early on in the war before peacefully surrendering to the Royal Navy - in sharp contrast to the fate of many French vessels at Mers-el-Kebir. 
Refitted she would join the Allied cause in 1943.

—description from the publisher's website

Built as a training cruiser for the French Marine Nationale the Jeanne d'Arc was both floating school and combat capable warship. 
During WWII she formed part of a flotilla taking French gold to Canada before joining Free French forces in 1943.

The single vessel of the Jeanne d’Arc-class was designed to use the same guns as the Duguay-Trouin-class. She was considered a training vessel and as such didn't require the speed or armour of a cruiser. 
Stationed in Martinique, she joined the Allies in 1943, receiving a refit in which her torpedo tubes and aircraft were removed, and improved anti-aircraft armament was fitted. After 1943 she served in the Mediterranean.

—description from the publisher

Also known as the Jaguar-class, Chacal (Jackal)-class destroyers were designed to fulfil a scouting role for fleet battle lines and to attack enemy shipping in the Mediterranean. As a result, they possessed poor endurance, mounted no anti-submarine weapons, and had woefully inadequate anti-aircraft fire, especially in the face of the German Luftwaffe.

The six ships of this class were all named after predators (Chacal, Jaguar, Léopard, Lynx, Panthère, Tigre) and saw wildly varying actions and fates during the war.

This pack enables you to represent three of these vessels on the tabletop.

—description from the publisher's website

Mimic Taking is a trick-taking game for 3-4 players where the winner of a trick is the player who plays a card closest in rank to the lead card.

It is a "must-follow" game, and the winner of a trick takes all of the cards from the trick face-down into a score pile.  If the trick was won with a trump card, the winner may discard some of the cards without collecting them.

Points are awarded at the end of a hand based on comparing the number of cards a player took in the suit where they took the most and the suit where they took the least, with a bonus being awarded if the number of cards is the same.

The player with the most points after a number of rounds wins!

There were to have been four ships of the Richelieu-class, with the first two laid down in 1935 and the second pair following in 1935. 
Gascogne was cancelled and Clemenceau never completed. The last ship followed a modified design which placed one of the turrets aft of the superstructure – the original design had both main armament turrets forward, much like the Dunkerque-class. 
Designed as a counter to the powerful Italian battleships being built, the Richelieu was protected against 15-inch shells and mounted eight 15-inch guns of its own, on what started as a 35,000-ton hull. Neither ship of the class was quite finished at the time of the French surrender.

Richelieu, which was almost complete, escaped to Dakar in order to keep here under French control. She came under repeated British attacks that were intended to compel the battleship to join the Free French Naval forces or sink her, thus denying the Axis opportunity to deploy the Richelieu for their own means. 
In 1942 she eventually, joined the Allied cause and, after a refit in the USA, was deployed to the Far East to serve with the British fleet for operations against the Japanese - although present at the Battle of the Malacca Strait, she was too far away to directly engage the enemy vessels.

She was part of the force that liberated Singapore in the aftermath of the Japanese surrender in 1945 and later operated in French Indochina before being recalled to France in December 1945. 
She saw training duty on and off active-service for the remainder of her existence, eventually being sold for scrap in 1968.

—description from the publisher's website

In 1923 it was decided to convert an incomplete Normandie-class battleship to a carrier. 
Despite a refit in 1935, she was far too slow for fleet operations in World War Two and was demilitarised in 1942.

In 1943 she joined the Free French Naval Force where she served for a time before being converted to an aircraft transport.

Béarn was the only French aircraft carrier to serve in the war – of the two ships of the projected Joffre-class, one was cancelled and the other was not completed.

—description from the publisher's website

When launched, the Surcouf was the largest submarine in the world, only surpassed in the Second World War by the Japanese I-400-class submarine.

Her intended role was that of commerce raider and her design was intended to maximise this mission. She had a range of 10,000 miles, could sail for 90 days and was equipped with a Besson MN-411 floatplane in a hanger aft of the tower to scout for victims and spot for her main battery.

Her torpedo outfit included four forward internal tubes, a quad trainable mount aft and a second quad trainable mount aft equipped with lightweight torpedoes. She even had a prison hold for up to 40 captives. However, the most striking aspect of her design was the inclusion of a special, watertight turret forward of the tower which carried two eight-inch naval guns.

Surcouf was a very complex design, plagued with mechanical troubles. She was difficult, slow to dive and rolled badly on the surface in rough seas. She was also so low to the horizon that the effective range of her 8-inch guns was greatly reduced. When war broke out, Surcouf was in the French Antilles and by the time she returned to Brest was in need of repairs. When France fell, Surcouf escaped to England, where she was seized by British sailors. She was subsequently turned over to the Free French who regarded her with pride while the British Admiralty considered her, perhaps unfairly, something of a joke.

—description from the publisher's website

In Warriors of Orlandia, each player chooses a Warrior card and four Support cards. These cards are used throughout the game to defeat the opposition in a tactical battle.

A player wins the game when either the opposing Warrior is defeated, or they have the highest life at the end of the fifth round. When a Warrior’s Life is 0, that Warrior is defeated.

The leader of the game draws a theme (for example, WILD). The other players then quickly choose between the "point of view" cards in their hands, the one that has the most to do with the chosen theme. Then, the leader chooses one of the cards, and the player who discarded it walks a square on the board. The letter need not be perfect for the topic. The leader usually chooses the most creative, funny or interesting card.

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Grime & Gold at the Ghostgate
description:
Grime & Gold At The Ghostgates is a dungeon crawl adventure module for Dungeon Degenerates. It drops you into a swampy dungeon in the Würstreich filled with an abundance of Scrogs, Scroglins & other nasty, moist monsters that would happily have you die in the dungeon & munch on your bones!

Grime & Gold At The Ghostgates comes with the exclusive Scroglin Soldier card for use with Dungeon Degenerates & a full-color map.

Second printing with fixed text ; fixed for first printing with omitted text available as pdf.

--user summary

It is the Dawn of the Temple Vigil! The congregation prays happily ... but, suddenly, some worshipers begin to be demonized! ENCOTSTO! BACKREST! They all scream!
It's time for Exorcism!
The faithful must gather and decide who is Leaning and do Exorcism on him! At the end of the service, who will remain anointed?

A player is chosen as the narrator, he is not part of any of the teams, but he will lead the game as a pastor of the church. The rest are divided into two sides: Backrest and Faithful.
Backers want to drive all church members crazy and win if the number of members is less than or equal to the number of backers. Believers need to exorcise all the backs to win.

Each turn the backrests secretly choose a player to be eliminated and all players vote to eliminate a second player, who can be one of the backrests or another faithful one.

Your goal in TRAPEZ (トラペーツ) is to create a large block of your color in the playing area. The challenge is that every piece played features both your color and the color of your opponent!

The playing area is a hexagon composed of 19 smaller hexagons. Each player starts with nine hexagon tiles that are half light and half dark, and one tile starts in the center of the playing area. On a turn, place a tile in any orientation you choose on any unoccupied space of the playing area. If this placement fills the last empty space around another tile, you must rotate that newly surrounded tile 60º either clockwise or counterclockwise. (If you surround multiple tiles at the same time, you rotate only one of these surrounded tiles.)

If you create a block of seven tiles that have your color connected across them, you win the game. If all the tiles have been placed without someone winning instantly, whoever has the largest block of color wins, with ties being broken in favor of the starting player.

•••

このゲームは2人用の本格的なアブストラクトゲームです。

六角形のタイルをおく！
タイルを囲んでまわす！
自分の色をつなげる！
たくさんつなげたら勝ち！

非常にシンプルなルールなので誰でもすぐに遊ぶことができます。
六角形のタイルを回すだけで驚くほど状況が変化するため、高い思考力を必要とするゲームになりました。
タイルを置く場所や向きを少し変えるだけで勝敗に大きく影響するため、飽きることなく何度でも遊ぶことができます。

アブストラクトゲームが得意な人でもこのゲームを極めるのは簡単ではないでしょう。

—user summary (Japanese)

Marmot is a simple placement game designed by Mark Steere to be easy to program.

Marmot is played on an initially empty hex board.

Players take turns placing a token of their color on any empty hex that is adjacent to no more than 1 token of the their color. As soon as one player is unable to make a placement, that player loses the game.

Each player chooses a set of colored game pieces (red, yellow, blue). Each tile represents the product of two multiples. Players then randomly selects ten tiles and places them at the correct intersection of the vertical and horizontal lines which represent the multiples. For example, 45 is placed at the intersection of 9 and 5. Each player is awarded ten points for each correct tile placement. The process is repeated until there is a winner with the most number of points.

Great for learning new cultures!

We are all different, but all equal. With so many countries to learn from, this will be a perfect start for a lifelong
appreciation for cultures.

Exploring people and cultures activates children’s natural curiosity, and keeps them engaged time and time again.

The child rolls the dice, chooses a continent and places a pawn there, chooses a card. We check that the card fits the continent. If so, the child solves the task. For the correct answer, he can add a stamp to the players' passport. At the end of the turn, he draws 2 tokens from the bag. If there are two conductors - the players lose one of the tickets.

If they fill out their passport before using all their tickets, they win.

This is an educational game designed to stimulate interest in chemistry, general science, and math in a fun and entertaining way.

Players move around the board while answering questions. One set of cards features questions about the Periodic Table and the other set includes questions about general science and math. The player who circles the game board three times is declared the winner.

Designed for 2-6 players ages 10 and older.

Includes board, cards, pawns, and element symbols.

WINNER OF BEST WAR THEMED GAME IN THE 2021 52-card game contest ***
        
    


OPERTION AKIMBO is the code name for a top secret Allied WWII mission to liberate key areas in northern France & set the stage for a full scale invasion at D-Day.  The game is a solo card game using a 2-sided 54 card deck.

You General, must organize the joint effort between the US, Canadian & English forces in this critical operation. Your task is to lay the optimal path through the French country side, organize men and air support & achieve key objectives. Finally, it is critical you deliver all 3 squads to the rendezvous point.

Each turn you will choose 1 card & 1 side of that card to play. One side will be a path through the countryside with enemies to overcome & the other is a completed mission objective, unit forces to deploy, or air support.

You win by completing three 10 card paths, overcoming all enemies by either ground and air troops & completing 15 mission objectives before all cards are used.

The game is fully PnP on BGG and was designed for the 2021 54-Card deck contest.

-description from designer

boardgame: Pub Battles: Austerlitz – One Sharp Blow
description:
After defeating the Austrians at Ulm and occupying Vienna, Napoleon’s first campaign as Emperor should have been over. The Russians then declared war, joining the remaining Austrian forces. As winter approached, the long, tenuous French supply lines through enemy territory began to crumble. Worse yet, the Prussians were forming on the border, threatening to join the coalition. Napoleon needed a victory now!

A master of deception, he lured the Allies into attacking by thinking he was much weaker than he really was. As the Allies attacked his right, their center became dangerously weak. As Napoleon ordered his decisive counter attack on the Pratzen Heights he stated, “One sharp blow and the war is over.” It was. Austerlitz became Napoleon’s greatest victory.
The true French Order of Battle is unknown. The Allies must attack forcefully to capitalize on French weakness. If they run into strength, they must be able to quickly transition to a secure defense. 
 Beautiful large map. More blocks than Marengo but less than Waterloo. This hits the sweet spot.
Tons of replayability. Yes, you can do what Napoleon did. This game opens things up to the many other possibilities. Craft your own brilliant plan of deception to defeat the Allies. There are many ways to approach this battle from both sides.

It is always fun to be Napoleon in a game but this game also presents a very interesting problem to the Allied player. There are many ways for the Allies to approach this battle. This game lets you explore and try them out.   
The unknown OB also works great for solitaire play!
