Find the Animals hiding in nature!

'Animal Malmienu' is a fun game of memory and speed. Hide animals in the grass, forest, and rocks and guess which is the last to remain hidden.

Four different games cater to a variety of age groups and player numbers.

Detective Battle (3 players; Age 10+; 20 minutes): Players reveal their animals hidden in nature one at a time and try to guess the final two missing animals

Memory Challenege (4-5 players; Age 8+; 15 minutes): Pass around the animals hidden in nature and try to remember them all. Which animal has been left out?

Speed Game! (3-5 players; Age 6+; 15 minutes): A race to capture the missing animal after checking all the animals hidden in nature.

Hide-and-Seek (2 players; Age 6+; 10 minutes): The animals are playing hide-and-seek. Can you guess all the animals correctly? A great game for young children.

Dragon Killers is a variant of the Dragon Hunters fan extension created by SCOTT.
The objective of this fan expansion is to allow players to attack the dragon from Dragon Killers stations armed with spears forged with metals from meteorites whose sharp spearheads can pierce the magical beast’s armoured skin. The princess will also favour the heroic victors with a temporary alliance.

The objective of the game is to collect candies – Treat cards – in five suits. Treat cards have various points value and players try not to exceed the value of the Haunt card when collecting Treat suits. Otherwise, the Treat suit is haunted!
The game is played over a series of tricks and winning a trick allows a player to choose which Treat card to take first. After 3 rounds, players total the value of their collected Treat cards and haunted Treat suits score only half of their value.
The player with the most points is the winner.

The new TGC edition have a brand new colorful graphics as well as 3 additional modules: A Co-Op mode, Special Treats and Wild Tricks (total of 18 new cards).

Participated in the BGG 2021 Two-Player Print and Play Game Design Contest.

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: Out of this World
description:
A mini expansion with 60 new, crazy, silly, sci-fi and fantasy themed unique cards that will take your Pursuit of Happiness games out of this world!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Crook is a trick taking game that requires each player to bid on the number of tricks they plan to take during a round of play. The person to the left of the dealer leads out by playing one card of any color and play goes clockwise with each player playing one card per turn.  Players must “follow suit” by playing a card of the same color that was led out.  If they are out of the color that was led, they can play any color including the trump color.  The player that wins the trick takes the cards played (they are set aside to indicate a trick won) and then leads out for the next round of play.

The goal of the game is to score the highest number of points. If a player's bid is made exactly (not under or over) add amount bid plus 5 points. If bid is not made, subtract amount bid or the number of tricks taken – whichever is the greater number. This game is easy to play, but becomes very difficult to accurately predict the number of tricks that will actually be taken.  The game ends with a special round where each player is dealt one card and each player shows every other player their card by putting it on their forehead (without looking at it).  Bids are made and the cards are laid on the table to see who takes the trick.

—description from the designer

Ever since that fateful day when George Armstrong Custer’s battalion of the Seventh Cavalry met its fate on the Little Bighorn River, men made careers by selling their stories claiming to be the sole survivor.  
By the 1950s the last of these men, as well as the Sioux and Cheyenne participants, were in their twilight years.  
They all vied for a chance to tell the tallest tale and have their story become a bestselling book which would dominate the United States understanding of the battle.  
Was Custer a barbarian -or- a hero of Manifest Destiny?

You must weave your own exciting story of the battle and seek fame and fortune by fighting your way through the most dramatic moments of the Battle of the Little Bighorn!

This mini-campaign is structured in quick “acts” that could be played in a single evening. 
Both sides face decisions that allow for a great deal of replay value and let the players write their own story of the Battle of the Little Bighorn….

Players choose their own adventure with one side picking the scenario and the other choosing the set up.  
There are a possible 27 different battles that can be played.  
The mini-campaign will consist of three of these gunfights.

RUTHLESS, a complete set of western gunfight rules, is also included!  
This booklet has everything you need to play!

—description from the publisher

Untold Stories #3 provides a cooperative campaign for Nemesis: Lockdown. They are presented in a graphic-novel/comic-book style, over 60+ full-color pages.


     60+ A5 pages
     Punch board of special tokens
     A lot of additional gameplay.

In What's Your Sign?, players compete to create the most unique and humorous horoscopes for each other. In each round, a "Horoscope" card is drawn, and each player choses an "Ending" card to complete the Horoscope. The winner of each round is decided by a rotating judge. Pro tip: pick your caption card to match the judge’s sense of humor, desires, or sign.

—description from the designer

Secluded in the deepest wasteland lies the monastery of Wattenbeek. For hundreds of years it defied the course of history and provided a holy retreat for a small group of nuns. But time does not stop at the venerable convent, and it is hard to find young nuns willing to move into such solitude. There is not even Internet! Despite great protests on the part of the abbess, the convent is now to be sold and for the first time in years there are guests within the sacred walls. The prospective buyers, who have traveled a long way, have their own plans for the monastery, but when the real estate agent Günther Hübsch is found dead in the monastery garden on the second day, the blood freezes in the veins of those present: Because the perpetrator is among them. The police are alerted and the abbess invites all the guests and nuns to the dining room to convict the murderer and then hand him over to the police.

—description from the publisher

MISSION OF THE GAME
Your task is to take the 12 tribes of Israel out of the slavery of Egypt and lead them with Moses into the Promised Land. During their journey the people of Israel meet many difficulties, which can affect their morale. The task of Moses (the player) is to keep their morale high enough, otherwise traveling will stop. God is with Israel in the pillars of cloud and fire. God helps his people to reach the destination by giving manna from Heaven and water from the rock. Sometimes stubborn people stir up the wrath of God and they have to sacrifice sin offerings in order to reconcile their deeds and get blessings. God rewards the obedience of people by letting them carry the Ark of the Covenant. The winner is the one who reaches the goal and offers first a Sacrifice of Thanksgiving.

The players will advance in pre-determined path in the game by throwing a dice and will have to keep their moral points above 2 by getting maximum of one for free or paying three/five silver coins. The players can also bog each other down by bribing the enemy causing the other to pay to progress or get slowed down. Leading the race after Mount of Sinai is also important, the leader will have the Ark of the Covenant giving him the choice to make additional step.

boardgame: Nickelodeon Blue's Clues Find The Clues, Matching Board Game
description:
INSPIRED BY BLUE’S CLUES AND YOU!: This game is based off the hit kid’s TV program Blue’s Clues and You! Look for clues around the house just like Blue does in the show! Blue’s 3 clues will lead you to a solution.

FAST-PACED MATCHING GAME: Blue will give you 3 clues. To find them, look quickly through the house and on the board. If you can find the third clue quickest, you’re the winner of that round!
GREAT LEARNING TOOL: Matching games help kids with color and object recognition.

QUICK START, FUN AND ENGAGING: Just take out the game components, read the easy-to-follow instructions and you're all set. For 2-4 players, ages 3 and up.

Spin Master offers a variety of board games & puzzles for kids & adults. Classic, card, family, trivia, party games & more. Puzzle lines feature characters for kids of all ages, adult jigsaw, 3-D & more.

—description from the publisher

Gem Rummy is similar to standard Gin Rummy except each card has a background color & gem symbol; Kings have two background colors; (Jokers are included but are not used in hands). Adding colors to each card allows for more interesting combinations (like having all eight gems in hand).  Another difference is that (2) 6-sided dice are used at the beginning of each hand to establish the "Knock number", and also the "Diamond number" for a special meld called "Queen's Diamonds".

The object is to get your hand down to a few points of Deadwood by getting Melds.

There are 3 phases to each hand: 1) DEAL & ROLL; 2) CARD PLAY; 3) SHOWDOWN & SCORING. 
 Cards in Melds and Lay-offs will not score for your opponent, but have no inherent point values themselves.  Cards not in Melds or Lay-offs are called Deadwood and might add points to your opponent’s score.

There are 4 different Melds available: Sets, Runs, Stashes, and Queen’s Diamonds (the latter two are new). 

     SET: At least 3 cards of the same rank (i.e., 5, 5, 5).
     RUN: At least 3 consecutive cards in the same suit (i.e., 6, 7, 8, all clubs).
     STASH: At least 4 cards of the same gem color (i.e., 4 Emeralds). 
     QUEEN’S DIAMONDS:  Any Queen + one designated Diamond card that is determined by the initial roll number on the dice.  Both dice are rolled to find one number (i.e., 3 and 4 = 7, indicating 7 of Diamonds is the card). An “11” or “12” indicates JACK of DIAMONDS. 


Also, "Gem Parts" is a bonus players can earn by having all 8 gems at the end of a hand.

A new minimum score rule keeps the game moving along nicely.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Addams Family: A Delightfully Frightful Creepy Board Game
description:
Two to Seven ghouls compete in a race around the Addams family mansion to finish tasks (collect tokens) so they can enter the path to the swamp and arrive there first!

Along the way fellow family members may try to trick you into helping them via possession, push you backwards when they land on your space, have a roll-off with someone to win tokens... or perhaps the mansion itself may try to trap you. Visit the cemetery, the haunted tree and more grotesque locations on your quest!

In Anyday Funnies, you are a cartoonist rushing to draw a comic strip before you hit your deadline.

Each player gets one play sheet to draw on. 1st player rolls  three dice. If you roll a pair or a trio, only you get a bonus.

Starting with the player who rolled, select 1 number from 
the dice & draw/write what is on the chart below.

After all players have drawn, X out one deadline clock. The game ends when all clocks are x’d out. Use the scoring chart to determine score.

All players show each other their comic (you get a bonus if you make someone laugh!) The player with the highest point total wins.

—description from the designer

This is a card game where you combine ingredients to make meals.  Opponents can sabotage meals by placing flies or hairs in your meal, causing you to lose points. You gain points based on the meal you create, plus any extra ingredients you have added. The first person to reach 20 points wins!

—description from the designer

Iconoclash: Castle Clash features 4 original fighters to add to your roster:


     Conquistotter: This shiny loving otter will collect and steal items, until he has enough to throw them back at his opponent.
     Lilac Everdawn: The Crown Princess of the Seraphim Empire will put an end to her opponents with her graceful swordplay. Her speed and adaptivity make her one flexible fighter.
     Spark: Spark is a tricksy mage who attacks at range. Zone your opponent to keep them at bay.
     Tidal Golem: A titan from the ocean’s abyss. Use its mighty strength to crush all that stand in your way.


In Iconoclash: Castle Clash you’ll take the fight to the heart of the Seraphim Empire, with the Sunset Tower and Castle Dawn Maps.

A Standalone Expansion - Season 1

-description from publisher

In Battle of the Hexes, players will take part in a war of spell cards. They play as mages with a set of spells they choose from, who do battle to the death. There are different types of magic as well as different types of spells; and the player must carefully select their strategy and spells to defeat their opponent.

Players must each build a hand of 15 cards, by drawing by turn from a draft of cards. These cards can then be played on different lanes, damaging only what is in their path unless stated otherwise on the card. If no other card is in their respective lane, damage from the cards goes directly to the opposing player’s health. Once a player loses all their health, they lose the game.

General Gameplay:

Gameplay occurs over several stages. Players keep track of whose turn it is through the turn indicator card. They must simply flip it when their turn ends.

Draw Stage:

During the draw stage: All cards sets should be shuffled first. Determine a starting player, that player is dealt the “Destruction Spells” set of cards. Then they will choose one to keep for their hand, and pass the rest of the cards to the opposing player, who will then choose one of their own, and return it to the starting player again. That process should repeat itself until each player has a maximum of 5 cards of that particular set. Any remaining cards should be placed aside for later use in the game. Afterwards, that same process is repeated with the “Summoning Spells” set of cards, followed by the “Alteration Spells” set of cards.

Play Stage:

This stage is where players play the cards they have chosen during the Draw Stage. The starting player from the previous stage starts here. During this stage, players get to use their 6 stamina points to play cards they have in their hand. Each card requires a certain amount of stamina points to be played. Any unused stamina points at the end of this stage cannot be used as extra in the next round. For the first round only: once the player has played all the cards they want, it is then the opposing player’s turn to play their cards in the same manner. Each card type must be placed in the correct area of the battlefield sheet according to their respective type symbols. “Destruction” and “Alteration” cards must remain face down until the Battle Stage starts. Players may stack up as many of these cards in one lane as they want, but only one “Summoning” card is allowed in one lane at a time. Players may draw cards from any of the draw decks at any time during this stage, but it will cost them 1 stamina point.

Battle Stage:

During this stage, the player who played their cards last gets to attack first. The players’ cards will deal damage to the opposing player’s cards only on their respective lanes unless stated - otherwise on the card - and according to their damage points on the card. Damage is done to both sides simultaneously. Cards that belong to the same spell class deal only half damage to each other. Any card that loses all its HP points must be discarded. All discarded cards are returned to their respective draw deck, placed at the bottom of each. If the opposing player’s lane is empty, then damage would go directly to that player’s own HP. Players keep track of how much damage each card is dealt through the red health pieces. One red health piece means the card lost one HP, and so on. Players keep track of their own health through the counters.

Following this stage it is back to the play stage for the opposing player. Whenever players run out of cards, each draw deck is taken and reshuffled, and the players draft cards between each other in another Draw Stage.

Black Flag is a nautical theme game, in the game you can choose your role, recruit crew, transport goods, and expand the fleet to dominate the Caribbean.

Cards:

Character card
Pirate: Each time you recruit a crew member, you can get one extra person.
Businessman: Each trade will get an extra 5 gold.
Governor: Can station one or more ships in the port, When a ship is stationed, all income from transactions in the port is attributed to you.

Ship card
Heavy cruiser: 30HP 3DMG 3RNG 1SPD
Light destroyer 20HP 3DMG 1RNG 3SPD
20G/Ship
10G to repair well

Goods Card
Wood 1G Land-Island 3G
Sugar 1G Island-Land 3G
Drapery 2G Land-Island 7G
Rum 1G/5bucket (Each crew member consumes one piece per round)

Ship Equipment Card
Speed card: +1 SPD
Shield card: +3 HP
Cannon card: +2 DMG
3G/card

Crew Card
2G/crew
Used for ship-to-board warfare

How To Play
Choose a ship as your lead ship (Fleet speed depends on the speed of the leader ship)
Initially you will get 100G
Choose your own birth port
In your turn you can choose to move, buy goods, buy/repair ship and attack other players' fleets.

Combat system
Sea War: Cannon attack between fleets, If the HP of the ship is to zero, it will be sunk.
Ship-to-board warfare: Close to the enemy fleet, you can board the enemy ship for close combat. Offset the crew, and the more people win.

How To Win
I.Destroy other players
II.Businessman can earn 1500G to win
III.The governor can occupy 8 ports and win

You board an aircraft with a team of adventurers to perform many trips through the cities of Celestia and recover their wonderful treasures. Your journey will not be safe, but you will attempt to be the richest adventurer by collecting the most stars.

However, in this new game, the captain will draw a card from the pink deck, which is the question deck, basically cards with assumptions of the player, such as "I like pineapples on my pizza". The other players need to guess if it’s a yes or a no.

The players who get it right will be able to get stars from the next island. The players who get it wrong will NOT get stars from the next island.

The captain then draws 1 card from the dark red deck: the deck of destruction. An empty dark red card means that the ship will not face any natural disaster.
Everyone will draw 1 card from the green deck: the escape card.
The captain will then reveal his cards of destruction. They will then try to escape from these natural disasters. The captain will go first.

The first one to get 30 stars will win.

—description from the designer

Heroes Tournament is a deck building RPG game featuring multiple heroes and their unique powers. The winner will be crowned as the Ultimate Hero Champion and players must collect 9 out of 13 of their power cards in order to be the strongest hero.

On top of all of the battles between heroes, there will also be monsters lurking about in order to disrupt the process. Heroes can team up with each other until they've reached to the point of death match where they'll have to face each other.

—description from the designer

The game consists of 10 pastel-coloured dice and 50 cards representing ice cream cones with one to five scoops. Each player receives 5 dice and a half of the cards, face down. Both players turn the first card from their deck at the same time and then roll the dice. They save the dice matching the scoops on their card, and may reroll any of the other dice as many times as needed to complete their ice creams. The player that first completes their ice cream wins both cards, and a new round starts. When the piles are exhausted, the game ends. The player with the most cards wins.

We set out to build a game that truly captures the legend of Sasquatch. There have been more than 10,000 reported sightings of Sasquatch in the US alone, yet no one has successfully proven or disproven their existence. In Finding Sasquatch, you'll find elements of the legend of Sasquatch - mystery, deception, fact-finding, adventure, and a little right-place-at-the-right-time. We hope this game can bring some mystery and excitement to your game nights, and maybe even create some believers.

What makes Finding Sasquatch unique?

Gameplay depth: There are unique strategy elements around determining how and where to find Sasquatch and players must be strategic about how they influence Sasquatch's location, what information they share with other players, and what information they keep for themselves.

Caters to broad audience: The game has multiple layers of strategy and complexity, making it great for everyone from newbies to hardcore gamers. Play it for family game night, with your gaming group, or just 1 on 1 with your bestie.

Beautiful artwork and high quality cards: To fully capture the Sasquatch theme we focused on getting the artwork, graphic design, and physical features just right. Gameplay aside, we think you'll be pleased with the quality of the product.

—description from the publisher

In battle, there are no equals.

Unmatched is a highly asymmetrical miniature fighting game for two or four players. Each hero is represented by a unique deck designed to evoke their style and legend. Tactical movement and no-luck combat resolution create a unique play experience that rewards expertise, but just when you've mastered one set, new heroes arrive to provide all-new match-ups.

Deadpool is a single-hero expansion to be used with any existing Unmatched full set. It includes a hero miniature, a health dial, and 31 cards.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A Wildest West scoring condition card for Circle the Wagons, which was a twitter joke that made its way to a digital promo card, and later released physically.


Released in the April 2021 Board Game of the Month Club $10+ package.

boardgame: The Final Light-Year: The Cratos Sector
description:
April 2021 episode for The Final Light-Year.

This kit adds a new sector map, new overlord, minion and commander ships and a new event.


Released in the April 2021 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

Монополия Волгоград, or Monopoly: Volgograd is a revisited version of the classic game of Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the city of Volgograd in Russia.

Buy local businesses and win by bankrupting the other players.

boardgame: Wer hat dich, du schöner Wald..
description:
Players use magnets to remove items from the forest. If animals are removed, minus points are given. The player tallies up his points at the end and the one with the most points is the winner.

—user summary

A family card game where the roll of the dice, decides a players fate.

Included items:
58 Playing Cards
1 Rule card
1 Playing die

The aim of the game is for players to loose all their cards. Each player is dealt 7 cards at the start of the game. Each player then takes it in turns to roll the die to try and match the number with the number on their cards. If the numbers match, the player can put down the matching cards. If the numbers do not match, the player than has to pick up 1 card from the separate pile. sound simple enough? well... some of the numbered cards have symbols on them that can power certain activities in the game. These symbols include... Role again, next player pick up x4, Change direction, Next player miss a go. These cards can be great to trip up opponents or launch you towards victory. In the game there are also special cards. The 'CHOICE' card allows a player to put down a card, no matter the number they roll. Meaning a player with this in hand can always be saved, If a player is clever they may choose to keep hold of tis card. 
The 'STEAL' card will cause wars and arguments. This card allows a player to steal any opponents roll, at any point in the game. Meaning a player can put down their cards and the player that stole the roll can put down theirs. This card can flip a game on its head and launch players into the lead.

A game full of twists and turns, but to win this game do you need luck or skill? we will let you decide.

—description from the designer

Let's have a nervous breakdown with the ingredient card. You can make a Kua Aina hamburger by stacking the cards you got!

Players take turns sitting and memorize the front of an ingredient card that is then placed face down. Next. ingredient cards are flipped over and players have to find the match. Players can keep flipping cards until they have made a mistake. Then the mistake card stays flipped over and the turn goes to the player on the left. When the order is complete that player takes all the ingredient cards. Turn ingredients into the most burgers to win the game.

A collaboration between Daiso and the Kua 'Aina Sandwich shop in Hawaii/London/Japan.

Iconoclash: Battlegrounds  is a stand-alone expansion and features 4 new, original fighters for the Iconoclash system:


     Valiant Knight: Press-your-luck with every attack! The Valiant Knight commands an army of champions, and his attack deals more when he draws well from his Valiant Token Bag. But with every draw you risk the attack with a chance he may weaken his attacks or cause them to fail altogether.
     Amelia Fortress: Ready to take command of the fight? The undauntable commander of the 6th Armored Brigade will pulverize her opponents with her heavily armed mecha. She tends to pull off her greatest maneuvers when she's on the ropes and has only a handful of options.
     Monarch: Prepare to take flight and control the skies! Monarch readies her lance from above to deal extra damage. Monarch will use her magnificent wings to create great gusts and push everything on the board.
     Kali: Put on your uwu face! Kali commands an army of alien kittens that she uses to create an adorable minefield. Or she can collect them to create an explosion of kitten power.


In Iconoclash: Battlegrounds you’ll take the fight to the Final Battlefield and the Fallen Field where great battles in ages past have been fought.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Trivia game about one of Finland's major cities, Turku. The game has 936 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about Finland's former capital, Turku.

The name of the game refers to the old saying in the Turku dialect meaning "This side of the river" (as opposed to "The other side of the river") and refers to the river Aura which splits the city in two, into "old" and "new" parts. "This side of the river" is the old part with the cathedral, old city halls and market square etc., while the actual city center is now in the new part "on the other side".

The game is basically the Turku edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki.

Bees  vs. Bears is a strategy card game in which you play as a hive of bees, defending your honey against the horde of hungry bears. Each player starts with two honey tokens and tries to outlast the other hives. During the game you use special abilities as well as your army of  bee defenders, attempting to simultaneously fend of the bears and  weaken the other hives!

Bees vs. Bears is a high action, fast paced game with games averaging 10 - 20 minutes. It can be played with 2-6 people and though the game is easy to pick up, it is hard to master as there are sixty different cards, some with more complicated abilities than others.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Darkness Within: Age of Fantasy – Narrative Campaign
description:
Darkness Within is a narrative campaign for Age of Fantasy that follows the story of Misha, a young princess who must rise to the challenge and reclaim her throne after a vampire uprising brings darkness to the realm.

This game expansion for Age of Fantasy takes players on an adventure across 10 missions which are tied together by an exciting story of epic proportions. 
Rules for unit progression are also included, so you can watch your armies grow and evolve throughout the campaign.

Even though this narrative campaign was written with Humans and Vampiric Undead in mind, it is easy to imagine this type of clash to be happening between any of the other factions in the world, so feel free to swap them out with whatever armies you have available to play.

Note that to play this campaign you’re going to need the core rules for Age of Fantasy as well as some army lists, which you can find for free on onepagerules website.

A 3 player, 9-card game. At the beginning of the game, each player will draw 3 random cards from a deck of 9, and look at them. Each card is given a number of cubes that are placed on said card, these cubes serve as health, for example, kings have 6 health cubes, Queens have 5, Jacks have 4, and all other cards have 3. Each round, players have to place 1 or 2 cards of their choosing in their “card placing section”. Each time a player does this, they lose a piece of “health” on each of their placed cards (marked by moving the cube to resemble that its value has decreased by 1, ex: moving 6 to 5). After every turn, each player has to give one of their cards to the player in the clockwise direction. Once a card’s health reaches 0, the card is flipped over to show that it can’t be used anymore, it is then placed in the middle along with its health cube. Once a player’s entire deck is obsolete/can’t be used anymore, they’re out, and the last card standing wins.

Fyra is a card game about matching and stacking meant for 1-4 players who have, at a minimum, the ability to differentiate colors or printed patterns. You take turns matching up your cards with the ones already on the play field. The cards overlap but you must match the colors of at least one of the corners. If you can't find a spot for your cards, you get to place one next to the other cards, but have to take two replacements. Alternately, if you find a spot where you can match all four corners you don't have to take any replacement cards and get one step closer to going out.

The real fun comes in when you choose a team color and try to make sure as few of those squares are showing by the time you go out. Make alliances to spread one player's color all over the field and make their score worse while setting up spots where you can match up all your own corners the next time your turn comes around. There are 2 goals: be the first to get rid of all your cards and second, have the fewest squares of your color on the play field when you do.

You can also play a solo game trying to make the smallest pile of cards based on randomly selected hands of cards. A game with 4 players should last around 5 to 20 minutes.

-description from designer

Put on your chipmunk mask and transform into a cheerful woodland creature in Hide 'n Cheek! Players Hunt for acorns in this log-switching, nut-finding, cheek-stuffing game. Hide the Nuts, shuffle the logs, gather the acorns, and watch your chipmunk cheeks grow. Hid 'n Cheek brings home the feel of the forest in this nutty game of hunting and hoarding fun!

To play, hide 3 acorns under one or more of the 4 cups (depicted as logs) and then shuffle all the logs. The player on the left begins first and chooses one log to look under. Keep any acorns one might find and stuff them into your Chipmunk Mask cheek. Play continues with each successive player looking under the logs until all 3 acorns are found. A new round is then started with 3 more acorns.

Game ends when all 40 acorns are distributed. The player who collected the most acorns into their cheek is the Hide 'n Cheek champion!

YADOKARICK is a trick-taking game in which 1-7 players try to find the right size shell for their hermit crab from beach detritus.

The game consists of two suits, and in addition to a suit and rank, each card has a "shell size". During the game, the lowest card played to a trick will be able to adjust the target shell size up or down. The highest card of each color played to a trick will take the cards of that color with the largest size and add to their hermit crab. The bodies have a maximum size and may have a "roof" card added.

At the end of a hand, positive points are earned for total shell sizes less than or equal to the target, and points are lost for shells that exceed the size or for players who added too many cards to their shell. The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Robocalypse: A drinking game for f*cked up futurists
description:
Delve into critical and heated discussions about the future of artificial intelligence around the dinner table while having a drink (or five...)

Robocalypse is an AI-inspired, critical thinking, drinking game. It encourages ethical debate (heated discussion) and a critical examination of future technologies and societies. It's perfect for a rowdy dinner party, pre-drinks with rogue friends or intellectual discourse with your sober Sallys.

Robocalypse is simple, first you read a Judgement card aloud to your Council. This Judgement card contains a question which will be thought provoking, a little weird, and divisive. If you can’t all come to a consensus, pick up an Anarchy card which contains a mini drinking game, and let the beverage consuming commence. At the end of your round, the person in your council with the most f*cked up argument is given the Judgement card. Whoever is rewarded the most Judgement cards at the end of the game wins! Find out who shares your morals and who’s a little f*cked in the head. Maybe you’ll learn a little too...

We want to curate an experience for people to feel comfortable talking about AI, regardless of their technical ability or knowledge. We want to help discern the ethical complexities of machine learning to expose the myths and empower our players to critically evaluate and engage in its social implications in the most fun way possible! This game can also be played without the drinking element by removing the Anarchy cards.

—description from the designer

Simple set of fantasy miniature wargaming rules available for free.

Turn Sequence: 
Initiative Phase

description:
Far Distant Ships: a quick playing set of rules covering fleet actions in the age of sail where linear tactics were to the fore.

The aim of these rules, which draw upon techniques and concepts from LFG’s Cannon, Cross and Crescent  combined with a development span covering some 20 years has been to develop a quick play set that focusses on the command and control aspects of the battle, allowing large battles such as Chesapeake Bay, the Saintes and Trafalgar to be played out in an evening’s gaming. 
Signalling is key, and complex plans may fall apart if a vital signal is missed, or an order executed late.

The rules cover the main ship types involved across the period, focussing on ships of the line and rated frigates, in particular in their role as signal repeaters. 
Stats are also included for civilian ships, smaller warships and bomb vessels, and shore batteries and fortifications, allowing the full spectrum of naval operations to be played.

The rules have been written with 1/2400 models in mind but will work with models in larger scales.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Ninth Edition): War Zone Charadon – Act I: The Book of Rust
description:
Forge world Metalica is the furnace heart of war production in the Charadon Sector.

Upon the orders of Abaddon the Despoiler, Typhus leads a vast invasion into the Charadon heartlands, seeking to besiege Metalica and infect it with Nurgle's plagues. Beneath his foetid banner fight not only the forces of the Death Guard, but also warbands from the Black Legion, Word Bearers, Alpha Legion, and countless others. Alongside them come twisted Chaos Knights, endless tides of mutants, cultists, daemons, and even the towering abominations of the Legio Morbidus.

Yet against this invading horde stands an Imperial alliance between the armies of the Adeptus Mechanicus, the Adeptus Astartes, the Astra Militarum, and many more. Fighting to dominate the systems of the Lirac and Obolis Sub-sectors, these mighty forces unleash their deadliest weapons in a war that threatens to annihilate the very worlds over which they fight.

Inside this 96-page hardback book, you'll find:

- Riveting background that reveals the sheer magnitude of the threat descending upon the Charadon Sector. The thrilling narrative details the opening gambits and developing war fronts that will push the Imperial defenders to their limits and beyond.

- An in-depth campaign system you can use to join the war for the Obolis and Lirac Sub-sectors on the side of the Imperial defenders, Chaos invaders, or anarchic xenos raiders.

- A wealth of Crusade campaign content, including unique relics and advancements for your units to reflect your Crusade force's participation in this sprawling war.

- Rules and matched play content including Armies of Renown – exciting themed forces that provide a whole new way of mustering a Warhammer 40,000 collection – and codex supplements for the Adeptus Mechanicus, Imperial Knights, and Drukhari.

- Additional content, including all new Legendary Missions, which enable you to refight iconic missions that are key to the narrative of the story.

This hardback book also comes with a black 1/4" ribbon marker.

You will need a copy of the Warhammer 40,000 Core Book to use the contents of this book.

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Ninth Edition): Crusade Mission Pack – Plague Purge
description:
This mission pack is a narrative play expansion for the Warhammer 40,000 Crusade campaign system.

Within, you’ll find the rules required to play games of Warhammer 40,000 set in the Charadon Sector, which has become embroiled in war since the arrival of Typhus and the Death Guard at the head of a vast Chaos fleet. This mission pack has been specifically designed for use in narrative play Crusade games, where your campaigning forces can wage war across the Obolis and Lirac Sub-sectors. With plagues running amok and devastating new weaponry being brought to bear on every front, will your Crusade force rise to prominence or buckle beneath the weight of the terrible conflict?

Inside this 112-page mission pack you will find:

- Six new missions each for Combat Patrol-, Incursion-, Strike Force- and Onslaught-sized games, all set within the war-torn Charadon Sector
- A selection of new Plague Purge Agendas, Requisitions, Stratagems, and more

The pack itself is presented in a full Canadian wiro ring-bound book for easy use and reference on the tabletop during games, and comes with an elastic closure.

You will need a copy of the Warhammer 40,000 Core Book to use the contents of this mission pack.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Second Edition): Broken Realms – Teclis
description:
Archmage Teclis leads his Lumineth hosts against the Great Necromancer in a battle to save the realms. Nagash has brought a ghastly era of hauntings upon the Mortal Realms, for the power of death is on the ascendant. Only now, when the hour is darkest, does the light of the Lumineth shine forth. Teclis, aelven god of magic, leaves his beloved dominion of Hysh to make war upon the Ossiarch Empire – but in doing so triggers a counter-attack that may see his sovereign lands destroyed. Who will win this ultimate duel of arcane might?

The next engrossing instalment in the Broken Realms saga moves from Morathi to Archmage Teclis and his Lumineth Realm-lords as they launch a risky venture against the Great Necromancer. The unravelling story will have massive consequences for several factions including the Lumineth Realm-lords, Maggotkin of Nurgle, and forces of Death – once again shifting the balance of power in the Mortal Realms. You’ll find fresh ways to play your games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, alongside rules for new units, warscroll battalions, and more.

Inside this 152-page hardback book, you’ll find:
-The thrilling tale of the Lumineth Realm-lords’ perilous campaign against the entrenched legions of Death – illustrated with stunning new art.
- New rules for the Lumineth Realm-lords, including a bevy of units from the Hurakan Temple. This book includes the full set of rules and background for every newly-released unit, so can be used alongside the first edition of Battletome: Lumineth Realm-lords.
- Allegiance abilities for the Cities of Sigmar and Lumineth Realm-lords, representing Xintil, a Free City in Hysh, as well as the Great Nations of Alumnia and Helon.
- Rules for Streets of Death – battles in the tight confines of cities across the Mortal Realms.
- Six battleplans and Realms of Battle rules to help you recreate the events and locations from the story.
- A suite of warscroll battalions and rules to enhance your Cities of Sigmar, Maggotkin of Nurgle, Lumineth Realm-lords, Flesh-eater Courts, and Ossiarch Bonereapers.

Important Note:
If you already own a copy of the original Lumineth Realm-lords battletome, Broken Realms: Teclis gives you everything you need to fully update your Lumineth Realm-lords army, in addition to the groundbreaking new lore and artwork. The updated Battletome: Lumineth Realm-lords simply includes the Lumineth content from this book, which makes it a handy compilation if you prefer to keep it all together.

Economic game. Buy cooperatives and equities, collect rent from fellow players. Repeat as necessary until everyone else is bankrupt.

Players take turns in order. A turn begins with the rolling of the dice and moving the piece around the board the corresponding number of squares.

If a player lands on Fortune cell he draws the card from the pile and obeys its instructions. If the player stops on a cell with an unowned cooperative, he can buy the entity for the listed price. He can decline the purchase. In this case, the entity keeping available for the other players. If the cell with the entity already owned by another player, he must pay the owner's rent (the price will be noticed on the card based on the level of the cooperative entity). Also, a board contains the special cells for equities exchange. Each full round on the board the cost of equities change and player can sell them to increase own cash or to buy other equities by a lower price.

A competitive card game for 2-4 players played over a number of 6-card hands until one player achieves the required gold to win.

Each player is a deep sea diver trying to find one of many sunken Spanish Galleons which may or may not have gold. Each diver is dealt 6 "Depth" cards and given two single coin treasure cards. The "Depth" cards consist of two suits - Octopus and Anchor and are numbered from 1 to 16 (meters). There are also two wild cards which are equal to zero meters of depth and may be used in forming discount hands or to deceive your fellow divers as to how many total meters may be in your hand.

Through discarding sets of two or three cards, the diver lowers the number of total "meters" held in their hand. Once the total is below 8, the diver may opt to declare "diver down" and claim the top most Treasure card. But be careful, another diver may have a lower amount of meters in hand and declare "Diver Down Deep" and claim that treasure instead, as well as claiming a single gold coin treasure card from the original declarer.

First diver to obtain Treasure cards totaling 10 gold coins is declared the winner.

Afterglow is the hyperlight space skirmish wargame set in the far future. The players assume the role of STARCORE Commanders, a hybrid of human sentience and an alien supercomputer, and control a task force of hyper-sophisticated warships in objective-based battles.

Players design their task force of one to three ships using cards, then plot their actions against one another simultaneously. There is no randomization and every action pushes a game towards it's definitive conclusion. Every attack is potentially lethal and there is no armour. The only protection is for the ships to briefly travel back through time and space to a safe location: this is known as the Afterglow effect.

—description from the designer

The Biocraft may be engaged in one-off battles, or players can use the rules for the campaign where players maintain a persistent fleet of ships. description:
Players attempt to win chip pots based on lottery numbers turned over on the game board. Six green number cards are placed on the board, with the first three turned over. Players are then dealt cards from a red deck and ante chips under each of the cards on the board.

On their turn, a player may either play a card towards the board or draw one if they don’t have any numbers that match. Players must then discard one card at the end of their turn, regardless of whether they play a card or not. When a player can match the three number combination, they claim the pot under the third card, then turn over the fourth card. Play continues the same as before, with the fifth and sixth cards revealed once the previous pots are claimed. The player with the most chips at the end wins.

The object of the game is to get your Mr. Machine marker to the toy factory while avoiding hazards on the game board. Players move around the board by using various parts of a replica of the Mr. Machine toy that was sold around the same time. The first player to successfully get their Mr. Machine token to the toy factory is the winner.

Entrepreneurial tycoons in the year 3113 spend scant portions of their vast fortunes hiring unscrupulous scientists to bioengineer ferocious creatures to compete on a remote island in "Alpha", the world’s most brutal blood sport not banned by the International Broadcasting Memorandum. In this two-player hidden movement deck-building hand management game, each player will assume the actions of one dangerous predator that exists to achieve one objective: reign as the Alpha.

How do they do this? Players will choose seven action cards, each of which represents the passage of one hour of time, from their available deck to play that round. Players then take actions simultaneously, resolve them, and repeat the process until each player has one card remaining (*), which gets upgraded based on the player's current location on the board.

Player's creatures can Move around the map, Hunt the piddling local wildlife to avoid Howl penalties, and Track their opponent to ascertain their location. Players must also navigate un-upgradable Sleep and Scat actions that make it harder for them to stay undetected. Players who perform the final available action, Sneak Attack!, lay it all on the line. They either win the game, or let their opponent know exactly where to find them.

(*) Unless their opponent has located them, in which case, game over. Chin up...they've got that creature's DNA sequencing on file somewhere...
The players start their journey to various leisure activities around the board. The first player to reach space 90 wins the game.

Five explorers are trying to prevent the rising of the undersea kingdom of R’lyeh and the awakening of Great Cthulhu with it. To achieve their goal, the Disk of R’lyeh must be destroyed. The Alaska-class fell midway between a heavy cruiser and a battleship, and the United States Navy considered these vessels large cruisers rather than battlecruisers. 
They were designed as cruiser-killers, tasked with destroying post-Washington Treaty heavy cruisers. As a result, they were given 12-inch guns, significant armour protection, and could travel at up to 33 knots. They resembled current generation battleships in appearance, with the familiar two forward, one aft main battery turrets, massive columnar mast and a multitude of 5-inch dual purpose guns along the sides of the superstructure. However, they were built to cruiser standards, lacking the armoured belt and torpedo defence of capital ships.

Commissioned late into the war, USS Alaska saw limited service. 
Operations included those off Iwo Jima and Okinawa in early 1945, such as anti-aircraft defence in carrier task forces and shore bombardment. 
After the war, she participated in the Korean occupation. 
She was decommissioned in February 1947.

—description from the publisher

One of the largest battleships ever built. 
Based superficially on the South Dakota-class, the USS Iowa had heavier armour and was 200 ft. longer. It was also incredibly fast, due to increased output from the engines, and its greater size allowed it to mount more anti-aircraft guns, making it a formidable prospect for attacking planes. Invariably used as flagships for task forces, the Iowa-class was also supremely equipped to act as escorts for the fast carrier fleets of the Pacific.

During World War Two, USS Iowa transported President Franklin D. Roosevelt across the Atlantic to Algeria, bound for the conference with Winston Churchill and Joseph Stalin in 1943. 
Transferred to the Pacific fleet the following year, her operations including the preparatory shore bombardments at Kwajalein and Eniwetok and the screening of carriers around the Marshall Islands. 
She was present for the Japanese surrender in Tokyo Bay, serving as the flagship of the Third Fleet under Admiral William F. Halsey. 
She would go on to serve in the Korean War before being decommissioned into the “mothball fleet”. 
Reactivated in 1984 in efforts to counteract the expanding Soviet Navy she was finally decommissioned after 19 total years of active service in 1990. Today she serves as the USS Iowa Museum at the Port of Los Angeles.

—description from the publisher

USS Hornet was a Yorktown class-carrier serving in the Pacific Theatre during World War Two. 
She launched the Doolittle Raid (the first air operation to strike the Japanese Archipelago) on 18 April 1942. 
She later participated in the crucial Battle of Midway as well as the Buin-Faisi-Tonolai Raid. 
As part of the Solomon Islands campaign, USS Hornet was involved in both the capture and defence of Guadalcanal and the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands. 
It was during these latter operations that she was irreparably damaged by enemy aircraft. Abandoned by her crew, she was sunk by an encroaching force of Japanese Destroyers – her sinking marked the last US fleet carrier to have been sunk by enemy action.

Of the Yorktown-class aircraft carriers, built in a series of three, only the USS Enterprise survived the war, with the USS Yorktown sunk during the Battle of Midway. 
The USS Enterprise went on to become the most frequently decorated US carrier of the war, even earning the British Admiralty Pennant, the only ship outside of the Royal Navy to do so. 
She was finally put out of action in 1945 by a kamikaze attack that severely damaged the hanger deck.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Yokai Quest
Yokai Quest is a game where players lead a group of heroes who must face a multitude
of enemies and dangers.
The main enemies of the heroes are the Yokai, mythological creatures from Japanese
folklore. Each Yokai has its own distinctive traits and abilities, from the evil Oni, to the
funny Tengu, to the prankster Tanuki. But they are not the only enemies the heroes
must face, the world of Yokai hides a multitude of hidden dangers.
Throughout the campaign, the players will follow the plot of a story that will take them
through different scenarios, where they will have to face all kinds of challenges. After
each scenario, win or lose, the plot will continue until the story is complete.
Yokai quest has been designed with simplicity in mind, so that anyone, of any age group
and knowledge, can play yokai quest after just one reading of this rulebook.
But let’s not waste anymore time! We know you are itching to start, so let us go right
into the world of Yokai Quest.

Yokai Quest Strikes Back brings with it the following changes and improvement to the original Yokai Quest!

Major Upgrades:
1.  Major revision to the rulebook and Gameplay. We did not just fix the rulebook, we have now completely revised the game. Yokai Quest Strikes back difficulty level has been designed to be challenging for experienced players while at the same time a Little Heroes mode (Easy mode) has also been created for children and new players.
2. The original Yokai Quest had 16 scenarios. Yokai Quest Strikes Back brings you +20 scenarios in a separate, new +80 pages campaign book!

Gameplay Tweaks:

Strategy
    1. Hero movement : Movement is now an action. Heroes no longer have costless movement. Additionally, leaving a square adjacent to a yokai will now cost double. Players will now need to strategically plan their hero movement and placement.
    2. Friendly Fire : Powerful abilities have been tweaked - in general, heroes can no longer unleash a skill without a care in the world : swinging their sword at their allies will cause significant damage! The addition of friendly fire means that hero position, skill choice and energy expenditure decision is now even more strategic!
    3. Players may no longer swap items at any time during their phase. Swapping items from the inventory to the hero board can now only be done during the "take a breather" phase, at the end of a hero turn.
    4. Energy token management : Energy tokens can now be used to recharge a skill. This adds another level of strategy to the energy token management. Do you recharge a skill, or save the energy to unleash another skill?
    5. The concept of Line of Sight has been added to the game
    6. Terrain elements have been properly incorporated into the game. There are more strategic decisions to be made around these elements, such as Shrubs, Magic Barriers, altars and monoliths.

Spawn Rate and Yokai Difficulty level
    1. Yokai Spawn rate has been modified , there is now the normal mode (harder), and an easy mode.
    2. Additionally, each spawn point will now spawn a yokai each turn, unless specified otherwise by the scenario conditions
    3. Yokais attributes, attack and defense stats have been tweaked and modified.

Additional changes
    1. Heroes no longer receive treasure tokens for defeating a Yokai, unless specified by scenario conditions. Treasures must be earned!
    2. Skills or Abilities have been completely revamped. Each Hero now begins their adventure with a limited number of skills, and are able to purchase more powerful skills with the victory points (experience) they gained during their adventures.

This game is played like regular checkers except you press the Electric Spindicator when it is your turn. Where it stops determines how many spaces\players they can move:

"0" - player doesn't move - pass to next player
"1" - payer must move one checker one space diagonally forward
"2" - player must move either one checker two spaces or two checks one space each.
"3" - player must move either one checker three spaces, three checkers one space or one checker one space and one checker two spaces.

Continuous jumps are considered one move.

Moving backward and forward between two squares with a king is not permitted on a multiple move.

boardgame: Abomination: The Heir of Frankenstein – The Secret Cabal Promo Cards
description:
Promo pack of three new cards released for "The Secret Cabal Gaming Podcast is at Full Froth in 2020!" Kickstarter campaign.

Blue-Eyed Butcher (Scoundrel) - Even the pets aren't safe.
Moral Dilemma (Humanity) - 'Tis a dangerous game you play.
Understudy (Research) - Good help is hard to find.

-description from designer

A shrewd investor builds Greenhouses and Observatories to help her fund critical research. A young business tycoon stumbles upon the Telegraph, catapulting him ahead of his peers. A group of like minded individuals decide the new invention Dynamite might help them... level the playing field. In Farms & Factories: Inventions, everyone has a chance to change the world and maybe score a few points along the way.

In Farms & Factories: Inventions (as in Farms & Factories) players draw and play Action & Building cards from a communal deck until it runs out of cards. Inventions adds a new card type, Tech, which is shuffled into a separate deck at the beginning of the game. Unlike with Actions or Buildings, a player must patent a Tech before it can be played by discarding cards from their point pile. This means each Tech requires careful analysis to decide if it will pay off in the lost points. Tech cards are very powerful however, with lasting effects that can be used as soon as they are played, and often shape a player's strategy.

In additional to 30 unique Techs, Farms & Factories: Inventions adds 35 new Buildings and Actions, adding complexity and strategical depth to the original game.

-description from publisher

Cardopolis is a solo or cooperative game where you as mayor (or co-mayor) are responsible for guiding the growth of your city to be the biggest, best city possible while protecting it from disasters!

The game is played in two Stages: 
- Town stage
- City stage
During each stage, you will draw cards, play zone and building cards to grow your city, and resolve disasters.

The goal is to build your city to have the greatest Population by the end of the City stage.

-description from designer

The McClains are one of the oldest and richest families in Valley Falls. So when the current patriarch of the family, Edison McClain, asks for your help and is willing to make it worth your time, how can you say no? Edison was planning on naming his heir and the next head of the family. So he called together all of the McClains and proposed a game as a last test to see the worthiness of those he deemed had potential. But someone in the family had other ideas. During the family get-together, the McClain’s oldest and most prized possession, a medallion bearing the household’s coat of arms, was stolen. This 500-year-old family heirloom is believed by the family to be the reason for their wealth and success throughout the years. Without the medallion Edison can’t name his heir, and he fears the family will fall into ruin. Will you be able to find the thief and the medallion? Can you recover The Inheritance?

Difficulty Level - 7/10

Allison Zeller has a secret admirer. She thinks the mysterious guy is sweet enough at first until his behavior escalates past simple admiration and spirals into full-blown obsession. Someone is stalking her and growing impatient that they can't have her. This infatuation culminates in a full-blown attack on Allison's life, leaving her at death's door. It's likely that whoever did this to her will come back to finish what they started. Can you find out the identity of Allison's stalker before it's too late? Can you unmask The Watcher?

An abstract strategy game for 2 players.

Each player has 4 tiles - 1 of each isogram.  These are placed on their end of the board matching the isogram and the direction its facing (orientation).  Using the roulette wheel, pieces are moved and rotated per the wheel's diagrams.

The goal is for one player to land their isograms on the  matching squares (color and orientation) of the opponent's baseline (or his matching tile piece) and remove their own piece from the board.  The first player to remove their four pieces from the board wins.

In Prime Ordeal, two or more players take turns drafting armies from a "Spawning Pool" (a face-up collection of Creature and Bonus cards that all players have access to) to get ready for battle. Each creature has a Power Level, represented by a single number. These numbers are added together to form a player's Total Power Level when battling. As the game progresses, so does each player's army and Total Power Level.

To draft (referred to as "Create" in game), players discard combinations of Prime cards from their hands into the Prime card discard pile. Each Creature or Bonus card features a Creation Cost, specifying the combination of Prime cards necessary to create it. The Creation Cost may feature any combination of the following Prime card types: Bug, Sea, Bird, Reptile, Mammal, and Amphibian. Creatures themselves have types that directly correspond to their Creation Cost.  Players are encouraged to collect creatures that share types to increase synergy and for better chances at acquiring Bonus cards.

After each player takes a turn Creating, the round concludes with a mandatory, free-for-all style battle. Each player's Power Level is compared and those with the highest or tied for highest Power Level win the battle, while each other player loses a Life.

Players draft in between each battle and the game continues until only one player remains with any Life. The amount of Life chosen before the game will determine how long a game lasts. It is recommended to start players at 7 (short game), 11 (medium-length game), or 13 Life (long game).

This strategic, card-based brawler features a modular design.  The core set, Genesis, functions as a standalone game.  Players may purchase expansions and shuffle them directly into the core set to change the game entirely. Groups may decide on which expansions to include and which to omit for a customizable gaming experience. Prime Ordeal is traditionally played as a free-for-all; however, its design accommodates team play as well.

-description from publisher

The game is designed to help familiarize one with the multiplication table. The objective of the game is to cover numbers on a circular board with their own markers. First player to do so wins the game.

—user summary

A traffic simulation game to teach road rules to new drivers.

Each player takes a pair of cars and moves them by rolling dice.  Road hazards are introduced at numbered squares.  Hazards are played out and fines assigned and paid at court.
Event cards give and remove points. The player with the most points wins.

Horn of Plenty  is a trick-taking card game in which four players win tricks by matching colors on cards and also gain generosity tokens by being nice to the other players.

Information and resources about this game: https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/2177937

There was a devasted planet called Earth.
Once were habitated by human beings, and not only: then they came.
Pugs. All over it!

Planet of the Pugs is a cooperative game for 2 up to 6 survivors, running time about 20'-30', 8+.

During their turn, players can play up 2 action (a mandatory one + an optional one) then the Pugs run their cards, to determine Devastation.

Mandatory Action: 
- Draw 2 cards

Optional Actions: 
- Play 1 card from the hand
- Block Devastation – discard 4 copies of the same object to turn down by 1 level the Devastation and take a Special Relic
- Switch Object with other player

World Action:
- Draw many Devastation cards, looking to the players’ cards and to reached level.

Devastation bar has 12 levels and 4 steps.
Each step add a difficulty level like not to talk at all or play cards from other player hand.
The more Devastation bar increase the more World Action will increase too.

Three Ending Game Condition
• SURVIVE: the players get up to 5 Special Relics to create the Final Defense!
• DEATH: a player lose 3 life points
• DEVASTATION: the Devastation Counter reach the Max level

A 2-player word game using the same mechanics as Mastermind.

The Wordmaker (player 1) creates a word at the top of the board and hides it with a screen. The Wordbreaker (player 2) tries to guess the word by placing Yellow pegs in the board to indicate the letter and its position in the word. The Wordmaker than replaces the Yellow pegs with Black or White Pegs to indicate if the correct letter comes before or after the guessed letter.  If the letter is guessed correctly, the Yellow peg stays on the board.

Points are scored for the number of tries it takes to guess the word.  The player with the lowest total score of all rounds wins.

Start Your Engines! Now you can hold your own series on desktops everywhere!

Players decide on the number of laps they want to complete, and place their car in the lane of their choice. On their turn turn, they spin the spinner to either move their car the number of spaces indicated, or follow the directions of the black flag space indicated. Whoever completes the determined number of laps first wins.

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Completionist Guise Promo Card
description:
A promo card for the game Sentinels of the of the Multiverse. It was available with Ultimate Collector's Case and is also included in Complete Hero Variant Collection.

Completionist Guise

Innate Power: "Impressive Collection" - Replace a hero character cards in play with a variant of that hero. Put the replaced cards safely under this card. Either use the power on the replaced card (treating any hero names on that card as "Guise") or Guise deals 1 target 2 psychic damage.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The new, fast-paced card game that combines wordplay, trivia and a dash of deception!

Each round begins with a player selecting a card which will give them a secret word. They must then reveal the first letter of the word to the group.

Players then begin thinking of words beginning with the revealed letter. Anything goes! The aim is to come up with clues that other players will be able to SYNK with you without the cardholder figuring it out.  When you've thought of your SYNK! word propose the definition to all players. If another player thinks they know you lock eyes, countdown from 3 and... SYNK! If the two players guess correctly then the next letter of the secret word is revealed. Now all players need to think of a word that starts with the first AND SECOND letter of the secret word. However if the cardholder also guessed the SYNK! word then it's back to square one. The game continues until a player thinks they know the secret word.

If a round comes to a halt with players struggling to figure out the secret word, then the cardholder must reveal its definition. The first player to think they know the answer must then shout “SYNK!”. If a player is able to either correctly SYNK! the secret word with another player or guess the word from the definition they win the round. If players SYNK! or guess incorrectly the cardholder wins. First player to win three cards is the victor and gets full bragging rights - until the next game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Dark Watch The Harpy Promo Card
description:
These is a variant card for The Harpy (it has different HP/Power from the other card).

HP 27

Power: Refocus

Flip one control token. You may play a card.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Trivia game about one of Finland's cities, Tampere. The game has 900 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about Tampere.

The name of the game means "See you at the rapids" and refers to the Tammerkoski rapids that run straight through the city (although now largely covered/developed) from lake Näsijärvi - itself one of the largest lakes in Finland and an important means of communication and trade before the advent of railways and the motor car - to lake Pyhäjärvi, and which the city was originally developed around; the rapids provided a ready source of power for the weaving mills, paper mills, engineering works etc. of the emerging industrial revolution of the 19th century and started Tampere on the path to becoming what it now is.

The game is basically the Tampere edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki. Unlike that version however, this is not completely bilingual; only 300 of the 900 questions have been translated to Swedish and there's a separate deck for those cards, furthermore the cards are not categorized but simply have the "easy" or "difficult" option on them.

In Mist Fleet, each player leads a battleship fleet. The object of the game is destroy all of your opponent's ships.
In each turn player fire cannon to opponent's fleet, opponent announce hit or miss, but they can lie.But this game is also suitable for those are not good at lying, because all lying ablity is given by ship itself.

Each player has 6 ships and 6 empty sea cards,place their 12 cards face down to form a 4x3 formation
Each player on his turn has 2 actions.
For each action, player has 3 options
1) Attack directly: attack 1 of opponent's card. The opponent look at his card. If it is a ship, he say “Hit”, if it is not a ship, he say “Miss”. Put “Hit” or “Miss” token on that card.
2）Attack adjacently: attack a space between two cards. The opponent look at both his cards. If either of them is a ship, he say “Hit”; if neither of them is a ship, he say “Miss”. Place “Hit” or “Miss” token between margin of two cards.
Whenever a ship's life point is equal of below 0 (life points on card minus “Hit” on the ship card and around the ship card), the ship is sunk. Ship's owner shall flip the card face up.
3) Radar scan. Scan one cross point of two margin lines. Opponent look at his cards around scan point.Opponent count all this cards current life point and say that number. Put a radar scan number token on that point.

Basic game is for 2 players. Support up to 4 players by using two copies of game.

在《雾海舰队》中，玩家将带领自己的舰队，在迷雾之海中与对手决战。玩家可以通过炮击或雷达扫描的方式，找出并摧毁对手的舰队。最先击毁敌方所有战舰的玩家获胜。

FAD is a 2 player card game that puts you in the heart of gameplay. Score points by doing 'Activities' with your 'Friends', while avoiding any 'Delays'. The first to 100 points wins. The game is available as a print & play product.

Design your own character from a series of pre-made faces (like in a computer game RPG) to bring yourself into the game.

The game is a really fun, super easy to learn, card game with strategy and a little bit of luck.

There are 120 cards including:

x60 Friends cards
x30 Activity cards
x30 Delay card

How do you make a game about the great city of Lakeland, FL? While it's impossible to include everything, we jammed all the LAKELAND we could into this box. It's full of family fun, outdoor activities,  Florida history and of course... lakes, a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience Swan City? Here's a quick idea of how to play! Buy your favorite LAKELAND properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and parking fines and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun! Get your game on! So choose your token and advance to GO LAKELAND! You may soon be the Mayor...or out of the game for eating too much pizza at the Silver Moon Snack Bar.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
The Talisman Pocket Game

Comes in a paper bag with illustration and title on the front and rules of the game for the war games on the back; The bag is 22 x 14 cm.

The rules describe two simple games, the Target Flight Game and the Capture Ships game.

Contents: 

     4 cardboard cards with the color-illustrated game boards for the Target Flight game.
     8 game boards (2 per card) on the back of the Target Flight Game for the Capture Ships game. I believe the original game came with a small pencil.

This game will transport you into the magic and mystery of the medieval era. Choose your character, move with cunning among the rooms of the Castle and meet as many characters as possible. Let them help you find out who is hiding in the royal rooms and especially in the Princess’s room! Your Powers, together with those of the Special Cards, can benefit you but don’t let them escape and use them at the right moment… An engaging strategy game for all ages, with simple cards that will turn into squares to go through, recreating a mystery and an ever-changing story for each game. '

—description from the designer

In 16th Avenue players try to make it in the country music industry of the 1980s. The board spaces players move through are actual locations in the Nashville area.

Players start as struggling musicians and have to collect four items (e.g. an agent and a publishing contract) to establish a career, at which point they have to collect things such as a US tour and a gold album, etc.; there are four levels of success in all.

The game was self-published under the name "Grabbitt & Hyde."

Owonikoko is played over seven rounds in which all players are engaged in the money hustle, striving to be the richest at the end of the game.

On active player’s turn the following actions are possible, however only 3 actions are permitted on active turn. This can be a combination of any action here or a repetition of one totaling 3 actions.
1.    Draw card: player draws a card from the top of the draw deck
2.    Play an action card (A.C): player plays an action card from hand and resolves said action
3.    Play a counteraction card (C.C): player plays a counteraction card to stop or effect current active action
4.    Sell goods: play goods card and gain gold accordingly

When not the active player, only one action is possible and permitted during other players turn
1.    Play a counteraction card: play a C.C card to cancel effect of an active card, however this can only be done once while that player is active.

End of Game:
At the end of seven rounds, all players reveal their gold and richest player wins. If players tie, player with most goods win, if not they share victory.

-description from designer

EXCITING GAME OF QUESTIONS – Ask up to 10 questions to guess the state on the Game Card! Does this state have a lot of beaches? Are the Red Sox from this state? Be the first player to win 7 game cards!

ABOUT THE GAME – This game is all about the 50 states of America! Learn all about what these states are known for, their characteristics, how they were named, & more in this super fun game of guessing! This game is for 2-6 Players.

WHAT’S INSIDE – Includes 50 Game Cards and 6 Clue Cards.

—description from the publisher

A fun race in which each player represents an animal, but no player knows who is what. Walking with all the animals is a way to confuse the players. The first three to cross the finish line receive medals, but only the player with the most gold medals is the winner. Come on, start the race and don't let them know you're winning.

Each player secretly draws an animal that they must represent and receive three cards.

On their turn, each player must choose one of the cards in his hand to discard and walk the same number of spaces indicated on the card, with the same animal drawn on it. Then he draws a card to replace his hand.

Special cards are used to bring new dynamics to the game.
The player whose animal crosses the finish line first will win.

boardgame: Brutal Quest: Fantasy Narrative Skirmish Gaming Based on the Planet 28 Rules
description:
Brutal Quest is a fantasy narrative skirmish game, built on the Planet 28 rules engine.

Brutal quest is designed to provide you with a set of simple wargames rules, allowing for fast and exciting games of miniature combat with warbands of kitbashed characters.

These rules can also be expanded with the in-depth campaign and narrative systems included, allowing you to add roleplay sessions, individual warband goals and long form story archs to your battles.

This book contains all the rules you need to play a game of brutal quest including custom character creation and advice on running campaigns.

Also included are a set of paper miniatures to allow you to get playing asap.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Berlin'84: A Cold War 3! Skirmish Level Wargame in Berlin
description:
BERLIN ’84: Skirmish Rules for Cold War3!

August 1984, after tensions rise the unthinkable has happened! 
World War III has started with the Soviet invasion of West Germany. 
Berlin is surrounded and left to it’s fate with no possibility of being relieved or replenished, the units stationed in Berlin are in the fight of their lives as they are attacked on all sides by the East German Grenztruppen (Border Guards). 
Desperate city fighting ensues.

Take command of either British or East German forces in street to street fighting in Berlin. 
Each model represents one model and each side fields sections, with a platoon being the largest force fielded (though a company is do-able just rather large and may require more than two players!). 
Each model can move and fight, with a simple wounding system.
Tanks are formidable beasts but can still be brought to their knees by rocket launchers and skill full tactics.

This title is fully compatible with ALL other titles produced by Rooksdown Games and written by Dean Wilson; so any title can be used in conjunction with this book, bringing your games of WWIII to a new level.

SIX (6) unique city fight scenarios are included as well as a full army lists for the (UK) Berlin Brigade including stats for the FV432/30 with 30mm Fox A/C Turret and for the East German Grenz. Regt. With BTR-152’s, T-55am’s and UAZ jeeps with RPG-11 recoilless rifles.

-description from the publisher

Pays de Vitré-Fougères is a local board game based on the principle of questions and answers.

Players start in one of the town centers of the Vitré-Fougères, and then go discover all the sectors with 550 questions relating to geography, history, local celebrities....

A rock-paper-scissors card game with superheroes theme. Every player will be dealt a number of cards and they need to challenge other players during the game to keep their cards. 
The superheroes cards correspond to three different elements: water, fire  or woods, each one beats another one.

The player will the challenge card will challenge any other player. Both cards will take one card from their hands and at the shout of "Water, Fire, Wood!" they will reveal their cards. The stronger elements winds and the winner will gets both cards (unless there is a Power Eraser, that can be only used once). If both cards are identical, they are discarded.

The last player still having cards in their hands will be the winner of the game.

-description from designer

This game is a trivia game in the paper press world. It has no relation to the game Scoop from 1953.

The aim of the game it to become editor in chief of one of the ten newspaper of the game To achieve this, the player must create a team of journalists in a newspaper  to become a section editor and then editor in chief.

At the beginning of the game a player is the advisor and place its pawn in the middle of the board. The first player start the roulette on the board, when its stops, he adjusts the board to be able to enter into a newspaper building. He will then have to answer to a question thanks to a combination of numbers on the board. If he answers right, he places a pawn of his color in the newspaper. He has begun his journalist team. If his answer his wring, he becomes the advisor. The others player can try to answer to the question during the hourglass time. The first that answer become a journalist in the newspaper.

Once all players are journalists in the same newspaper they can proceed the same to access to the chief of section rank. When a player is chief of section he proceeds the same to become editor in chief.

The first to be editor in chief wins.
