Steinmannli consists of 4 different game variants

1. Solitaire
One player builds a tower of stones as high as possible.

2. Contest
2 to 4 players receive an equal amount of stones and build one tower by adding one stone after the other on a socle. Stones tumbling down must be collected by the player who caused the tumbling. The first player to get rid of all stones wins the game.

3. Clean up
All stones are placed on a wooden board that is placed on a socle. 2 to 4 players remove the stones one by one until the board tumbles down or the last stone has been collected. The player with tho most stones collected wins the game.

4. Seesaw
A small wooden board is placed on a socle. 2 players receive an equal amount of stones and place them one by one on the small board. The player who makes the board tumble down, loses the game.
Each of the two players tries to cover his 9 target squares with tokens (16x2 in four colours and shapes) in such a way that a colour and a shape occur only once in each horizontal and vertical row.

One player draws a card with an object pictured on the card. The other players may ask questions to the object answered by the card holder with "yes" or "no". If none of the players qot the object after 5 questions, the card holder scores one point, otherwise the player with the correct guess.

Panzerschiff: Expansion Set & Cruiser Module is an add-on for Panzerschiff 3rd ed. (available separately).  
You must own Panzerschiff 2nd or 3rd ed. to use this set.  Panzerschiff is an introductory-level tactical WW2 naval wargame for two players.  
Famous battleships, battlecruisers, pocket battleships, heavy cruisers, and light cruisers from eight nations are maneuvered into combat, and fire their guns (or launch torpedoes) to sink enemy ships.

Panzerschiff Expansion & Cruiser Module Set contains both of these add-ons, in one package:

     Panzerschiff Expansion comes with booklet with rule additions, set of 96 new warships and ship logs, and Reference Card.  
     Panzerschiff Cruiser Module comes with booklet with rules additions, set of 96 heavy and light cruisers and ship logs, and Reference Card. 



The game uses a system based on the system used in Avalon Hill's Bismarck (1st ed.), except with additions and refinements.  
You may replay historical or design-your-own WW2 scenarios.

Warships are rated in a variety of categories:  bow main guns, stern main guns, port & starboard secondary guns, speed, armor, engineering, fire control, vulnerability to plunging fire, and more.  
Players can look at a ship counter and its log sheet, and immediately determine its strengths and weaknesses.

The Panzerschiff game system is low complexity, and will appeal to novices or younger players, as well as to naval game vets who opt for the advanced rules that emphasize fun as well as authenticity.

With the standard game and this set, you will have all the significant warships of WW2 to play with. This new set is now available in PDF format.

WARSHIPS INCLUDED IN PANZERSCHIFF EXPANSION (48)

     GERMANY (5): Graf Spee, Admiral Scheer, Lutzow, Prinz Eugen. Admiral Hipper.
     GREAT BRITAIN (15): Anson, Howe, Duke of York, Nelson, Repulse, Warspite, Barham, Malaya, Valiant, Resolution, Ramillies, Revenge, Royal Oak, Kent, Cumberland.
     FRANCE (2): Suffren, Algerie.
     ITALY (3): Duilio, Trento, Trieste.
     UNITED STATES (12): Maryland, West Virginia, New Jersey, Wisconsin, Washington, South Dakota, Indiana, Massachusetts, Alabama, Guam, Baltimore, Wichita.
     JAPAN (6): Mutsu, Haruna, Hiei, Kirishima, Kako, Aoba.
     USSR (3):  Arkhanelsk, Kirov, Voroshilov.


WARSHIPS INCLUDED IN PANZERSCHIFF CRUISER MODULE (48)

     GERMANY (6): Blucher, Nurnberg, Koln, Konigsberg, Karlsruhe, Leipzig.
     GREAT BRITAIN (11): Exeter, York, Norfolk, Dorsetshire, Ajax, Achilles, Leander, Neptune, Orion, Belfast, Edinburgh.
     FRANCE (7): Colbert, Dupleix, Foch, Gloire, Montcalm, Galissonniere, Marseillaise.
     ITALY (6): Zara, Gorizia, Fiume, Pola, Garibaldi, Abruzzi.
     UNITED STATES (8): Boston, Quincy, Canberra, Pittsburgh, Cleveland, Denver, Santa Fe, Montpelier.
     JAPAN (4): Kinugasa, Mogami, Kumano, Suzuya.
     USSR (4):  Gorki, Molotov, Kalinin, Kaganovich.
     NETHERLANDS (2):  Tromp, Van Heemskerck.


—description from the publisher
Place numbered point tokens (1-5) in such a way that many victory points are scored.

Educational game for family

A double word is one that consists of two separate words making up one word. Clues, riddles really, are on each card. This is read out. The first player to call out the double word, wins a counter and the first to win 5 counters wins the game.

Example of a double word
One word = Bild (German term for picture)
Other word = Schirm (German term for umbrella)
Together they make Bildschirm (German term for screen)

Quidditch Tryouts! Match colours and slam your Quaffle cards on the Goalpost cards. You’ll need to think fast and score faster to win! The player with the highest score when all Goalpost cards run out becomes the new Gryffindor player. Double the fun: catch the snitch first when he comes by and score extra points for your team.

BoxOff is a solo abstract strategy game. It rises above the level of a mere puzzle because of its random set-up and user-defined board size.  The random set-up makes it like a playing-card solitaire game.

The game is played on a rectangular board of any size, as long as it has an even number of squares.  7x8, 8x12, and 12x15 are common sizes.  The board is filled with stones or marbles in various colors.  For an easy game, use 3 colors.  For a game of medium difficulty, use 4 colors.  For a difficult game use 5 colors. There must be an even number of each color, because they're going to be removed in pairs.  Typically you would use an equal number of each color.

A pair of stones of the same color may be removed from the board if the pair define opposite corners of a rectangle that contains no other stones.  They could be in a line (a 1xn rectangle) or diagonal corners of a non-linear rectangle.  The goal is to remove all the stones.

This game was first described in Games Magazine in 2013, and later in the Abstract Games magazine in 2020.  There have been electronic versions for iOS and Android.  BoxOff is now being published by NewVenture Games in three versions: a board game, peg game, and travel-size.

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope II: Cabaret in Lapin Blanc
description:
This is the second game in the series of "Scarlet Envelope" games.

This tabletop escape room mystery takes place in Paris, 1899. Colette, a gorgeous cabaret dancer, disappears. Her dressing room is full of confusing leads: among the jewelry and love letters, you find a threatening note and some truly unexpected things from the past. Who is she..? Find Colette and reveal her dark secret!

GAME CONTENTS:
- Cryptic Tarot Card with a secret
- Photo from Colette’s dressing room
- A piece of 19th-century newspaper
- Legal-size page from the ancient “Encyclopedia of Poisons”
- Threatening letter puzzle
- Love letters
- Cryptic postcard
- Digital lock you unlock online
- Website pages, videos
- Online hints in case you're stuck

Over ten various puzzles to solve, from ciphers to altering items in the most unpredictable ways. Answer submission and a storytelling video at the end. Tons of quality time in the middle!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope III: Distress Call from Outer Space
description:
The third game in the Scarlet Envelope series.

If you love detectives and sci-fi, this tabletop escape room by Scarlet Envelope is for you. The year is 2220, a century into human colonization of Mars. Your spaceship picks up the distress call from a Martian spy who has discovered a dark secret that can stop the war between Earth and Mars! Now, encrypted Martian files are in your hands, along with the fate of our Galaxy. Will you accept the challenge?

GAME CONTENTS:
- A huge space map (you’ll be surprised that it fits into the envelope!)
- Constellations map
- A cryptic ruler (to be used together with a map)
- Piece of evidence on a transparent film
- Audio SOS-signal from the Martian spy
- Spaceship Main Control interactive terminal (use commands to access info & additional files)
- “Earth Today” web news portal
- Two more hidden websites
- Two more hidden digital files
- Email correspondence with the United Planets Secretary-General

A fun detective investigation narrative with over a dozen of various puzzles to solve, from encryption to altering items. You will need a computer or a mobile device to play. Expect a lot of digital interaction (it’s the 23rd century after all.) Answer submission and a storytelling video at the end. Tons of quality time in the middle!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope IV: Breakfast for a Serial Killer
description:
This is the fourth game in the series of "Scarlet Envelope" games.

“The death of Janice Ward, a young mother of two and a waitress at Stanley’s Diner, is being investigated in connection to similar cases in the state...” In this murder mystery game, you work as a cop in the fifties. Bring justice to the victims of a serial killer who clearly has a thing for junk food and pretty waitresses! Expect an escape room in an envelope full of case files, evidence bags, coroner’s reports, and lots of digital interaction. You will unlock audio-interviews and video footage of suspects’ interrogations (with real actors!), and if you solve all puzzles and connect all dots, you will catch the killer yourself!

GAME CONTENTS:
- Videos of suspects’ interrogations
- Audio interviews with witnesses
- Coroner’s report
- Investigation report
- Vintage Diner’s menu
- Cryptic notes left by the serial killer
- Three plastic evidence bags with extra items to investigate
- Website pages with digital items

This game really puts your detective skills to the test! The gameplay is full of puzzles but is focused on the investigation. All the puzzles are crucial to access locked items and gather enough information to put the right suspect behind the bars. Roughly 2 hours of gameplay. Two levels of difficulty available.


     Parental Advisory: Mild Content (chilling but no bloody visuals.)


This game is a part of the Scarlet Envelope series of games. You can play them all in any order.

-description from publisher

Help the Princess is a board game where players  play a classic role of an RPG world character. The King is desperate and relies on a company of heroes to get his daughters in danger safely back to the castle. it is a competitive game but during the battles the hero can ask for help from the companions.

Help the Princess is a competitive game, but during the battles heroes can ask for help from the companions. Every face of the board is a new mission which can be played in a single game or in campaign mode.  The game is for 1-4 player. Players will have to decide if and when to fight the monsters that plague the various villages along the route, when to buy cards from the village market and when use potions or evocations.  Fighting,(with combat dice) summoning and buying cards at the best price will increase your experience points, while not fighting will make you advance on the path but without gaining any experience. In the game even the daughters of the king, the princesses are eager to help you in battle ... they love to use their long sword!

The player who brings the princess to safety and who has the most experience points wins.

Remember: you help the Princess, but without the Princess help you can't win!

Can you imagine a princess with a broadsword?

—description from the designer

Mindwalkers is a social deduction game in which players have the powerful ability to hijack the minds of their opponents, forcing them to do their bidding.

The game takes place over five days and four nights. During the day, players travel around the magical city of Elysia to work towards one of two main objectives: accumulating energy at The Origin, or banishing opponents from the council. Both are accomplished through voting, so securing the trust and cooperation of other players is key to victory. Players can also draw and play spells or magical artifacts to gain a strategic advantage.

At night, players further their agendas by Mindwalking one of their opponents. Mindwalks are issued by passing written commands to the victim, hidden from their opponents' view. These commands are open ended and completely up to the player's imagination!

The game ends when a player or faction completes their hidden win condition. Will you be the one pulling the strings over the council, or will you end up dancing to someone else's tune? Control your opponents. Control destiny.

In Curry the Favor, players take on the role of two friends that take turns buying each other lunch specials at a local restaurant. However, they always want to impress the other and give a better meal than the one that was last given to them.

Every turn in Curry the Favor, you will buy your friend a meal, trying to “one-up” the meal they last bought for you - in some aspect. But, you will want to spend your money wisely, because the player who received the most valuable meals in the end wins.

—description from the designer

Kit is an easy to learn game and fun for the avid card player or if you're a beginner. Rummy style game where you make kits or runs, make the right combinations, and be the first to go out - then watch your opponents scramble to cut their losses. The goal of the game is to be the player with the lowest score at the end of the 10th round by making your entire hand into kits and/or runs.

The game consists of two 44-card decks. Each deck has four WILD cards and four colors: Blue, Gold, Green, and Purple. Each color has ten cards, 3 through 12. Wild cards can be considered any number or color that you wish. There are also rotating wild cards that change every round. The rotating wild cards depend on the number of cards dealt that round. When 3 cards are dealt, the 3’s are wild, when 4 cards are dealt the 4’s are wild and so on until the last round when the 12’s are wild.

A kit is three or more cards of the same number and can be any color. You can have as many wild cards in a kit as you want. A run is three or more consecutive cards of the same color. A wild card can replace any card in a run. You can have as many wild cards in a run as you want and they can be adjacent to one another.

Dealing: 
1. Shuffle both decks of cards together. 
2. For the first round deal 3 cards to each player, dealing one card at a time. Note: For each round of play, the number of cards dealt increases by one card. In the second round 4 cards are dealt, in the third round 5 cards are dealt, and so on until dealing 12 cards to each player in the final round. The dealer position rotates after each round. 
3. Place the remaining cards in the center of the table and turn over the top card. The stack of cards is the draw pile and the card turned up is the start of the discard pile.

Playing: 
1. The player to the left of the dealer goes first and play continues clockwise. 
2. Start your turn by taking the top card on the discard pile or drawing a card. 
3. Complete your turn by discarding one card. 
4. Continue to make kits and/or runs until you are able to go out. 
5. Going out: Once all of the cards in your hand are part of a kit or run, lay down all of your cards face up on the table before discarding your last card. 
Note: you must be able to make kits and runs from the exact number of cards dealt in the round. You must have one card left to discard. You can go out on your very first turn. 
6. Once a player has gone out, all other players have one final turn. Start your turn as normal and after discarding, lay down your kits and runs. Any cards that do not fit in a kit or run will count toward your score. 
7. The cards that are in kits and runs do not count toward your score. Players add up only their unused cards. The card values are equal to the number on the card and Wild cards are worth 20 points. The player that goes out first gets a score of -5. The scorekeeper records and adds up the score for each player. 
8. The player to the dealer’s left shuffles all of the cards and deals the next round. 
Note: Increase the number of cards dealt by one card and keep track of the rotating Wild cards

-description from designer

boardgame: New Phone, Who Dis?: Family Edition
description:
Players rotate drawing a sent (or “inbox”) card while the rest of the group competes to play the funniest “reply” card. Once everyone has played their chosen reply card, the rotating judge awards a point to the funniest one. The player with the most points at the end of the round wins.

Includes: 240 family-friendly Inbox cards, 300 family-friendly Reply cards, and instructions.

boardgame: Tremble, Humans! Card Game: Spiel 2018 Promo cards
description:
A set of four convention promo cards:

Essen - Reward: 5, Industrial Centre / Europe

Nuremberg - Reward: 5, Scientific Centre / Europe

Columbus - Reward: 5, Scientific Centre / North America

Indianapolis - Reward: 5, Cultural Centre / North America

A chaotic tactical family game. 2 to 4 players do not try to throw their pieces into the box, as they end up getting them out of it again under difficult conditions.

The game is played in 3 rounds of 2 phases.

Each player picks stones of a single color. In the first phase, players stand around the box and try to throw everyone else's  stones in the box. This phase ends when all the stones of one player/color are in the box. Players get a point for each of their stones in the box.

At the start of the second phase, someone chooses a handicap card and everybody tries to pick their stones out of the box with this handicap at the same time. This round ends when one person has removed all their stones from the box.  All the other players get 1 point per stone left in the box.

After 3 rounds, the game ends and the winner is the player with the least total points.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The game contains card with luggage items printed on them.

Players draw cards and try to name the items depicted in the order in which they were drawn.  When a player makes a mistake, they're out of the game and have lost.  The last player who's still in the game, when all other players have lost, is the winner.

The game-box contains 36 card, divided as follows:

     3 Cards with the rules, in Dutch, English and French.
     6 Cards with items, with the background color begin yellow.
     6 Cards with items, with the background color being orange.
     6 Cards with items, with the background color being blue-ish.
     5 Cards with items, with the background color being red.
     5 Cards with items, with the background color being purple.
     5 Cards with items, with the background color being green.


Although there are 6 different colors, the rules do not indicate that they are to be used differently or have different value compared to others colors.

This game was published as part of a promotional campaign for the KFC restaurant group.

—user summary

The game contains cards with the names of colors printed on them.  Each named color is printed in 5 colors (all that are also named) and two jokers.  The cards are dealt evenly to all players, with each player holding 3 cards in hard, the rest as a drawing stack.

The purpose of the game is to get rid of your own cards first, including your own draw pile.  To do this, when it's your turn, you try to match one of your hand cards with the color or word of the last played card.  If you can't match, you draw a card from your own draw pile.

The dealer plays the top card from their draw pile as the first card to match.

The game-box contains 36 card, divided as follows:

     1 card with the rules.
     7 cards, in 5 colors + 2 jokers, with the word 'Blue'.
     7 cards, in 5 colors + 2 jokers, with the word 'Green'.
     7 cards, in 5 colors + 2 jokers, with the word 'Yellow'.
     7 cards, in 5 colors + 2 jokers, with the word 'Orange'.
     7 cards, in 5 colors + 2 jokers, with the word 'Red'.


Each word is printed in the colors blue, green, yellow, orange and red once and two cards have a mix of the colors, indicating them as joker cards.

This game resembles Crazy Eights / UNO, but without the "take that" cards.

This game was published as part of a promotional campaign for the KFC restaurant group.

—user summary

The game contains card in two colors, each color has 4 creatures printed on them, the same creatures for each color.  Both colors have 24 cards, each creature has 6 cards in both colors.  There are 48 players card, 6 advanced assignments cards and 1 rule card, making a total of 55 cards.

Players pick one of either color (blue or red) and take all cards in that color and shuffle them up.  They then take turns playing a card, adding it to the set, either horizontally or vertically, but not diagonally.  Their goal is to make a set of 4 cards, either horizontally, vertically or diagonally, of their own color.  The type of monster is not important, only that it's your color.  This can also be played to get 4 in a row, with the rules unchanged otherwise.

There is an advanced version of the game, this uses the 6 advanced assignment cards, where you have to place your monsters in a specific shape to win.  This also includes a "X * monster" card, which means you only win if you get 4 monster (or 5 in the 5-in-a-row variant) in the same row.

This game resembles a paper version of a 4/5-in-a-row type Tic-Tac-Toe game.

This game was published as part of a promotional campaign for the KFC restaurant group.

—user summary

The game contains cards with numbers and 'actions' printed on them, in several colors plus a few jokers.

The purpose of the game is to get rid of your own hand cards first.  To do this, when it's your turn, you try to match on of your own cards with the color or symbol of the last played card.  Jokers are always valid to play, if you have them.  If you can't match, you draw a card from a shared draw pile.  If you can match that card, you can instantly play it.

The game-box contains 36 card, divided as follows:

     3 Cards with the rules, in Dutch, English and French.
     7 Cards with number 1-5, plus 1 sleep (skip turn) and 1 turn (reverse direction) card, in the color yellow.
     7 Cards with number 1-5, plus 1 sleep (skip turn) and 1 turn (reverse direction) card, in the color blue.
     7 Cards with number 1-5, plus 1 sleep (skip turn) and 1 turn (reverse direction) card, in the color green.
     7 Cards with number 1-5, plus 1 sleep (skip turn) and 1 turn (reverse direction) card, in the color red.
     5 Card with additional actions, 2 "take-2" cards, 3 "change color" card, in the color black.


This game resembles Crazy Eights / UNO, but with some the "take that" cards, but not all.

This game was published as part of a promotional campaign for the KFC restaurant group.

—user summary

The Darkness is the first expansion for Wyrd: The Descent.

Emerge from the Hollows, beaten and weary only to Descend again and again, for the World of The Living is ever threatened by The Darkness that surrounds it.

But be careful, the Horrors are not the only threat. Demons claw their way into reality, and will take Possession of you should you delve too deep.

For you are not Wyrd yet.

In The Darkness you will attempt to link your games of Wyrd: The Descent together by developing your Wyrd and avoiding the risk of Possession.

This is the full colour rulebook for The Darkness updated to 2.15 including an updated FAQ, clarifications to the Laws of Possession with examples and the Sigils for the modular PnP Hollows tiles in the reference section. There are also two play sheets to print out to draw your Hollows on, with and without a grid.

You will need a copy of Wyrd: The Descent 1.2+, a set of RPG polyhedral dice and a pen and some paper to play.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: クローバーブーケ (Clover Bouquet)
description:
For two people. Draw one card from the opponent player's hand with the same contents, add one card from your own hand and compare the size of the total number, but only when 4 is mixed, the strength of big and small is reversed. It's a rule.

A model for reading games. It's okay to read each other like this! It's strong to be able to count, but there is also a moderate amount of luck. With the information of the drawn cards drawn by each other, I wonder how to deal with the hand. It solves the problem that if you are completely lucky, it is just gambling, and if it is abstract, the one who is accustomed to the game tends to be unilaterally beaten / beaten.

二人用。中身一緒の相手プレイヤーの手札から1枚を引き、自分の手札から一枚を追加して合計数の大きさ比べをしますが、4が混じった場合のみ、大小の強弱が逆転、というシンプルなルールです。

　読み合いゲームのお手本。読み合いはこういうのでいいんだよこういうので！　カウンティング出来ると強いけど、引き運の部分もほどほどにある。お互いに引いた引かれたカードの情報がある中で、手札とどうやりくりするかを悩む。完全に運だとただのギャンブルだし、アブストラクトだとどちらかゲーム慣れしている方が一方的に殴る/殴られるゲームになりがちなのをうまく解決しています。

Pigeons are surrounding the hamburger! 
A game in which pigeons fight over a hamburger and are mixed up.

You will receive 6 pigeon cards of your color. Take out the boss pigeon and place it in the middle.

Alternately "issue pigeon cards" or "move pigeon cards" from the first player.

To "put out", select one pigeon card you have and place it so that it touches your pigeon card in the field with "sides" or "corners". However, it cannot be placed in a place where it touches the opponent's boss.

In the case of "move", move your pigeon card so that it touches your or your opponent's pigeon card with "sides" or "corners". After moving, you can touch the opponent's boss by "side".

If you repeat your turn alternately, the number of pigeon cards in the field will increase and spread. However, there is a limit to the "size" of this game. The size of the card that can be placed is "vertical and horizontal, only up to 4 pigeon cards". Areas larger than four are treated as "walls" and cards cannot be taken out or moved.

You win the game if the opponent's boss pigeon's card is vertically and horizontally surrounded by 4 squares. The pigeon cards do not have to be your own pigeon card, so make good use of the opponent's pigeon cards and the 4x4 wall to enclose the boss pigeon to win the game!

ハトで囲み込め！ハンバーガーのエサバをめぐるハトが入り乱れるゲーム。

それぞれの色のハトカードを6枚ずつ受け取ります。ボスハトを取り出し、真ん中に配置します。

最初のプレイヤーから交互に「カードを出す」か「カードの移動」をします。

「出す」場合は、手持ちのハトカードを1枚選び、場に置いていある自分のハトカードに「辺」か「角」で接するように配置します。ただし、相手のボスに辺で接する場所には出せません。

「移動」の場合は、自分のハトカードを、自分か相手のハトカードに「辺」か「角」で接するように動かします。移動後なら、相手のボスに「辺」で接することができます。

交互に手番を繰り返していくと、場のハトカードが増えて広がっていきます。ただ、このゲームには「広さ」に制限があります。カードを配置できる広さは「タテヨコ、ハトカード4枚分だけ」です。広さ以上の場所は「壁」扱いとなり、カードを出すことも移動もできません。

勝敗は、相手のボスハトの、タテヨコ4マスを「ハトカード」や「壁」で囲ったら勝ちとなります。ハトカードは自分のハトカードでなくてもOKですので、相手のハトカードや壁もうまく利用してボスハトを囲い込みましょう！

boardgame: Biblios: Quill and Parchment – Chapel Cards
description:
Use your abbot influence to choose tasks and earn more piety points with this 8-card pack.

Introduces Step 4: Compline where players bid on how much influence they would spend to gain the chapel card's benefit.

—description from the publisher's website

boardgame: The Butterfly Garden (Second Edition): Endangered Species
description:
Save endangered species to score more VPs!  This 10-card pack includes 2 variants.

—description from the publisher

Mathematicus is used in three different modes: basic, normal and advanced. 
The board is the same, but different elements are used in each mode such as specific dice, tokens, cards and tools. 
The game in basic mode has simplified rules compared to the other two modes.
Another interesting aspect of Mathematicus is that it can be played simultaneously in different modes by different players, each of which answers questions suited to his level of knowledge.

in basic mode Mathematicus has the structure of a "goose game", that is, dice are rolled and progressed along a path made of spaces, in each of which one is called to carry out certain actions or have benefits or penalties. In Mathematicus two different dice are used, the tetrahedral die (4 faces) and the dodecahedral die (12 faces), leaving the player to choose from time to time which one to use.

You advance along the path by answering the questions (of the level chosen) posed by the cards. Questions must be answered within the indicated times.

Goal of the game: to collect as many figures of famous mathematicians as possible, even taking them away from other players, (using the "Fields Medal" or event card) but you cannot have two figures of the same mathematician.

In advanced mode the game allows you to become familiar with ancient calculation tools (Napier's Sticks and the Calculator Rule), with interesting numbering systems used by various civilizations of the past, and also with binary calculation, essential in the current world of electronics and information technology.

Extracted translation from the rulebook

This is a promotional survivor figure from the Zombicide 2nd edition Kickstarter

A man of action, Gabriel grew from a rank-and-file policeman to a respected anti-drug cop.  Fighting zombies well before the real zombies came, he designed a very efficient intervention method to deal with drug-laced thugs in close-quarters.  A zombie tide destroyed the society he was fighting for and soon made him a legend among survivors: a wise gunslinger willing to help anyone meeting him with a good cause to fight for.

boardgame: La Fête des Vignerons, le jeu
description:
In "La Fête des Vignerons, le jeu", you play as gods who control (with varying degrees of success) the weather.

Helping each one a winegrower from the region, and for the good of the grapes and the wine, you will try to manage the elements.

Your objective: to protect you from the elements by guiding them to your opponents in order to guarantee you a great wine-growing year.

The player with the most saved vineyard at the end of the game wins!

Expansion to Hallowen Party (2019)

Halloween Party: Memento Mori includes:

-2 (3 via KS Stretch Goals) additional playable characters w/ associated reference cards (1 per character, with 1 in Spanish and 1 in English translation...6 total)
-10 Minor Spell Cards
-2 Major Spell Cards
-18 new Creature Cards (+3 additional from KS Stretch Goals)
-15 Ritual Cards
-3 Halloween Pet Cards (via KS stretch goals)
-12 reference cards (6 in Spanish, 6 in English translation)

There are vigorous beavers in the mountain valley.

description:

Players need to catch 6 live performances within 4 different stages in one festival day. We all know it's easier said than done. Pack up your festival bag and get ready for a  muddy, crazy, chilled, rowdy and epic adventure!

Corfù 1716 is a wargame for two players simulating the last Venetian victory against the Ottoman Empire.

After the Morea War and the Passarowitz Peace (1699), with the Venetian reconquest of the Peloponnese area thanks to the great Doge Morosini, the Ottoman Empire seeks its revenge. 
In 1715 the Empire is ready and with the Second Peloponnesian War, it recaptures, thanks to some fortunate events and the lack of competence of the Venetian Generals, all the Aegean islands and the whole Peloponnese. 
Now the Ottoman Empire is ready to strike the Adriatic Sea, the Venetian Gulf itself. 
Only the island and fortress of Corfù is guarding it. 
It is defended by about 5000 soldiers, mostly German mercenaries, lead by Marshal Schulenberg, the Venetian Commander in Chief. 30000 ottoman soldiers tried to storm the fortress but after 20 days of heavy combats the Ottoman Army withdrew. 
It would have been the last victory of the Serenissima, as it is called Venice. Antonio Vivaldi would have composed the oratory Juditha triunphans devicta Holofernis barbarie to celebrate this great victory.

In Corfù 1716 you will find 12 Venetian combat units, divided into 6 German Mercenary units, 3 Italian infantry units and 3 Ultramarine infantry and 20 Ottoman combat units, divided into 4 Janissaries units, 9 Kapikulu infantry and 7 auxiliary Serhaddkulu units representing the regiments and units employed during the siege. 
There are two Morale chits for a total of 34 pieces, 2 d6s, a map, 9 Special Event Cards and the rules.

The map represents the area where the battle took place; it is used to register the orders and the consistency of the two armies. 
The map is divided into Area, where to deploy the units and where the actions take place during the 20 turns. 
There are the Combat Tables, with the result of the Combat and the Morale Track, which registers the Morale level of the two armies and the Turn Table.

—description from the publisher

description:
Monster Bash is a light hearted card game where players try to be the first to play all their cards!

This classic monster themed game is a family friendly game that provides fun competition and simple mathematics!

Monster Bash is a simple game, the player who received the Slime Ball card is the first player and must play the Slime Ball card.

Each subsequent player, in clock-wise order, must play any number of cards equal to the next numerical value; 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and so on. Play continues until either one player has no cards left in their hand or a player cannot play the required numerical value. You cannot play more cards then what is needed to meet the required numerical value.

-description from designer

Players move their steam locomotive by dice from a depot to one of the two stations, pick up a railway wagon and deliver this wagon to the other station. The railway track gives opportunities to overtake other trains or to use an abbreviation.
After this work is done, the player to arrive first at the depot wins the game.

—user summary

The cup is placed in the middle of the board (number 7) and all the markers are placed besides the board (a "bank").

When it is your turn, you roll the two dices and put one of the marker (from "the bank") in the matching circle (from 2 to 11).
If that circle is already filled, then the player empties it by taking all the markers (putting them into personal stash).

When a player hits 7, the player has to pay one "owned" marker into the cup.
When someone hits a 12, that player gets all markers in the cup.

When all the markers in "the bank" are gone, then it is all reversed: when you hit a number: remove a marker from that number (as long as there are markers left).
When you hit a 7, you have to put one of your markers in the cup.
Hit a 12, and you get all the markers in the cup.
The winner is the one with most markers, when all are gone from the board (and from the bank).

No strategy, pure luck

—user summary

description:
Lupercalia beckons. Through decadence and debauchery these lustful figures can be called from the depths of Earth’s history. Espers made manifest only through esoteric quiet whispers shared amongst the elite. Such coveted creatures are called upon for far more than combat. Yet, their true names and seals are clutched closely to the hearts of those who covet them most. Jealousy worms its way into the heart, and rots at the very soul of those who know these secret combinations. In Aloha Earth, players take the roles of alien "Experts of Human Things" to debate the details of a now-extinct (due to...something?) human civilization. Every earth-year, the Human Summit meets to debate important topics such as whether a "Triple Vegan" is a "Transcended Human Being" or a "Sports Pass performed only with a Cabbage".

The whimsical improvisational style puts all players on an even playing field. Because the alien experts don't know what humans were actually like, they can infer results that might be based on complete nonsense. Because YOU are the expert, however, no one can say "You're wrong!"

The scoring in the game inspires collaborative storytelling. After a debate, all players vote on someone who told the most convincing backstory for the object or reference. That player scores two points, but then must award a bonus point to another player who best helped them come to that conclusion!

description:
Mur is an abstract strategy board game for two players, which was invented by Canadian, Desmond R Davies, in Atlantic Canada in 2021. The concept behind Mur's game design draws inspiration from the book of Genesis. The Mur board is a round grid with 25 intersections and the playing pieces are small checkers engraved with ancient galleys making the game set reminiscent of the ancient city of Atlantis as described by Plato in his Critias dialogue.

There are a total of 15 game pieces: 14 ships and one kraken. Seven ships are black and belong to the first player, seven ships are white and belong to the second player, and one piece—the kraken—is red and is a neutral piece. On their turn, a player may place, move, or withdraw a ship. Ships that have been surrounded (closed on) by an opponent must be removed by its owner but may return to play on a later turn. Since there is no capturing and therefore no permanent elimination of material or territory, the game of Mur functions as a perpetually shifting puzzle. The goal is to be the first player to execute three closes, OR the first to execute a close on the Kraken. Visit murfederation.com to learn more!

-description from designer

Calling all Knights is a 2-5 player trick-taking card game for the whole family.

Players act as potential rulers of the kingdom trying to rid the land of pesky monsters. They do this by hiring Knights to fight on their behalf and claim bounties. There are 54 different unique looking knight characters making up the knight deck. For each monster on the field, players send one of their knights out to battle. The best knight slays the beast and gets points for the player.

Monsters are slain by the knight with the highest number. If the knight matches the color of the monster, they trump the other knights of a different color. Monster point values are based on how impressed the villagers are. For example, dragons are worth the most at 4 points each. However, if you slay the bunny, that's negative 4. They just don't realize that the bunny is the most dangerous of them all.

The game is played in 5 rounds. The game starts with each player having 2 knights per hand, but that amount increases by 2 each round with the final round having 10 cards in each players' hands. Each round players bid on how many monsters they will take. If they guess correctly, they get extra points. Bonus points increase each round as it gets more difficult to predict. At the end of the last round, whoever scored the highest wins.

boardgame: Tyrants of the Underdark: Board Game
description:
Tyrants of the Underdark is a territory control game with a deck-building element.

Each player leads a house of Drow in a section of the Underdark below the Sword Coast. The Drow house is represented by a deck of cards, with each card being a minion in that player's deck. Each minion belongs to one of five aspects of Drow society, and those aspects correspond to different strategies in the game, e.g., malice minions excel at assassinating opponents' troops, while ambition minions are best at recruiting additional minions and promoting minions to your "inner circle", which is a special zone that increases their value at the end of the game.

When you set up the game, you create an 80-card deck by shuffling two 40-card half-decks together, with the half-decks being Drow, Dragons, Demons, and Elementals.

A central marketplace has new minions that can be recruited through influence, one of two resources in the game; purchased cards are placed in your discard pile, then shuffled together with other cards in your deck when needed. The other resource is power, which allows you to place troops on the game board, expand your forces across the map of the Underdark, manipulate happenings in the city, and assassinate enemy troops.

Players gain points by controlling sites, recruiting valuable minions, promoting minions to your inner circle, and assassinating troops, and whoever ends the game with the most points wins.

NOTE: This 2021 printing includes the Aberrations & Undead expansion.

ヒモトキ (Himotoki) is a 2 player abstract game from Japan. Players thread a cord through holes in a board that stands between them, so that polygon shapes are formed on both sides. With only ever seeing one side, the first player to successfully declare that they have made exactly four polygons, combining the shapes made on both sides, is the winner.

The board is 21cms square, and shows a pentagon pattern on both sides, with a hole at each point and another hole in the centre. The players hold the board up between them, and take turns to thread the red cord back and forth between the holes. In doing so, the thread starts to outline small shapes, triangles and polygons, as it criss-crosses itself.

Players must use deduction and understand the patterns they can't see, so that when exactly four shapes are made, they can declare and win the game.

By passing the cord through the same hole twice or more, it might confuse the other player as to how the shapes are being formed.

If the players think that too many shapes have been made, they can declare and start over, with no winner that time.
user summary

boardgame: September's Corsair's: The Thompson Trophy Races, 1946-1949
description:
Following the outbreak of another world war in September 1939 the National Air Races, including its showpiece Thompson Trophy race, were suspended for the duration of the war. In September 1946 the National Air Races were back, now under the leadership of the Cleveland Air Foundation. The Thompson Trophy races, however, were changed from those of the pre-war era. Gone were the racers built by private aircraft corporations or made by designer pilot craftsmen. In their places were war surplus fighter planes stripped of their armaments and modified for even more horsepower. The modified Corsairs, Air Cobras, Mustangs and Lightnings were flown almost exclusively by a younger generation of pilots groomed by wartime training and experience. With no limits and few rules, the post war Thompson Trophy races portended for those who sat in a brand new purpose-built grandstand even greater thrilling aerial spectacles than those witnessed seven years before.

Each copy of September’s Corsairs is composed of the following:

•    17x pilot cards
•    14x plane cards
•    14x die cut aircraft units
•    1x die cut 1946-1949 “Pack” unit
•    6x course balloon markers
•    One 8 page rules and scenario booklet

Please note that September' Eagles is required to play September's Corsairs.

-description from designer

boardgame: 信仰通貨 (The Faith Of Currencies)
description:
信仰通貨 (The Faith Of Currencies) is a game from Japan, where players are currency traders trying to maximise their wealth. But the value of the currency depends on how much faith each trader has in the religions. Players are trying to promote the worship of one deity, whilst also preparing to switch allegiances if the deity falls from favour.

The game has a market board to show the relative exchange rates, and a track board to show how the faith is fluctuating, using small cubes for each religion (the rulebook is called Prayer's Guide).

There is a deck of cards which is used to calculate the player's score at the end of the game. At the start of the game, two cards are dealt up to move two prices. Then the deck is shuffled and players are dealt three cards. Four more cards are dealt up to form a draft.

The game has a huge stack of crisp bank notes, beautifully illustrated in the style of real banknotes, to represent each religion. The Esoteric Order Of Dagon is the US dollar, and the Euro (Zeus), Yen (Amariti) and Yuan (Communism) are also used, along with a Flying Spaghetti Monster coin (banknote). The backs of the notes are blanked, so players cannot know what anybody is holding. Players are dealt two notes of each of the five types at the start.

The game is played over a series of rounds, one more than the number of players. In a round, a player may trade currencies with the bank, or change their faith, or adjust the market prices.

When a player trades currencies, they sell what they want from their supply and buy from the bank at the current rates. The number of banknotes exchanged then alters the faith in each currency.

For example, a player might sell 2 Communism at 12 each, and buy 3 Amariti at 8 each. This moves the Communism cube along the faith track two places, and the Amariti cube moves three places. The Communism cube is now on a +1 space, so the price rises to 13. The Amariti cube is now on a +/-2 space, so the player can move the price up or down two places, for example up to 10.

Moving these cubes triggers the round ending. When a faith cube has lapped and reached its starting point, the day's trading ends and a new round starts.

A player can change their faith by discarding and drawing cards from the open supply or the top of the deck. Each religious card shows an effect on the value, dependent on the number of cards held (1,2 or 3). The value of the religion is based upon different factors, such as how much the price is, or how much total currency is held, or for the FSM, how many sets of notes you can form.

The final action a player can take is simply to move a faith cube, and adjust the market price accordingly.

Players continue to take turns carrying out these actions, until the round ends, and after the final round ends, players calculate their scores. A player's score depends on how many of which faith card they hold, and the relative prices, and how many banknotes they hold. Once calculated, the player with the most points wins.

By the open trading of banknotes and open adjusting of prices, other player's might deduce what hidden faith cards a player holds. But a player could be bluffing and trying to manipulate the market whilst holding different faiths in their hand. As the market develops, a player will want to switch their faith to maximise their final score.
user summary

boardgame: The Pure Land: Ōnin War in Muromachi Japan, 1465-1477
description:
The Pure Land: Ōnin War in Muromachi Japan, 1465-1477 is Volume XIV of the COIN Series originally designed by Volko Ruhnke. It depicts a devastating civil war in 15th century Japan that reduced Kyoto to a smoldering ruin and precipitated the century-long warring states period—the Sengoku Jidai. Against the backdrop of this civil war between coalitions led by the Hosokawa and Yamana clan, the game also features peasant revolts led by the Jizamurai and religious unrest involving the Ikkō-ikki, the militant wing of the emerging Jōdo Shinshū (or True Pure Land) Buddhist sect.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: History: Kamakura Period to Muromachi Period

boardgame: What the What?!: The Party Game Reinvented
description:
WHAT THE WHAT?! is a 3-10 person party game where players compete to invent outrageous new uses for genuine old-time inventions. Featuring audacious attempts, fantastic fails, and kooky contraptions from the world’s patent archives.

It’s easy to play. The Investor reveals an illustrated invention card. Players become the Inventor and channel their inner hustler, comedian, and entrepreneur to pitch the most outrageous - or accurate - explanation of WHAT THE WHAT?!! is that?

How To Play
At the beginning of the game, each player is dealt 10 Pitch Prompt cards. Take one Invention Card and place it on the table. The Investor acts like a judge and the remaining players are Inventors, whose job it is to pitch these oddball inventions and impress the Investor to score points.

Inventors have 1-minute to write down their pitches or select one of their Pitch Prompt cards to use as a basis for their pitch.

“What you need when…” or “For people who….” or, “A device for...”

The Investor hands out points and turns the inventions over and reads the back of the card, revealing information about the invention. The next player to the left becomes the next Investor, and so on until every player, in turn, has been the Investor once.

Game over!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Winter Solstice Lucy
description:
Promo Pattern and Gear card that were included with the Winter Solistice Lucy.

The Winter Solistice Lucy kit comes with 2 miniatures. A 54mm scale model perfect for painters and a 35mm scale model for gamers looking for a fun survivor stand-in for their springtime gaming session. It also includes the Grim Muffler pattern and gear cards.

Can you find the lost village before the other Smurfs?

The race is on to find the lost village. Smurfs must try to stay ahead from other Smurfs by taking shortcuts and double moves. But watch out for Gargamel and Azrael, as they will set you back in your quest.

—description from the back of the box

Smurfs The Lost Village Board Game is a great way to have fun with kids.  Simple colour-based game mechanics are easy to learn, and with shortcuts and hazards along the way, the path to the lost village is more fun than ever!

—description from the publisher

A storage chest that also includes with new game play content.

This box has three independent containers inside and will comfortably hold all Roles, Races, Traits, and Legendary cards that are planned to be released.

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Klik's Madness Campaign Book
description:
This choose-your-own-path, board game/novel hybrid, is an ambitious product whose story takes place in the Runecrest Valley, before the Dead King arrives. Play through the Valley of the Dead King (Volume I) in an entirely new way and experience the unfolding of a complex and epic story. Your heroes will be pulled into the narrative and will drive how the story unfolds based on the decisions and actions you make.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Living Card Deck
description:
Continue your adventure in the Runecrest Valley! The content in this deck box requires the use of the Valley of the Dead King Core box.

Add 60 new cards to supplement the Valley of the Dead King Core Box.

12 Circumstances: Experience new Encounters, Treasures, Nature effects and more.

24 Quests: Play through a twelve-card Quest arc that brings a rich story to life that unravels quickly during play. Additionally, you'll find 12 independent Quests to help the quirky gnome Bezzelquark.

24 Peril Cards: Newly introduced in this deck, these dangerous cards initiate challenging scenarios for the heroes to overcome and offer huge rewards to those who can!

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Encounter Deck
description:
This Encounter Deck adds a new way to play the Valley of the Dead King. All previously existing encounters from the Core Box, the Expansion, and the Living Card deck are reprinted with epic new art!

These decks add a new way to play the game by separating the Encounters into their own deck. Additionally, these encounters are not restricted to the volume they originally appeared in, allowing you to add and customize what encounters will appear in any volume you play.

Contents include:

Rules insert describing this new playstyle 
32 Full art encounters

Boons offer new and increased rewards on the next Encounter you face!

Game Difficulty increase cards. Each deck will come with 3 Game Difficulty increase cards.

Conditions/Keyword list: Add conditions that appeared exclusively in certain volumes to any game.

Role variations: updated rules for the Roles who Summon Encounters

How do Boons work exactly?
Boons are newly introduced in Domain of Mirza Noctis. When you draw one of these from the Encounter deck, you’ll draw another card. If it is another Boon, you keep drawing. If the last card drawn is an Encounter, you’ll gain extra rewards after battle. If the card is not an Encounter(a difficulty increase for instance), you’ll shuffle the Boons back into the Encounter deck. Boons offer new and increased rewards!

-description from publisher's website

In Shark Hunt!, you and your friends are patrolling the beach for sharks that have been taking bites out of the beachgoers. You will roll a die that will indicate the movement of the sharks. Then, all the hunters will move their boats in pursuit. If a shark reaches a boat, the shark attacks and attempts to sink the boat, gaining points for the sharks! If a boat reaches a shark, the hunter attempts to capture the shark, gaining points for that hunter! If a shark makes it to the coast, they attack a beachgoer, gaining points for the sharks! It is a battle for beachfront supremacy!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Domain of Mirza Noctis – Living Card Deck
description:
Continue your adventure in the harrowing realm of Krasvetelia. The content in this deck box requires the use of the Domain of Mirza Noctis Core box.

Add 60 new cards to supplement the Domain of Mirza Noctis Core Box.

12 What is This? Day Cards: Experience new Encounters, Treasures, Nature effects, Escorts, and more.

12 What was That? Night Cards: Just like the "What is This" living cards, gain several new experiences to augment your nightly travels.

12 From on High Investigation Arc: Play through a twelve-card Investigation arc that follows an Angel as he investigates the disappearance of one of his allies. Will he too, succumb to Noctis's influence, or will you save a fallen angel?

12 Blood Ties Investigation Arc: Play through a twelve-card Investigation arc that brings you dangerously close to Noctis's blood collectors. Find out what they're doing and how they're doing it as this dark story unfolds.

12 Encounters: Add new illustrated Encounters to your core box.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Domain of Mirza Noctis – Return to the Domain of Mirza Noctis
description:
This expansion presents a future timeline for your heroes — what happens when Noctis succeeds?

The dark lord has gathered the blood needed for his ritual and has ascended to godhood. But his goal of ascension wasn’t what people thought it would be. His hostility against the commoners of the land has eerily ceased. New, terrible horrors have arrived in the land of Krasvetelia, seeking to tear it asunder. Did he summon them? Or was he preparing for this eventuality all along? Noctis may be the only one strong enough to stand against these horrors. But did he gather enough power, or will he succumb to these titanic forces from another plane of existence? Only your heroes can help shape Krasvetelia’s destiny.

The Horrors have arrived.
Tragedy. Calamity. Disaster. Cataclysm. Forge an entirely new journey in the darkening land of Krasvetelia and gather power as quickly as you can to stop the four terrible horrors that have appeared in the world. You’ll face each, in a different order each time you play. Your only ally is Noctis himself, but can you keep your sanity, or will you succumb to madness?

What does this Expansion add?

3 new epic Roles and 11 powerful Races to add to your collection (perfect for solo and small group play!)

4 variable level Horrors - face them in a different order and experience contrasting effects each time you play

Madness - Your heroes will accumulate Madness, but can you keep your sanity long enough?

Epic Power Ups, Encounters, Investigations, Events, Treasures, and more for each of your Core box card decks

Mythic Legendary Items and Allies

New Keepsakes to keep your heroes going after death

A new punchboard for your map that interacts uniquely with the four Horrors

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Aventuria: Al'anfanian Blessed One of Boron
description:
This pack of 55 cards presents you the new heroine Zephira Golgarez, an Al'Anfan Blessed One to Boron. The pack contains hero cards, talent cards, a life card, the starting hero deck of 30 action cards and the 18 cards from the heroine's wealth of experience, which are used in connection with the Aventuria legends mode.

Dieser Kartenpack aus 55 Karten präsentiert euch die neue Heldin Zephira Golgarez, eine al'anfanische Borongeweihte. Im Pack enthalten sind Heldenkarten, Talentkarten, eine Lebenspunkte-Karte, das Start-Heldendeck aus 30 Aktionskarten sowie die 18 Karten des Erfahrungsschatzes der Heldin, die im Zusammenhang mit dem Aventuria Legendenmodus zum Einsatz kommen.

—description from the publisher

This pack of 55 cards presents you the new hero Geron Orphan Maker, a Garethic double mercenary. The pack contains hero cards, talent cards, a life card, the starting hero deck of 30 action cards and the 18 cards from the hero's wealth of experience, which are used in connection with the Aventuria legends mode.

Dieser Kartenpack aus 55 Karten präsentiert euch den neuen Helden Geron Waisenmacher, einen garethischen Doppelsöldner. Im Pack enthalten sind Heldenkarten, Talentkarten, eine Lebenspunkte-Karte, das Start-Heldendeck aus 30 Aktionskarten sowie die 18 Karten des Erfahrungsschatzes des Helden, die im Zusammenhang mit dem Aventuria Legendenmodus zum Einsatz kommen.

—description from the publisher

The central element of this supplementary box is a handy little booklet with two new adventures - Under the Northern Lights and The Streuner Shall Die - as well as “Director's Cut” versions (significantly more extensive versions) of well-known adventures such as The Legacy of Wildenstein, Forest Without Return and Ship of Lost Souls. All of these adventures use the new Aventuria story mode featured in the box. A number of story mode playing cards are also included.

The story mode can be played in combination with the legend mode or independently.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Das zentrale Element dieser Ergänzungs-Box ist ein handliches Büchlein mit zwei neuen Abenteuern – Unter dem Nordlicht und Der Streuner soll sterben – sowie „Director's Cut“-Versionen (also deutlich umfangreichere Versionen) altbekannter Abenteuer wie Das Erbe von Wildenstein, Wald ohne Wiederkehr und Schiff der Verlorenen Seelen. Alle diese Abenteuer verwenden den neuen Aventuria-Geschichtenmodus, der in der Box vorgestellt wird. Zudem sind eine Reihe von Spielkarten für den Geschichtenmodus enthalten.

Der Geschichtenmodus kann in Kombination mit dem Legendenmodus gespielt werden oder unabhängig davon.

—description from the publisher

In this oversized extension box, we present the new Aventuria legends mode with around 600 cards. Here your heroes finally have the opportunity to travel to the nearest settlement after an adventure and invest their hard-earned adventure points in new, improved cards. This makes every hero and heroine not only stronger and more powerful, but also a real Aventurian legend with a very individual hero deck and many special abilities.

When designing the legend mode, we paid particular attention to making it interesting for beginners and casual gamers, so that every Aventuria player really gets something out of it.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In dieser übergroßen Ergänzungs-Box präsentieren wir euch mit rund 600 Karten den neuen Aventuria-Legendenmodus. Hier haben eure Helden nun endlich die Gelegenheit, nach einem Abenteuer in die nächstgelegene Siedlung zu reisen und ihre hart verdienten Abenteuerpunkte in neue, verbesserte Karten zu investieren. Dadurch wird jeder Held und jede Heldin nicht nur stärker und mächtiger, sondern auch zu einer echten aventurischen Legende mit einem ganz individuellen Heldendeck und vielen besonderen Fähigkeiten.

Wir haben beim Entwurf des Legendenmodus ganz besonderen Wert darauf gelegt, ihn auch für Spieleinsteiger und Gelegenheitsspieler interessant zu machen, damit wirklich jeder Aventuria-Spieler etwas davon hat.

—description from the publisher

This box contains all stretch goals of the Mythen & Legenden Kickstarter crowdfunded expansion including new alternate gender heroes.

It contains 220 cards (alternative heroes, trait cards, new minions, leader actions, events, hero titles and much more)
and Die cut sheet with 12 hero stamps
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Ruparstein is a land that didn’t stand out from the rest of the world until their rulers decided to export their national entertainment: the golem races. Nowadays it’s a very widespread sport with lots of racers and an anxious and unconditional audience. Skilful gnomes prepare their iron golems, necromants stitch up bodies together to create their ultimate flesh golem, dwarven conjurers sculpt stone to give birth a solid runner, and a hundred other participants wait for their call to the start line. The best runners have been called. It is now the time to make your coach a rich man and to catapult him to golem’s glory.
—description from the rulebook

The game is played by a referee and multiple racers.  Players create individual golems, primarily by random roll.  Play then proceeds in turns, each turn spans four distinct phases.  During Phase 1, players determine initiative (who will go first that turn.  During Phase 2, players move their pieces in initiative order.  During Phase 3, players check for mechanical problems.  During Phase 4, the referee adjusts the board as needed.  The game ends when the first player - the winner - crosses the finish line.

The game is provided as a PDF rules document with suggestions on common items that can be used as game components.

—user summary

boardgame: Cra Diavolo, or, "That Ferocious and Insolent Toad"
description:
Brigand or Patriot? Sworn enemy of King Tadio Polo and his Ferach allies, the legendary Cra Diavolo is the focus of resistance against the sovereign of Nepolise. Let Todoroni rebels join battle!

An unofficial expansion for Flintloque Reloaded.

—description from the rulebook

boardgame: The Guns of Cra Diavolo, or, "The artillery of King Polo takes flight"

Scenario for Flintloque Reloaded, for two players.

        - Cra Diavolo


Flintloque Reloaded Scenario (for two players)

—description from the rulebook

boardgame: The Devil's Roost, or, "When the going gets tough, the tough get going"
description:
"Messieurs, today you have the opportunity to make a great service to our country, or to die in the attempt."

        - Generale Champignon


Scenario for Flintloque Reloaded for two, three, or four players.

—description from the rulebook

boardgame: By the Shimmering Sea, or, "Three Orcs in a Boat (To Say Nothing of the Dog)"
description:
"Well blow me down and gouge out me eye if them ain't pansy elves in them girly uniforms yonder!"

        - Marine Jack Tarr


Scenario for Flintloque Reloaded (for two or three players).

With the rulebook now adopting the revised victory conditions from the old More Missions pack, we've updated it (now just called the Missions pack) to reflect this. We've also added the missions from the Experimental Missions pack as well, consolidating them all in one place. Includes 6 print and play cards.

The pack contains Twenty missions: • Annihilation • Breakthrough • Bridgehead • Contact • Cornered • Counterattack • Dust Up • Dogfight • Encounter • Encirclement •  Encounter •  Escape • Free For All • Escape  • Fighting Withdrawl • Free for All • Gauntlet • Killing Ground • No Retreat • Outflanked • Scouts Out •  Spearpoint •  Vanguard •

Each mission has a double page telling you how to play the mission and detailing the mission special rules. You can either print each mission out on a single piece of paper (front and back) or print it as a booklet with each mission on facing pages.

The Battle Plans mission selector allow you and your opponent to choose the most appropriate missions for your forces. Each player chooses a plan (Prepared Attack, Hasty Attack, or Defend) and then you look up a table to see which mission to play and who will be the attacker.

—description from the publisher

In Dominus, players will be fighting for control of the Europe and North African territories. To do this, players will be able to either use the military symbols combination or the prestige symbol combination.

Players will do this until the end of the game, and the player with the majority of armies in a territory, will win the first place in that territory and score most for whatever the condition is in that territory. We should also note that this board replaces the military board in the base game.

In Portus, your neighborhoods will be able to craft goods which you can then export from your port!

Once you think you have enough goods for export, you can simply turn over those goods to the supply and score prestige depending on what types of goods you have exported! Sometimes, some goods will cost you population, so you need to plan carefully for such occasion.

Love Links is a 2-4 player game that lets players create and wear bracelets made of precious metals and jewels.

Players place 2 bracelet links during their turn, joining to links already placed by other players according to the properties of links already played.

If a bracelet is completed then the player receives points according to length of the bracelet, the metal in the bracelet, the jewels and its other features. And the player receives the bracelet to wear!

—description from the designer

The player with the most points after all bracelet links have been played wins.
3 in 1: L.A.M.A., L.A.M.A. Party Edition and L.A.M.A. Dice

The game board is nine tic-tac-toe fields in a 3x3 grid. Whoever manages to lay a line of three fields wins - but only one of the nine quadrants. It's not that easy at all: The opponent determines which quadrant you are allowed to play on with his previous move. In this way he can prevent one of his lines from being interrupted.

—user summary

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Hidden Gems Bundle
description:
The Hidden Gems Bundle is an expansion pack to Cards Against Humanity.

Contains six different themed packs and 10 all-new cards.

Contents: 190 cards total (150 White cards + 40 Black Cards).

Collect a winning combination of Lucky Cats in this colourful, cat collecting card game from Ridley's Games! The aim of the game is to collect a winning combination of Lucky Cats before the other players to win a round. The first person to win two rounds wins the game! There are 2 winning combinations of cats: 7 of the same numbered cats in different colours or cats numbered 1-7 of the same colour. Contains instructions, 65 game cards, 4 reference cards, packaged in a novelty Lucky Cat shaped case.

The Board is a track from 1975 up to Number 1. Players must answer trivia questions on the UK pop scene for 1960 to 1986. Up to 4 players get their own question book. There are 2,500 questions in the game which is a good number and enhances replay ability.
Before the start each player writes down 10 song titles and keeps that secret.

As you move up the board (usually by rolling a die) you will land on one of 9 types of squares.

IN CONCERT - Pick another player. Pick a number from 1 to 10. That player now has to mime the title on his secret list with the number you chose. You get 2 minutes to get the song title.
BLANK SPACE - You are told a question. Get it right, you can have another turn. Get it wrong and you end your turn.
COVER VERSION - Choose another player and select a number from 1 to 10. That player now reveals the song on his list. You must then sing two consecutive lines in the song. Get it right and you get another turn, Get it wrong and you end your turn.
HYPE - Guilty of illegal song placement. End your turn and go back to Start.
BAN - Having a song banned from the radio is a sure sign of selling lots of copies. Move up 10 spaces on the board.
END OF TURN/FREE GO - No question to answer and continue your turn.
REMIX - Ask a question to any other player from your question book. If that player he stays where hew is. If he gets it wrong he moves 6 spaces down the board.
THE TOP TEN - No dice movement. Instead you answer a question correctly to move 1 space up the board. Get it wrong, you stay where you are. If you are on Square Number 2 you must answer 2 consecutive questions to get to Number One. If you get there first, you are the winner.

The game was an advertising item for Pepsi.
Peter Powell (b.1951) was a very popular BBC Disc-Jockey. His image is on the cover.

—user summary

In Katzekartonen, two players compete to end the game with the most cats in the boxes of their color. Play occurs in two phases: first, there is the "Experimental Design." Place boxes of your color into the lab room game board. But be careful, your opponent may place their boxes inside yours, Changing an opportunity for you to score to one for them.

Once all the boxes are placed, it's time for Cat Herding! Try to place more cats in your boxes than your opponent. But cats are tricky, and rarely stay where they're placed! Place a cat beside another, and it will jump to another box, which may cause the next cat to jump, which can cause the next... It could be a whole cascade of chaotic cats. Can you tame the chaos to herd more cats I to your boxes and force your opponent to cause a "Cuckoo Kitty" and end the game?

This game utilizes a unique take on the tile-laying genre by adding a layering component and a secondary movement round. Its "strategic chaos" gameplay makes it fun for veteran "crunchy" players and new gamers alike.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 大怪獣ヴェナタス VS 強欲な異能建築家 (Dai kaijuu Venatas vs Goyouku na inou-kenchikuka)
description:
大怪獣ヴェナタス VS 強欲な異能建築家 (Daikaiju Venatas vs Goyoku na inou-kenchikuka) is a game from Japan with players constructing properties and sending monsters to destroy the opponents' buildings.

Over 6 rounds, players place cubes in the different sectors of the city. As buildings are constructed, the land prices rise in the sectors. Then the players send Godzilla crashing around, which brings down land prices. Then they sell the buildings if they have the matching cards in hand. Unsold buildings stay in place, risking destruction next turn.

There are caps on the number of buildings to be built or sold, and the cap rises over the game.

At the end of the game, the player who's made the most profit is the winner.

boardgame: Path of Light and Shadow: Solstice
description:
After many winters of war, the Realms lay in ruin. The feud of the deposed heirs to the throne has spilled out across the sea, reaching the forgotten shores of the Hinterlands. There in the abandoned cities and broken temples, relics of impossible power await. But so do their keepers, the ancient race Viratis that walk between the light and the shadow.

In this expansion you will explore megalith ruins on a new island, utilize magical relics, and command new forces that move between Cruelty and Mercy for powerful effects.

Inside the box you can find new player cards for existing factions, a new Region deck for the Hinterlands that include the new Viratis faction, and special map Regions that contain powerful Megaliths instead of towers.

The KS campaign also included a URL to a free mini-solo mode: Faraway Invaders.

-description from publisher
