publishers: るりるりゲームズ (ruri ruri games)

description:
Light-strategy tabletop card game. Each player takes the role of a mountain Llama Farmer trying to make the most money ('Bucks') selling llama-wool rugs.

Players start with their own small herd of randomised primary coloured llama cards. Each turn players expand, shear, weave or sell.
Expanding adds new primary coloured llamas, or expands the llama 'family-tree' making more complex colours and patterns available.
Shearing produces wool, either unpredictably with a repeatable 'light shear' based on selected dice and numbers on the llama cards producing wool for any farmer or a one-shot 'close shave' which guarantees wool at the cost of a chilly looking llama!
Weaving combines wools into new colours or rugs and selling trades rugs for bucks in the markets. High-fashion markets want specific rugs and pay well, but a general market will buy any rug cheaply.

The game ends when all the high-fashion markets close, and the farmer with the most bucks wins.

Strategy choice comes from choosing the right rugs and llamas to go for, balancing expanding and shearing and knowing when to apply a close shave.

—description from the publisher

Players must travel along the board collecting pizzas.  The first one to get to the end of the board with 3 pizzas wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Magna Acies! Armies & Battles: Punic and Macedonian Wars
description:
This booklet is the first in a series, entitled Armies and Battles, accompanying MAGNA ACIES! "battle size" rules for 3D wargames for the Ancient age (without the rules, therefore, this booklet serves you little purpose).

We decided to start from the Punic Wars and the Macedonian Wars because these were probably, from a tactical point of view, the most interesting conflicts of Antiquity, which marked (together with Alexander's campaigns) new reference models for Military Art.

During these conflicts, the Romans had to abandon the primitive concept of the simple "attack on the center" to worry about the wings as well, while the phalanx ceased to be the most successful formation of Antiquity: fundamental themes, as you can well understand, decisive for 15 subsequent centuries and even beyond.

This booklet consists essentially of three elements:

     three "Quickstart Scenarios" (with attached game tables),
     fifteen "Army Lists" (always with attached game tables) and
     six "Battle Maps"


—description from the publisher

Nico is a promotional character (miniature plus character card) for Zombicide 2nd Edition.

A master of wielding dual katanas.

boardgame: BattleTech: Alpha Strike – Succession Wars Cards
description:
Alpha Strike is BattleTech’s fast-playing game of armored combat. Alpha Strike Succession Wars Cards contains the ‘Mech Units that survived centuries of warfare across the Inner Sphere. Fullcolor, poker sized, and laminated for dry-erase use, you just need miniatures and dice and you’ll be battling for control of mankind’s tenuous future.

     98 double-sided, dry-erase cards expand players’ available ‘Mech roster.
     10 double-sided combat command cards include special rules to play units of renown from BattleTech lore.
     Includes all the ‘Mechs from Technical Readout: Succession Wars

boardgame: Making the Rounds: A Micro Nursing Game
description:
You have 2 shifts to try and treat all of your patients in your hall by monitoring, evaluating, medicating, taking samples to the lab, and even treating Code: Blues. Each patient will have 3 needs you will have to meet with your action cards. Drawing action cards will let you decide where you should start and placing your nurse meeple there and trying to pull the right combo to complete the task at hand. If you succeed, you can flip the patient card over, if you don’t succeed, you turn the card sideways and on your next shift you can try to treat that patient again. If after 2 shifts you only have 1 patient not succeeded then you will have 1 over time shift to treat that patient.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: BattleTech: Alpha Strike – Clan Invasion Cards
description:
Alpha Strike is BattleTech’s fast-playing game of armored combat. Alpha Strike Clan Invasion Cards contains the ‘Mech Units that sprung from the distant Periphery to terrorize the Inner Sphere and ‘Mechs designed to repel them. Full-color, poker sized, and laminated for dry-erase use, you just need miniatures and dice and you’ll be battling for control of mankind’s tenuous future.

     98 double-sided, dry-erase cards expand players’ available ‘Mech roster.
     10 double-sided combat command cards include special rules to play units of renown from BattleTech lore.
     Includes all the ‘Mechs from Technical Readout: Clan Invasion

Mage War is a FREE trading card game where you construct your own cards and make decks to battle other players. It is a game of strategy with a focus on balanced mechanics that put opponents on equal footing. (No need to worry about an opponent having an advantage over you because they spent more money to build their deck.) The rules lay out all the guidelines for designing and creating your own cards and decks as well as how to play. The limits of your power are the limits of your own imagination! Craft your own cards and build your own strategy around your own play style and pit your ideas against your friends.

—description from the designer

In Szybkie bańki ("Fast bubbles") game, the objective is to find the right card faster than your opponents.

The game is played with cards featuring colored bubbles of different sizes and quantities. Each card belongs to a specific color group, indicated by a liquid droplet in the corner. At the start, cards are sorted into separate stacks by liquid color.

Each round begins by revealing a task card, which shows a pattern of bubbles that players must match. All players search simultaneously, scanning the stacks to find the correct card by following these steps:

     The color of the first bubble on the task card determines which stack to check first.
     The size and number of bubbles help narrow down possible matches.
     Players must mentally trace the clues from one card to another until they find the exact match (if this card was trace twice).


The first player to correctly tap the right card wins it, along with any linked cards. A wrong guess costs the player one previously earned card.

The game ends when all cards have been played. The player with the most cards wins. In case of a tie, the last player to collect a card is the winner.
Children's racing game where players upgrade their cars and try to win trophies during the 3-laps' drive. The one who gets the most points in the end wins.

A comic-strategic game in which the participants "measure up" the coolness of their dad. They are credited with the properties of heroes in games, comics and films. Collect combinations and monitor the progress of opponents. The game combines simple and strategic gameplay with a fun design full of references to popular games and films from the 90s and 2000s.

Players use Honor cards to "pimp" their dad, dressing him in armor and presenting him with various kinds of artifacts that refer to popular games, films and comics. And opponents' dads can be thrown with negative discouraging cards. Reaction cards allow you to fend off opponents or strengthen your actions, and Freak cards are the most powerful arguments in the fight against other players. For each set they collect, players receive Honorable Points and new powerful cards.

On his turn, a player can play from 1 to 2 cards. Before playing an Honor card, the player must say loudly and clearly: "And my / your daddy ..." - and then the text from the card. Otherwise, the card will not work and will be discarded.

English version of title: And my daddy has chainsaw hands....

Play continues until "The End" card is reached and points are totaled.  The player with the most points wins.

Be the first to assemble a monster from different body parts and bring it to life!

A monster must have the following body parts: A head, a torso, a left arm, a right arm, a left leg and a right leg.

Use power cards to improve your chances or to hinder your opponents! This is where the game really comes to life. From creating monsters with different background colours to stealing cards from your opponents or destroying their power cards, you'll find many fiendish ways to give yourself an advantage - but beware, your opponents will be trying to play the same tricks on you!

—description from the publisher

Choose one of the four Rat Queens from the Image Comics bestselling Comic book series of the same name, each with asymmetric and unique playstyles, in order to save Palisade from total destruction. Power up your Queen (Betty, Hannah, Violet or Dee) with ass kicking new abilities as you take on hordes of evil, despicable villains.

Friendship and teamwork are critical to gaining strength and momentum as you slaughter and stack up the bodies of your enemies. Rat Queens utilizes a fun and intuitive Friendship System, where leaving a Queen Token on the board for other Queens allows them to directly improve their skills and unlock special abilities. Work together, massacre evil, and score as much loot as you can before the big bad boss arrives to flatten Palisade and erase it from history.

Sharpen your blades, pack your gear, and imbibe an intoxicant of your choice.

It's time to take Vengeance on Villains.

Poop Poop is a collection set card game for children.

The children are farmers that have to preparing to fertilize their fields. They need the help of farm animals. Each animals have to eat and drink to poop. Collecting all poop is needed to win.

Each player have five cards, when the player has at same time an animal, a water and food can discard them and take the corresponding poop.

There are also some special cards to stole poop, or change poop with other players.

—user summary

アザトース (Azathoth) is a card game from Japan mixing anime and Cthulhu themes. A variation on Old Maid, players are trying to form up pairs of cards to empty their hands. Some of the cards are for the Old Gods. The cards have text on them, and the Old Gods have special abilities. Players must always keep Old Gods cards in their hand (they start with one), and if they are found without one, they draw a penalty.

The main cards are anime, with letters of the alphabet, such that a player asks another for an A or a B and so on. Making up sets lets the player discard them.

The special abilities of the Old Gods restrict how those cards might be played.

Like many Loserdogs games, the game is sold in a large ziplocked bag with minimal production. The cardstock is thin but playable.

boardgame: Hero Realms: Imperial Justice Promo Card
description:
Part of the Game Trade Magazine (GTM) May 2021 issue 255 but only for copies sold in stores in the USA. It will also later be available to buy from different sources, like conventions.

Name: Imperial Justice
Faction: Imperial
Gold Cost: 4
Card Type: Action
Card Text: Add 6 to your combat pool, Imperial Ally Ability: Draw a card for each opposing champion you stunned this turn.

The Castle Panic Deluxe Collection is the long-awaited, ultimate, deluxe edition of Castle Panic and all of it's expansions. This giant box contains 5 games in their limited edition, deluxe versions including: Castle Panic, The Wizard's Tower, The Dark Titan, Engines of War, and the brand new expansion Crowns and Quests.

Each game retains the same mechanics fans have loved for over a decade, but now feature deluxe upgraded components. Each game comes with all new art, plastic Walls, Towers, and castle pieces, plastic Monster minis that feature a unique spinning base to track damage, wooden Monster tokens, promo cards, a deluxe monster bag, playmat, and much more. This massive collection features 180 plastic pieces including 97 Monster miniatures!

Castle Panic is a cooperative game for 1 to 6 players where everyone is working together to defend the castle from monsters coming in from the forest. The players must make it through all 49 Monster tokens and have at least 1 tower still standing in order to win the game. If the monsters destroy all 6 towers, the game ends and the monsters win.

In The Wizard's Tower, a friendly wizard joins your forces, giving the players access to powerful magic spell cards. But the monster army returns, bigger, smarter, and faster than ever before.

In The Dark Titan, the ancient Titan, Agranok returns to lay siege to the castle. Agranok is proceeded by his Heralds and brings new monsters while the players get help from Support Tokens and the Cavalier.

Engines of War brings a whole new economy to the game where players can assign tasks to the Engineer and collectively pay Resource cards toward those tasks to build traps and weapons. Meanwhile the monsters press the attack with Siege Engines and new creatures.

The newest expansion, Crowns and Quests, introduces playable characters with unique powers. The players must attempt to complete challenging quests such as retrieving magical items, destroying cursed temples, and even teleporting the entire castle to safety all while fighting off the monster army.

This collection is the centerpiece to the Castle Panic Deluxe Collection Kickstarter and as such will not be available through retail distribution.

-description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description: In the early 1950s, Brazil was agitated by a campaign in favor of the nationalization of the oil industry. This game is based on this spirit: it is a simple game, the players spin the roulette wheel to transport their equipment to the drilling point. When they get there they automatically lose a round and start their progression through the houses corresponding to the land to be drilled. The winner is the first to reach the oil layer with an exact bid.

The 実名報道 (The Jitsumeihodo) is a game from Japan in which players identify famous people by their real names. In japan, Jitsumeihodo means 'real name reporting' and is the somewhat controversial practise of identify people in news coverage by their real names.

The game has a deck of 60 cards, 30 being the real names of people, and 30 showing a colour photo of the person. On the back of the photos is the same photo min B&W and a short text explaining what crime they were arrested for. For example, a popular comedian caught with cocaine, or a hit-and-run driver.

The 30 photo cards are shuffled and laid out randomly on the table, showing only the colour photo side. The deck of names is shuffled up and placed in the middle, so the first name can be read out.

Players try to match up the correct name to the face. However, this is difficult since the people are generally known by a public name, not by their real name.

The game can be played solitaire.

Like many Loserdogs games, the game is sold in a large ziplocked bag with minimal production. The cardstock is thin but playable.

boardgame: Taxi!: The "Drive as You Please" Traffic Game
description:
In Taxi, you play the role of a taxi driver whose goal is to pick up passengers and deliver them to the airport. All the while, you have to obey the rules of the road. If you are in violation of the driving laws, then you can become targeted by the police.

Players control their taxi plus they control one police car. On your turn, roll two 6 sided dice. You assign one die to yourself and one to your police car. But you also have two optional methods for the roll:  you can assign both dice to your police car move, or you can take one of your dice, assign it to your taxi, and DOUBLE it. This represents speeding, which is a violation.

Whenever you break the law, you become vulnerable to being captured by other player's police cars. Other violations include: picking up passengers from the wrong side of the road and going down one way streets the wrong way. If you are caught by police for violating the law, you lose any passengers in your cab, you go to traffic court and pay hefty fines.

Game ends when there are no more passengers to pick up. The taxi driver with the most money wins the game and is obligated to look in a mirror and say "are you lookin at me?".

boardgame: Anno Domini 1666: Envoys of the High Porte – Janissaries Commoners Set
description:
Soldiers of the elite 32nd orta boluk are among the few that were not influenced by the crisis spreading in the Janissary corps. They are devoted to their commander and religion, they have hing morale and are very difficult to force off the battlefield. Despite having firearms they usually use it to start the fight and then move on to close combat.

3 plastic miniatures
6 Character cards including three cards exclusive for this expansion: 2x Janissary Veteran and Janissary Odabasi
9 item cards

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Anno Domini 1666: Bohun's Rebels – Moloitsy Commoners Set
description:
Armed with sabres and arquebuses, moloitsy form the backbone of Cossack armies. They are proficient in close and ranged combat, but their true strengths are their senses and their quickness sharpened by the tough life in the Ukrainian steppes.

3 plastic miniatures 
6 Character cards including three cards exclusive for this expansion: 2x Moloiets Veteran and Desatnik
6 item cards

-description from publisher's website

Fire Team Vietnam 2: Platoon Command is a Sub-Tactical level game of the Vietnam War, (1965-1975) that places each player in the role of a Company Commander, Sections and Squad of the U.S. Army or Allied Infantry Company against the forces of the National Liberation Front (NLF) (Viet Cong) and the North Vietnam Army (NVA) through various missions in the Vietnam.

These rules are an evolution of Fire Team Vietnam in a Version 2.0, with the aim of simplifying the rules so that it is easier and faster to play. 
But also to be able to play at 2 tactical levels with the corresponding rules and maps:

     Platoon Command = Sub-tactical , 1 hex = 5 meters, a Game Turn = 1 minute.

boardgame: Sunday League Football: A Board Game
description:
Sunday League Football: A Board Game

Sunday League Football is a 2-4 player board game that brings all the heart-stopping drama and tension of the beautiful game right to your tabletop.

Take control of 1 of 4 unique amateur Football Clubs, each with different football philosophies, strengths and weaknesses and go for the title. During the week You'll need to build your squad, hire the right talent, choose a manager that complements your football style, learn the best tactics to carry out your game plan, and choose the starting eleven for each match.

During match days you go face to face against your rivals in intense, fast paced, and strategical matches. All decisions on the pitch come down to you. Managing your footballer resources, abilities and tactics in the best way possible added to the correct killer instinct to know when to strike is key to win.

The battles of World War Two ravaged the entire world. 
Where the Band of Brothers starter set is very much concerned with the European Theatre in the wake of D-Day, this starter set shifts focus to the Pacific, pitting the United States Marine Corps against Japanese Island defenders in some of the most brutal and hard-fought battles of the War.

This starter set is focussed on the island-hopping campaigns enacted by the US to reclaim Japanese advances. 
Unlike the land war that raged in Europe, North Africa, and the Soviet Union, where vast armies of tanks and infantry could manoeuvre and fight over large tracts of open ground, the Pacific campaign was fought mostly on a series of relatively small islands, densely packed with jungles and protected by the sea. Each island had to be landed on, fought for, and the defenders rooted out of, individually.

Containing two opposing forces set amongst the many islands in the Pacific theatre, Island Assault! contains the Bolt Action 2nd edition rules as well as a scenario booklet guiding you through your first steps in the game.

Before long you’ll be adding to your new army and wreaking havoc on your foes be they fighting for Uncle Sam or the Emperor!

-description from the publisher

This is the big day of 2200!

A declaration of friendship between Planet Earth and Planet Kepo has been announced. Kepo aliens are so excited to study more about human traits. Hence, a big global event is held: A Game of Traits!

KEPO: A Game of Traits is a social party game that helps you reveal what people think about you. It has 3 different game
modes: 1 cooperative game & 2 competitive games. Players will be challenged to test their self-awareness and their ability to observe other players’ traits. With more than 400 traits and 3 difficulty levels to play, every single game will create a unique experience, filled with fun and new stories!

boardgame: 太陽と雨と虹  (Sun, Rain and Rainbow)
description:
太陽と雨と虹  ("Sun, Rain and Rainbow") is a card game. The cards consist of 36 cards in 7 color suits. Each suit has either a Sun or Rain(s). The color of suit can be recognized by the card back. The box comes with 4 games (with their rulebooks): "Sun Rummy”, "Rain Go Out", "Rainbow Trick" and "Weather Line".

"Lamii of the Sun"
Put the card in your place and make a role. By recombining the cards in the field and making good use of the special effects of the roles, you can create a role with a higher score and aim for a higher score by rising faster.

"Rain go out"
I will put out cards of the same color or continuous colors face down. I don't even know if it's "rainy" or "sunny". By reading and intuition, I will compete to get out of my hand as soon as possible.

"Rainbow Trick"
An anomalous trick-taking game where you control the points you earn. The winner will take the card by comparing the strength of the cards issued by everyone. Repeat this process, calculate the points you can get with the cards you get, and compete for points according to the points.

"Weather line"
Cards are put out alternately to make a four-row line. Compare the number of "own teams" for each row, and the one with the most will conquer that row. Compete for victory or defeat by the total number of conquered rows.
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description:
DINGO is a card game for the whole family, where you have to be the fastest creating words with the syllables that appear on the cards. But there are cheat cards you'll have to avoid!!.

The winner will be the player who previously runs out of cards in hand.

—description from the publisher (translated)

DINGO es un juego de cartas para toda la familia, donde tendrás que ser el más rápido creando palabras con las sílabas que aparecen en las cartas. Pero hay cartas trampas que tendrás que evitar!!.

El ganador será aquel jugador que antes se quede sin cartas en mano.

—description from the publisher

Go on the funniest roller coaster and be the first to finish the ride!

Deal all the cards evenly between the players. They will keep them in their hands facing down.

The starting player is the player who has ridden a roller coaster most recently, otherwise the youngest player will be.

In turns, players play cards in the center of the table, one on top of other, in clockwise order. They must perform the actions corresponding to each played card correctly (see Game Cards). So that the first player who makes a mistake or otherwise, the last player who does it right, will draw 2 cards from the central deck as a penalty.

Regardless of the player who drew 2 cards, the game continues with the player who is next in turn.

The first player who runs out of cards wins the game!

Contents: 55 cards

-description from designer

boardgame: Inn & Stable Owners (fan expansion to Carcassonne)
description:
Inn and Stable owners of Carcassonne are fed up with the thieves getting all the points by owning the road.

In this Expansion the different features on the Carcassonne tiles can be used to get points even if you do not own the road.

A new way to score the Inns and the Stables.

During a strategic and diplomatic meeting between the delegations of each opposing power in Westeros (the King in the North and the Mother of Dragons), the castle is attacked by the White Walkers. Your time is running out as the ice boulder blocking the room entrance is is gradually melting.

A secret passage out of the room is hidden somewhere, will you find it in time? Will you put your political disagreements aside and work with your enemies in order to have better chances at survival? Live the moment as if it were your last, because if you can't fend off the White Walkers in 1 hour, you may well become one ...

This escape game can be played in two teams or as one collaborative group. One player needs to be Game Master and hide riddles and clues in two IRL rooms.

Contents:

1 organizer booklet containing explanations of setting up the game, pledges and answers

2 "mission" sheets with the summary of the rules of the game

41 puzzle cards and tips

10 special "Characters", "Bonus" and "Malus" cards

24 envelopes to hide

A play-based game for children age 18 months and up.

In this game, an adult player hides a fluffy bunny under one of the six cups, and the child's task is then to find the bunny.

The game has eight suggested play modes, depending on the child's age and development.

boardgame: Shadow Kingdoms of Valeria: Rise of Titans
description:
The Story of Rise of Titans: 
Lord Nore, the Lich King, has sacrificed his essence in order to become the Shrine of the Titans, allowing Wardens to resurrect their strongest warriors. As the Shrine magically snapped into existence, it also unearthed ancient spells that will help you turn the tide of battle or manipulate your status within the war. The listless Wraiths have finally awoken from their slumber and now crave retribution for the many wrongs done to monster-kin throughout the ages. Now is the time to send your hordes to great battles and crush the Humans, Elves, and Dwarves from Valeria that have taken your lands. With these new powers at your disposal, will you be able to reclaim your glory and become the next General of the Dark Army?

Introduction: This expansion includes four modules: Shrine of the Titans, Great Battles, Ancient Spells, and Wraith Dice. You may play with one or more modules, mixing and matching as you please. Modules can all be included in a game of Shadow Kingdoms of Valeria, though we recommend that you have a firm understanding of the base game and each module before including them all at once.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Birdsong is a Roll-and-Write game that immerses you into a role of a birder in a specific location spending a week spotting, listening to, writing about, and photographing birds unique to that area.

THEME
Travel to Glacier Bay, Alaska in search of Puffins.
Or head down to Big Bend, Texas to snap a prize photo of a Painted Bunting.
Maybe you just want to take your equipment and a taxi down to Central Park to listen to starlings as they return, heralding a new season.
You can also choose to search for ibis and spoonbills in Florida's Everglades.
Two of the international birding locations are the Galapagos Islands and New Zealand.

Each location sheet is different and has birds native to the area. Each individual bird is a scientific illustration, beautifully painted -- The game sheets themselves can even double as birding checklists for the specific locations.

MECHANICS
Birdsong has four different play modes:
Competitive 
Solitaire
Cooperative
Team v Team

In competitive play, players colored draft dice one at a time to allocate to three different areas of their unique player board (bird spotting, preparation, and research). Some areas require a specific color. Others require a number, and some might just require any die of any color. In this mode it's important to see what dice are beneficial to your opponents as well as to yourself. After each player has allocated 18 dice, the game ends and points are tallied from the different sections to determine the winner.

In solitaire play, the player allocates a die and then the next most valuable die disappears. Their task is to gather as many points as possible in 6 3-die rounds.

In cooperative play, everybody is working together to score as many points as possible for the lowest scoring player. In this mode, players will collaborate and give up dice to other players to keep the scoring gap small.

Team v Team play is a variant of cooperative with the exception the players can give/trade dice with their teammates in order to keep the gap small. Everybody on the player with the lowest score at the end of the game loses.

-description from designer

boardgame: Anno Domini 1666: Veterans of the Tercios – Hidalgos Commoners Set
description:
Hidalgos are an impoverished Spanish nobles. Often all their worldly possessions comprise a patchwork jacket, a rapier by their side and sometimes an old pistol. An old hat and a cloak are their shelter. Their faces are scarred, mostly because of drunken brawls rather than great battles but don't underestimate them - they use weapons with deadly efficiency.

Impoverished Spanish nobles.

3 plastic miniatures 
6 Character cards including three cards exclusive for this expansion: 2 x Hidalgo Veteran & Cabo
6 item cards

-description from publisher's website

Summary:

In 1-2-3 Cheese, players are a brilliant group of mice caught in a cheese heist. To escape, players must try to drop their cheese in other players’ stashes as quickly as possible. Are you one of the amateurs who always tries to drop one cheese at a time? Or, are you the profound mastermind who dares to cheese others three or more cards in a shot? After three rounds of tossing and gobbling, the player who has the most escape points wins the game. Are you ready to cheese?

Goal:

The goal is to have as few cards as possible. During the round, play cards to other players' stashes by either playing multiple cards of the same number or a run of three or more cards. The round ends when a player is out of cards. Players  receive escape points based on the number of cards in their hand and stash. After three rounds, the player with the most points wins the game. In case of a tie, the tied players share the win.

—description from the designer

Accumulate trauma and debt as you try to make it to old age in this dark parody board game. Start with an emotional flaw and an arbitrary amount of debt. Decide whether or not you believe in free will.

Players take turns rolling dice to move their way through the board. Spaces you land on require you to: pay rent, get into a traumatic situation, find a partner, have them break up with you, trigger a world event that hurts everyone in the game, so on and so forth.

Use jobs to help your fellow teammates along their way. 
Take therapy at an absurd price to keep your growing trauma in check or your character commits suicide. But don't land on the black space, or else.

The game ends when all players are dead. If you make it to the end of the board and old age, congratulations! You win or something. You're still dead though.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Quiz game about Paris landmarks.

The board is a map of the city, with a track wandering through it. If a player is the first to land on one of the numbered spaces, he draws the card from the deck of 30 cards and reads out the information on the pink side of the card. Then he turns the card over to see what instructions he has to follow. This are fairly predictable; Miss a turn perhaps, roll the die and move backwards, and so on. The player then takes the card and keeps it in front of him where all the players can see it. The card will have a value ranging from 5 to 30, with the value being commensurate with the penalty value of the instruction on it. If a subsequent player lands on a numbered field that has already been taken, then he treats the space as a normal space without number.

When the numbered space 30 has been reached, that player has finished. The others play on. When all the players have reached the 30 space, the 'won' cards are added up and the winner is the one with the most points.

boardgame: Lord Of The Drinks: Władca Promili
description:
You are the one they will tell you about

"His last party was like a Predator meeting with Sauron."

Discover the story of the Promila Ruler.

The time has come to go to the Bottom of the Mortuary, to give the Big Eye the Evil Cookie and take it to the Oven of Destiny - where it will be burned, incinerated, stoned, smoked and thus stop the Big Eye, which wants to cover the world with eternal darkness (yes, it has photophobia). Then there will be great joy ... and every joy should be poured out. In this evil plan, you will be disturbed by the cookie guardian - Bloodthirsty York. But don't worry ... if you fail to deliver the Cake to Piecyek, it may be eternal darkness, but you can always pour your sorrows out of despair.

Simple rules are a way to quickly engage your friends.
The rivalry, twists and turns of the Bloodthirsty York guarantee that you will be happy to come back to the game at subsequent events.
Powerful references to "a certain trilogy" will provide you with an additional, abstract dose of humor.
Everything in addition twice - two boards, two types of gameplay, it's twice as crazy party!

Challenge yourself with the Solo Board, featuring timed goals to reach before they expire, and research labs that give you the ability to reroll dice if you connect them to your network!

—description from the publisher

A unique board for Railroad Ink specifically designed for solo play.

The Enclave is an asymmetrical board game of strategy in the conflict between several fantasy factions. This armed conflict is centered around the Human kingdom, which is divided by four opposing sides: The Horde, Iron fist, Forest union and Cult. Each player takes on the role of one of the four sides.

During the game, players develop unique technologies of their faction, conduct research, claim or destroy different objectives on the playing board. They gather armies, build structures and attract neutral units to join their cause. To achieve victory, players must complete military and diplomatic missions, while also pursuing their factions’ unique secret agendas.

In the Funkoverse Strategy Game, you combine your favorite characters and go head-to-head in exciting game scenarios.
Each turn, you select one of your characters and perform two actions. Each character has access to basic actions like moving and challenges as well as several unique abilities that may be performed only by spending ability tokens.

Funkoverse uses an innovative "cooldown" system—the more powerful the ability, the longer it will take for the ability token to become available again—so players have to spend their ability tokens wisely. Each character in Funkoverse is unique, so players are encouraged to try out different combinations of characters and items in order to discover their favorite synergies and powerful strategies for the different game scenarios.

Funkoverse Strategy Game Expansion: Thanos is an expansion for any standalone title in the Funkoverse line-up.

Funkoverse Strategy Game Expansion: Marvel 101 Thanos Expansion is an expansion for any standalone title in the Funkoverse line-up.

In this game, we will draw simple graphs using resources available to us: vertices, edges, and Eulerian (this resource acts as currency). To draw a graph, we need vertices and edges. We use Eulerian to use the effect of an active Extra Power card.

Each round we will choose one resource card from the ones available in that round, one resource card usually contains a number of two different resources. There is also Extra Power that we can use for that round. This Extra Power changes every round.

Keep an eye out for the quantity, color, and type of candy. As soon as you see a common feature on 3 cards put them in your victory pile! Very fast, very simple, very fun. 
There are 5 colours, 4 types of sweets and 3 options of quantity on the cards. 
Cards are being laid out slowly. 
All players watch the opened cards and act simultaneously.
As soon as there are 3 cards with a common feature on the table players have to identify the feature and say it out loud. E.g. "Ice cream!" or "Blue!" or "Two!" 
Whoever calls out the feature first takes the cards.
The goal is to collect the most cards.
Where are some wild cards which can block a category or substitute any feature in category.

5 cool things about Lollipops

     everyone loves sweets
     fast-paced and noisy gameplay – perfect icebreaker
     keeping in mind 3 features instantly pumps up the reaction, speed, and attentiveness
     just a little space needed to play
     easy to carry around.


—description from the publisher

Divers go on an underwater hunt and must reach the firefish before their opponents in order to win. Whoever wins the respective trick can draw their diver and lay event cards. Each player has a stack of face-down cards and draws the top one. Whoever lays the highest draw card wins the trick and the cards. Then he draws the value of the collected move cards, won event cards he may place beforehand for his own advantage or disadvantage of the opponents. The full number must be drawn, several pieces may stand on one square, the target square must be reached directly, excess points must be drawn backwards.

—user summary

boardgame: ナンバースイッチ (Number Switch)
description:
ナンバースイッチ (Number Switch) is a game from Japan, based upon a local news story. An electronics store was giving away a free Nintendo Switch, but didn't use a sensible method of picking the winning number. As a result, two customers had the numbers either side of the winning number. The game designer used this event as the inspiration for this deduction game.

The game uses a suit of 1-13 cards from a normal deck of playing cards. One player is the retailer, the other players are the customers in the lottery. The retailer shuffles the 13 cards and removes one in secret, which nobody sees.

Then the retailer divides the 12 remaining cards into two piles and sorts them each from low to high numbers, placing them face-down. The players take turns to draw the top card from a pile and after getting three hand cards, a player make declare the hidden card. If they are correct, they win.

If the cards run out and no player has guessed correctly, the retailer can declare a number card that a player has (deducing in reverse). If the player does hold that card in their hand, the retailer wins.

As with other games from Loserdogs, this was produced in a simple ziplock baggie with only a page of rules and 13 playing cards.
user summary

The frogs (pawns) migrate from their winter quarters to the spawning water, but the pond is in danger of drying up. The players move their frogs, clean the pond and fill it with water again. The player whose turn it is draws event cards and the frogs move. They have to stop at danger spots and try to roll the number and the solution for the danger. If the attempt is unsuccessful, a dry stone enters the pond, but the other players can help, forego their movement and roll the dice to remove the danger. Once at the pond, the players can now remove dry stones, always according to the number of frogs already present. If there are fewer than 15 dry stones in the pond at the end and there is no toxic waste in the pond, the players have won.

—user summary

boardgame: The Final Light-Year: The Delta Sector
description:
May 2021 episode for The Final Light-Year.

This kit adds a new map, new crew and a new set of enemies, but instead of a new boss, the kit brings a Spaceport postcard that introduces longer form campaign play.

You'll place the spaceport between two sector maps and play two full games, with a little extra trouble in-between. The enemies and boss at the second sector are boosted, but you can use actions in the first sector to weaken them. It's an all new type of balance, looking toward the long game.

This is the first of many spaceports, each twisting the two game campaign in a new way, and even allowing bigger and bigger campaigns as more are released.


Released in the May 2021 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

Find a robot with the right combination of shapes or colors. Repeat the move and collect the winning card. 
Do the robot dance!

There are 20 cards with robots. Each has a unique combination of colors, shapes, and a unique movement. Small cards indicate colors and shapes.
To enjoy the game call your children and their friends, put 20 robot cards in a circle, small cards are in the center. Each round you open a small card. All players are looking for a robot with such colors or shapes. The first person to repeat the move receives the small card as a victory point. The goal is to collect the most cards.
5 cool things about ROBODANCE

     active and dynamic game
     combines developing fast response and attention with movement. 
     the moves such as dab, rock, and peace sign are trendy and kids like to repeat them.
     the complexity can be easily adjusted 
     all players are included in the process throughout the game.


—description from the publisher

Poets is a 6-card expansion that shuffles into the game, adds the ability to play with a 4th player and is fully compatible with the solo expansion, Skulls of Sedlec: Monstrance, as well.

This expansion to Skulls of Sedlec adds a new type of skull: the Poets. Poets want to be next to forlorn Romantics. They score points per adjacent, non-scoring Romantic.  For those that can't seem to pair up the romantics, you might split them up on purpose now to get the most points out of your poets.

Adding in the expansion is easy, just shuffle in the expansion cards to the base game and play.

boardgame: PURI! PURI! 漏ルカー (Poo Poo Leaker)
description:
This is a freebie game from Toryo Hojo designed as a "クソゲーRTA" (kusoge real time attack), which is a speed run to make a shitty game.

Players race to the finish line by rolling dice and choosing a result to use for movement, placing poop markers in the space they have left. If a poop marker is in the destination space, the player is eliminated. If another player's piece in the space, the active player rolls for movement again.

The game ends when either a single player remains on the board, or if a player crosses the finish line.

This was distributed as part of the Spring 2021 Market Crash pack and players must provide their own playing pieces.

This is a freebie game by Toryo Hojo where players are the head of local Japanese government and try to reduce the number of people affected by the decline of the economy from COVID-19.
Players muct make good use of the “Manbo” policy to slow down the new Coronavirus!

Each player controls regions of Japan that have a matrix of infected population and economic health. On their turn, a player rolls dice and performs an action from the chart:


     COVID excise tax increase
     Healthcare subsidy
     Unemployment stimulus
     Stay-at-home request
     Go eat
     Go travel
     "Manbo!"


With the other die, they must raise the infected population of the matching region.

Because the COVID outbreak occurred in the same year the Tokyo Olympics were scheduled to happen, the torch relay happens contemporaneously with player actions and is used as a timer for the end of the game.

The game was included as a part of the Spring 2021 Market Crash pack.

boardgame: ボッチャのようなもの (Some Kind of Boccia)
description:
This is a freebie game designed by Toryo Hojo and requires players to provide their own pieces for play. It was included in the Spring 2021 Market Crash pack.

Two players compete in a tabletop game of bocce. Players from the red side and the blue side compete by throwing the ball into the court. First, a white ball called “jack” is thrown into the court, and then the red and the blue sides both throw a ball toward the jack. The side with the ball farthest from the jack is the side that throws their balls in first. When one team has used up all their balls, the other team throws their remaining balls into the court. When all the balls have been thrown into the court, the side with the ball closest to the jack scores.

The throws are determined via dice rolls, which also determine the initial drop point of the thrown ball, any deflections of the ball if it collides with another, and the final landing space of all balls involved in the throw and resulting collisions. The board is comprised of a matrix of potential die rolls.

And advanced game adds "focus," which is used to give better direction to the throws.

'"For sheer fun, it's one hard act to follow . . ."

Act One is the exciting game where players do "almost anything" to try and get their teammates to guess titles of popular movies and TV shows. You'll laugh your head off (until it's your turn) watching your teammates do their best to act or read famous lines from movies and TV shows while they try to beat the clock (actually it's a sand-timer). The sooner teams guess titles correctly, the more points are scored! (But, who cares who wins, when it's this much fun).

No batteries necessary . . . Teammates not included.

Almost two thousand movie and TV moments from the 70's, 80's and 90's. Great party game.

Games Magazine Top Games Selection

Now includes: 25% more cards & the new "Almost Anything Goes" round!'

—description from the back of the box

You are a spacer; a freelance space traveler in a brand new solar system. You have a ship, a handful of spacebucks, and a wealth of opportunities. How will you become famous?

Spacers is a space adventure game where the goal is to become the most famous traveler in the solar system. You are free to earn fame in a variety of different ways, allowing you to live out the space adventure fantasy that you want. Engage in pick-up and deliver tasks, dice-based combat missions, and even choose-you-own-adventure style encounters!


     Trading! Exploring! Smuggling! Pirating!
     Evolving supply and demand system
     Encounters with varied outcomes
     Randomized setup
     Virtually limitless ways to play


—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroes of Stalingrad: War Stories – Otto Carius
description:
War Stories is a series of smaller expansions, much like Heroes of Normandie's Gazettes from before, but with a tighter focus on historical events and soldiers.

Where Gazettes where products designed for die-hard fans, War Stories will be more accessible, requiring less of the massive backlog of previously released expansions. War Stories will mostly be playable with just the core box, and the content of a War Stories issue itself, obviously.

Each issue will focus on a War Hero and will include a biography, his exploits, a dedicated scenario and one or two punchboards to integrate it into your game.

The first issue will be for Heroes of Stalingrad, and focuses on Otto Carius, a German Tiger tank commander who destroyed a total of 150 enemy tanks. This issue will also include a section of T34s for the Russian forces as well as the errata of the Frontoviks from the Heroes of Stalingrad game base box.

Featuring new artwork from Nicola Angius.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide (2nd Edition): Daily Zombie Spawn Set
description:
Contains 31 Daily Zombie Spawn Figures and a rules leaflet.

This is a Kickstarter exclusive extra.

boardgame: Forts & Forests:  The War for North America
description:
In Forts & Forests: The War for North America, you command one of the 18th century’s great superpowers in a struggle to control Canada, the American colonies and the vast wealth of the continent beyond.

But the French & Indian War is only part of a greater global conflict, so resources are limited and must be allocated with care. Militia is easily raised but unreliable. Regular forces deliver power and strength under fire, but they are expensive and must be transported across an ocean. Then there are gifts to the Indians, whose speed and fierce ambushes can turn the tide of the war, but only if their pricey allegiance can be maintained.

Forts & Forests begins with the British poised to launch an ambitious four-pronged attack to bring the war to a speedy end. They have greater numbers, naval superiority and secure ports, but much of their army is made up of untested colonial provincial forces. The push inland from the Atlantic coast will be difficult, and the support of the powerful Iroquois is tepid at best.

Though outnumbered, most French troops are battle hardened regulars recently arrived from Europe. They are supported by fearsome hordes of western tribes who have been convinced to take up the hatchet against the English invaders. New France also has a geographic advantage; troops and supplies can be moved quickly along the St. Lawrence and Great Lakes with relative ease. But the connection to France is tenuous, especially if the bastion at Louisbourg were to fall!

Forts & Forests’ unique combat system captures the challenges and uncertainties of the wilderness war. Over 300 historically accurate unit cards capture their differing combat, ambush, break, and speed ratings. No battle is a sure victory regardless of numerical superiority!

It will take strategy, determination, and a bit of luck to conquer a land of Forts & Forests and win the War for North America.

—description from the publisher

This time you won't be the usual unblemished hero, but the great infernal ruler, master of demons and dragons, summoner of divinity, loved and respected by your cult and feared by the greedy governments of the world!

Eternam is a solitaire dice and cards game. Each turn you’ll roll your dice and assign them on the monster cards you play. You must choose carefully your demons and dragons, because the stronger the monsters, the more souls they will require of you, and souls are the resource you need to win the game.

—description from the designer

Linkto Terre is a co-operative trivia game in which players team up to find the key amongst the 50 cards that feature unique earth art.

Together, players link all 49 clue cards to the 50 word cards. They then discover whether the remaining, unlinked word card is the key to their victory, validating its code with the answer card to know whether they've won the game!

Linkto Terre offers five levels of difficulty in the game as well as a competitive mode for those who want it.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"The motto of all the mongoose family is, 'Run and find out'; and Rikki-tikki was a true mongoose."

Based on Rudyard Kipling's short story, Rikki-Tikki-Tavi pits Cobras and Mongooses (or "Mongeese"?) against each other. Rikki-Tikki leads the Mongoose army against Nag and Nagaina and their band of snakes, but be careful when you are scouting through the thick jungle; a harmless snake scout may turn out to be the ruthless Nagaina herself!

Each side seeks to kill the enemy leader using an army of pieces with different abilities. The type of piece is kept secret from your opponent, so you can choose whether to bluff your opponent by keeping your leader brazenly exposed, press your luck with fearless attacks, or strategically cover your pieces with a careful defense.

A modern take on the game of Chess, Rikki-Tikki-Tavi makes each game a thrilling and unique experience that can no longer be "solved", and levels the playing field between enthusiasts and newbies, while still preserving tactical gameplay.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Art of Boxing Clever: Solo Play AI Expansion Pack
description:
The Solo Play AI expansion for The Art of Boxing Clever allows you to pit your wits against three skill levels of AI boxers. Each of the five boxing styles are available to fight against in the form of the 5 boxers from the base game.

Expand your Art of Boxing Clever board game experience with Solo Play. Can you beat a bunch of cards?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Art of Boxing Clever: Arcade Mode Expansion Pack
description:
The Arcade mode for The Art of Boxing Clever offers an alternative experience for faster paced gameplay. Perfect for when you are short of time.

Time becomes a factor in your decision making and the original The Art of Boxing Clever damage system is streamlined to create a simplified boxer health bar indicator. All the tactical options are available to you from the original game, but if it's possible this is an even more intense version of the ART of BOXING CLEVER.

—description from the publisher

Monster Clashhh! is a fast and fun ROLL & FIGHT game. You have to be first friends with all 5 monsters! When you be a friends with monsters, attack another player! Also you have to keeping the most life points at the same time. Risk management, tactical choices and luck are the keys which will lead you to the victory.

Gameplay

1. Every player receives one summoner’s sheet.
2. Players play clockwise.
3. First player rolls the four dice, trying to get a combo allowing you to fill the spaces of Monster or the spaces of Special Ability. You can roll 3 times the dices (choosing the one(s) you wanna roll again).
4. When you decide to stop (usually after the 3rd roll), trigger the effect of your final combos according to the rules.
5. When you finish, the player at your left can play his turn (by rolling his dice and completing his summoner’s sheet).
6. The game is over when a player succeeds in summoning the five different monsters while having the most HP or if all of his opponents lose all of their HP.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Art of Boxing Clever: Fighting Dirty Expansion Pack
description:
The Fighting Dirty expansion for the ART of BOXING CLEVER has been designed to add extra spice to the base game by introducing the underhanded tactics and dark arts of fighting dirty in the boxing ring. The expansion offers players new tactics to gain the upper hand over their opponents and inflict maximum damage on their most vulnerable areas.

Sportsmanship is now a factor. Will your boxer be an instigator or a victim? New options are available for you to decide which approach suits your needs.

Last but by no means least, introducing your referee Mr Geoffrey Weller who be on the lookout for any illegal tactics. Boxer's will be warned, deducted points or even disqualified if spotted.

—description from the designer

Travel to 8 Exotic destinations, with varying prices simulating the variables of real travel. Destinations such as Alaska, Costa Rica, Hawaii, Italy, Mexico, Tahiti.

Object of the game is to travel to as many Destinations within a set time.  There are no dice and spinner offers varying prices.  Player buys vouchers, for example 'Scuba diving in Tahiti 200 X Spin.  If he spins 1, pay $200.  If another player spins 8, pay $1,600 for the Same voucher!

This takes into consideration variables such as traveling High vs. low season, Deluxe vs. economy, Number in your party. This is what makes my game Unique, as there are no set prices in travel.

'Travel Ups and Downs' cards add fun surprises.  Up: Dream vacation, go to any Destination.  Down:  Luggage went on its own vacation, Pay $1,500 to the Travel Bonus corner.  Player who lands on this space gets all the loot!

—description from the publisher

Circustar is a perfect filler game for your game night. Each round, players receives 5 performance cards and players can score once per each performance during the game. So you have to manage your cards and choose which performance to score in each round to earn best score. 
But be careful! Other players may mess up your plan.

—description from the publisher

The Junkyard Expansion for Goons introduces a brand new Junkyard location to finally give players a chance to do something useful with all those annoying junk cubes laying around! Just snatch a junk cube, take it to the Junkyard and trade it for something useful! Just keep in mind that the Junkyard might want a little something in return...

The expansion also introduces 5 new Items to throw your money at. Get a hold of a super gnarly skateboard, badass nunchucks, a can of refreshing Knuckle Soda, gloves or a butterfly knife!

And of course, 4 more zany Villains with new Schemes presenting new risk, rewards and challenges! Will you throw your support behind Big Carls wacky wildlife shenanigans? Join Aztro’s psychedelic pattern based schemes, support Teresas explosive plans, or help The Void perform his uncanny rites?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Anno Domini 1666: Swords of Light and Darkness
description:
Swords of Light and Darkness is an expansion to Anno Domini 1666, a swashbuckling board game of intrigue and mystery, set in alternative reality 17th century Vienna.

In the Swords of Light and Darkness players will try to convice Elector of Brandenburg Friedrich Wilhelm to vote for candidate supported by their political faction. It depends on you whether Louis XIV of France or Charles II of Spain called El Hechizado  (The Bewitched) will become new emperor. In the shadow of the great politics we'll see duel between the two famous fencers: Salvatore Fabris, possessed by mighty demon Caarcinolaas and Gérard Thibault d'Anvers. Only few people realise that conseqences of duel between Sword of Light and Sword of Darkness could be much more serious than result of the imperial election...

In this environmental game, players try to prevent greenland from being covered with mud. In his turn, a player reveals a situation card that shows a positive or negative environmental behaviour. In the case of negative behaviour, the bad figure "Schmutzich" is moved towards a target field, in the case of positive behaviour, the good figure "Knud" may be moved towards the target field. In addition, the player rolls the dice and can move his own figure or "Knud" towards the target field. If "Schmutzich" reaches the target field first, mud is placed on grassland, if "Knud" and a player arrive before "Schmutzich", a rainbow piece is placed. The players win together if they manage to lay 4 rainbow pieces. The players lose if "Schmutzich" was able to lay 4 mud pieces.

—user summary

boardgame: バトルアスリート (Battle Athlete)
description:
バトルアスリート (Battle Athlete) is a racing game from Japan. Players are athletes competing in a road race across mountains and valleys. But the course has obstacles and the players push each other around or use items to get the jump on the others. The first to the finish line is the winner.

The race course is made of large tiles placed in a line. The game has a starting tile, and five course tiles, of which four are used, and a finish line. The course tiles have a variety of icons; walls, pits, arrows and objects.

Players choose the turn order, and the last player puts their athlete where they like on the start line, then the next player and so on. In this way, the start player does not get an advantage.

Players roll dice and move their athlete. If they end up on an icon, or push another athlete onto one, the actions are completed.

Pits make a player fall back 2 places and miss a turn. Walls delay players, and arrows push them in the direction of the arrow. The ? icon lets the player draw an object card from the deck. These objects allow the player to make advances, or delay other players, or even change the position of the course tiles.

The first player to reach the finish line wins the race.

The designer Misaki notes that this was one of their earliest designs. It became the third game published by the doujin circle, but had been a car race game sent to a magazine five years previously. The prototype was made 15 years before 2009, making it 1994. The designer thought it was too good a game to waste and brought it back into production in 2009.
user summary

The players compete as biathletes. They move their pawns with a dice over a circuit with uphills and downhills. The dice are also rolled at the shooting range.
The player who crosses the finish line first wins the game.
Several rule additions, e.g. event cards, risk minimisation when shooting, increased risk of falling when moving downhill and the use of tactical action cards extend the basic game.

—user summary

The game is in the tradition of the abstract connecting games like Hex and consists of 36 identical tiles showing a route in the shape of a "Y". At the beginning, all tiles are arranged face down forming a large square. On your turn, pick a face down tile and push it from the border into the square with the Y face up.
The player who first has a complete route from his side to the opposite side wins.

—user summary

Arithmetic game with a traditional card deck, from David Parlett, creator of Ninety-Nine.  From a hand of 5, players play a card at a time, either deducting from or dividing into the current sum. Totals start at 101 each hand and go down to as close to 0 as possible. The catch is: If card played divides into the sum without remainder then it may not be deducted but must divide. (For instance a 5 played to 20 must divide for 4 rather than deduct for 15, whereas a 3 must deduct since it can't divide into 20 evenly.)

An Ace counts either 1 or 11. A Jack played repeats the same value as the previous card. A Queen reverses the digits of the current count. However, you may only play a Queen if reversing the digits reduces the count. A King divides by any desired number higher than 1 and lower than the current, provided that it leaves no remainder.

The hand ends when one player plays their last card or is unable to play without going below zero.

boardgame: World War III: Team Yankee – West German
family: Game: World War III – Team Yankee 2 (Battlefront)

boardgame: World War III: Team Yankee – West German Starter Force: Panzeraufklärungs Kompanie
description: World War III: Team Yankee – West German Starter Force: Panzeraufklärungs Kompanie, Panzer Reconnaissance Company.
family: Game: World War III – Team Yankee 2 (Battlefront)

description:
აღმოაჩინე საქართველო , Discover Georgia

Players have to travel to different parts of Georgia and visit different cities

Each turn players draw cards and attempt to claim routes to achieve their objective (cards).
The objective cards indicate destinations between cities in Georgia and bordering countries.
Player scores points by claiming routes (the longer the more points) and achieving their objective (the further away, the more points scored at end of game).

The goal is to visit as many places as possible to earn more points. The player with the most points wins.

This game puts you behind the wheel of one of the most important tech companies in the last century. You will relive the stories of some of the most influential technology innovators in history that paved the way to what we are capable of today. People like Edwin H. Land, a scientist who invented a whole new paradigm in photography with Polaroid, or Nolan Bushnell who executed on the first ever video gaming ideas with Atari, that drove the world into an age of digital video games and personal computers. It will be like being inside an interactive documentary, where you play the founder first person.

You start as a simple tech start-up and through multiple plays you and the story evolve into giving you the responsibility of turning your first product successes into a real tech company that people worship. A company that aims to take over the world with cutting edge inventions by visionary and quirky personalities.

You will attract the best talent, develop prototypes and turn them into mind boggling customer experiences. You will need to compose a sweet spot of skills in product design and engineering, business, management, marketing, and people. Talent with certain know-how can help you create better quality products. But to make all that happen you need investors and business-savvy individuals who can open the doors you need to succeed in a competitive consumer space of other tech companies - your opponents who you meet over beers at conferences and in courtroom disputes over patent infringement.

Through a new and profound way of pairing a board game with an app on your phone, the initial barrier of getting past a complex set of rules is replaced by a progressive approach that teaches you the game through a simple first start-up story. Then it grows more and more complex over the course of multiple play sessions towards transforming you into becoming a massive tech giant in the end.

The app will also be used to communicate the narrative through audio storytelling, to communicate every mechanic’s tie-in to the theme and context, to manage gaming sessions in this new narrative format, where you can save your progress between play sessions, and to be a rules learning or reminder companion, to get you quickly back into the game, if you have had a break from it.

—description from the designer

One hundred dogs are on the run and you want to gather as many as possible in your kennel. Why is the dog's nose cold? Yes, answer that and other fun questions about dogs. Luck with the dice and good memory means that you can paw forward with Anders Bagge on the wonderfully mischievous playing field. A fun and varied game that is filled to the brim with dogs.

boardgame: Harry Potter: House Cup Competition – Draught of Living Death Promo
description:
Promo card only available at conventions through the USAopoly booth. Later added to Dice Tower Kickstarter 2021 as a replacement promo for the unfulfilled Harry Potter Hogwarts Battle promo.

You lose 1 Level but you give another player 3 Levels
